body:
Hey!

I'm open to improving the current dark mode scheme 👍, with the following caveats:

1. We still want to keep dark background. Not only is this pretty much the point since canvas takes up 90% of your screen (or more), but the dark mode output is also often what users go for in terms of aesthetics.
2. We do need to rotate some colors, namely black/gray colors (or more generally, very dark colors) — question is what would the heuristic look like.
3. I assume there are many users who either 1) prefer current dark mode, or 2) have in-progress projects (especially long-term ones like books) where making drastic dark mode scheme changes would affect them negatively. Not sure how to handle this. I don't want to keep two dark mode schemes around (one thing is keeping the logic, another is having multiple different dark modes in the UI).

(3) also addresses your first point:

> First off, rename the current Dark Mode to Invert Mode, with clear warnings of what might the output and bugs be.

If we change the dark mode, let's that in one go and not keep multiple ones. Unless you meant this as an interim solution until the rewrite is done, but I don't think current dark mode is anywhere near unusable to warrant this (well, except some of the bugs).

> I currently have Excalidraw with a custom background of the value #121212 (dark grey) that is consistent with Material Design's dark theme

That's the color we're using — I believe we're following the same design document (or generally Google's).
author: dwelle

body:
Sorry if this is just flying over the actual issue here, but shouldn't dark mode have like... a different palette of colors? Something that looks better than whatever change excalidraw makes to the colors maybe...?

I understand the idea of translating colors into things that stay readable in dark mode without having to change the actual color code, but that makes for a weird user experience when trying to use specific colors, and I don't think this technique of rotating and inverting colors works every time.

If the changing of colors needs to be kept, and I imagine changing the palette would clash with the question you brought up with your third point, could that happen in an actual color code level? So instead of an object with a color code of #364fc7 staying with the same color code but with y'know a different color in practice:
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12601762/150244604-2e2fb0ce-cd6d-44d5-bacd-ff6f4ad669b1.png)

The actual color-code could change to it's equivalent after the change:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/12601762/150245201-fed6d115-7c2b-48d4-9d04-1b71087112b9.png)

I'm kind of only thinking here about the color issue because that's my main problem with dark mode, I don't notice any performance drops on my machine. Currently, the feature of selecting the color code you want in excalidraw is... useless in dark mode.
author: Mobuos

body:
> * We still want to keep dark background. Not only is this pretty much the point since canvas takes up 90% of your screen (or more), but the dark mode output is also often what users go for in terms of aesthetics.
> 
> * We do need to rotate some colors, namely black/gray colors (or more generally, very dark colors) — question is what would the heuristic look like.

There are two main use cases in mind with this, the use case in which users wish to *invert* an already-consumed canvas, meaning they have ongoing projects and wish to switch them from light to dark mode instantly without tinkering with colors, and users who wish to start *new* projects with proper dark mode. 

This is the main reason I suggested keeping two modes, as completely changing the current behavior will certainly break ongoing projects. Asking users of ongoing projects to ditch them or manually transform the colours would be too much, especially in large projects. And ignoring users who wish to use a proper dark mode wouldn't be right. 

With `invert mode` you can apply the invert and call it a day for users who wish to invert the whole thing, which achieves your first two points. With `dark mode` you can modify the background of the canvas to a dark colour, change the colour palette, and be done with it. This achieves your first point and removes the need for the second one.

As for @Mobuos's question, provided I understand your question correctly: I'm pretty sure you can transform the hexa to RGB and rotate `(255 - value)` the RGB variables to fit the new mode, but there are bigger problems with the approach. This will pretty much be slow in JS for ongoing projects, and would still produce the same output for new projects, inverted colours.
author: KL13NT

body:
I was thinking the inversion and rotation would happen only when pressing the button to switch to dark/light mode, after that excalidraw would be simply using different colors. I really should have placed my suggestion in #3531 instead because that's all that it fixes: Typed hex codes in dark mode would show correctly without affecting anything.

I don't see how it would be slower though? Excalidraw already does the rotation in dark mode right? Plus it would only need to do the conversion of the hexcode **once**, again, when pressing the button to switch do dark/light mode, excalidraw wouldn't invert everything everytime, only the hexcodes would change to reflect the inverted value.
author: Mobuos

body: It does the rotation using CSS, which is a GPU-accelerated native operation. Doing it with JS would be a ton slower, AFAICT. Also, using different colors wouldn't solve it, as the inversion would apply to the alternative palette as well, and that makes for a whole different set of problems.

author: MattKleinsmith

body:
@dwelle 

> I assume there are many users who either 1) prefer current dark mode, or 2) have in-progress projects (especially long-term ones like books) where making drastic dark mode scheme changes would affect them negatively. Not sure how to handle this. I don't want to keep two dark mode schemes around (one thing is keeping the logic, another is having multiple different dark modes in the UI).

The dilemma is real. Excalidraw must sacrifice something. Currently, Excalidraw is sacrificing the accurate-dark-mode use case. The other options of sacrifice are:

1. The inversion use case
2. Simplicity of code

I recommend sacrificing simplicity of code, instead of sacrificing an important use case. In other words, I recommend sacrificing the developer's wants (_"I don't want to keep two dark mode schemes around"_), instead of the user's. Isn't that the proper hierarchy?

As for the UI, you could bury the accurate-dark-mode toggle here:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8968171/190869217-a4a00da2-bfce-4206-bdb6-c875a03bf566.png)
author: MattKleinsmith

body:
@MattKleinsmith Hi!

> Please don't change my images. That's my responsibility. Currently, I avoid using images in Excalidraw, because it inverts them.

Not something we do by choice, but due to how our dark mode works right now.

I assume you're using Safari? On other browsers that support canvas filters we selectively invert the images back (the double invert isn't 100% accurate, but for now it seems serviceable).

This is something we aim to fix at some point, but will require rewriting out dark mode.

> Please don't make the hex values wrong. "000000" should mean black, not white.

Isn't that the point of canvas-application dark mode? If we didn't, and only inverted the canvas background, then all your black/dark shapes would effectively become invisible.

Or are you suggesting that we don't touch the canvas and only invert the UI? Problem with this is that canvas covers 90% of the screen, and our goal here is for the UI to become easy on your eyes at night.

The secondary consideration is that some people want to create dark-themed drawings for various reasons (e.g. they publish on a dark-themed website) with minimum effort. Especially if they want to publish in both themes at the same time. If they had to manually toggle the colors to match the dark theme, that would go counter that goal.
author: dwelle

body:
Hi David (@dwelle),

Thanks for your reply!

> The secondary consideration is that some people want to create dark-themed drawings for various reasons (e.g. they publish on a dark-themed website) with minimum effort. Especially if they want to publish in both themes at the same time. If they had to manually toggle the colors to match the dark theme, that would go counter that goal.

Yes, the inversion use case is a great one for many people. Perhaps it should stay the default and have the right to have a button in the main UI: 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8968171/190870490-634dfb08-ac57-4302-8cc9-3947dff50369.png)

What I'm asking for is, yes, a second way of doing dark mode, buried in the workplace settings, so that the inversion folks are taken care of via a first-class button, while us accurate-dark-mode folks are also taken care of via a buried setting. A buried setting is better than no setting:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8968171/190870630-343cc508-94fb-4103-a290-1dc951711ab4.png)

---

> Isn't that the point of canvas-application dark mode? If we didn't, and only inverted the canvas background, then all your black/dark shapes would effectively become invisible.

No, I live in dark mode, as do many others. We use light shapes from the beginning. We do not toggle back and forth between light mode and dark mode. We arrive, we enable dark mode, and we rest there. Unfortunately, on the current version of Excalidraw, that means inaccurate colors for us.

> Or are you suggesting that we don't touch the canvas and only invert the UI?

No, the canvas is a target, too. Here's what we're asking for:

1. Dark UI
2. Dark canvas (via setting its color to a dark one, not via an inversion of a white canvas)
3. A hand-chosen default color palette that goes well with the dark canvas
author: MattKleinsmith

body:
Maybe the change between dark/light mode should only map colors to the new palette. You could even map the colors used for shapes using the inversion currently used... That would solve the accuracy of hex codes while avoiding messing up any use-cases right?

For example, red is #FA5252, when switching to dark mode all that is needed to do is map that color to #F86769... Ideally the palette of colors available without using hex codes would have a custom mapping (a custom paletter for dark mode), but the inversion route could be the default for everything else.
author: Mobuos

body:
> You could even map the colors used for shapes using the inversion currently used...

This is a great idea. It solves part 3: "A hand-chosen default color palette that goes well with the dark canvas".

> That would solve the accuracy of hex codes while avoiding messing up any use-cases right?

Unfortunately, there are two conflicting use cases:

---

### **Toggling** between light and dark at a moment's notice, even in the middle of project:

The best solution for this that I know of is **global inversion**, at the expense of color-code accuracy.

---

### Choosing light or dark at the beginning, for the sake of **color-code accuracy**:

The best solution for this that I know of is mapping the default color options _in the menu_ to an appropriate one, and mapping the canvas background to the appropriate one. This means **leaving the colors of the shapes on the canvas alone**.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/8968171/190877373-8234971c-aa1f-4707-825c-c4fc043b7921.png)

---

A solution to the conflict between the two cases:

1. Let one be default
2. Let the other be an option in the workplace settings
author: MattKleinsmith

author: dwelle

body:
It looks like the `resizeSingleElement` function is dividing by `boundsNewWidth`, which breaks once that value is `0`.

- [Replay of the bug here](https://app.replay.io/recording/excalidraw-4625-resizing-to-close-to-0-widthheight-locks-up--3b5f5120-e0b3-4e6f-a44b-c164fa2db4f9)
- [Loom video here]( https://www.loom.com/share/3e5ac9eb71b0427b859b7454a40fb3b8)
author: ceceliacreates

body: @dai-shi @dwelle This may also be related to #1610, because it looks like the width there is `-Infinity`.
author: ceceliacreates

body: fixed via #4804, feel free to reopen if it still exists
author: ad1992

body: Pinch to zoom? But, once we add pen-mode where touch events will be ignore, that won't work. So a good point.
author: dwelle

body:
This is exactly what I am looking for I think.

In Adobe products I am able to hold <SPACE> to Pan the canvas and optionally hold <CMD> to Zoom with the pen.  This works so well for navigating a canvas.

I would also love to be able to add a modifier key to the <SPACE> to drag which allows me to Zoom with the Pen as happens currently when I <CMD +/->.

I'm using the Dial/Wheel of my Logitech Keyboard to Zoom in conjunction with <SPACE> just now, but it's a bit awkward.

Any ideas or hacks to achieve this would be most welcome :-)

Thank you
Pete

UPDATE: Weirdly, what I wanted just randomly happened!  I was working with some text and my pen started zooming in and out!  Unfortunately, I was stuck in this 'mode' and had to perform a Reload after which it all went back to how it normally is!   Someone else mentioned this happening - https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/3931.  It seems like this bug was fixed, but I wonder if there is a way to switch this 'bug' on and off?!
author: Pedroespecial

body:
> Weirdly, what I wanted just randomly happened! I was working with some text and my pen started zooming in and out! Unfortunately, I was stuck in this 'mode' and had to perform a Reload after which it all went back to how it normally is! Someone else mentioned this happening - https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/3931. It seems like this bug was fixed, but I wonder if there is a way to switch this 'bug' on and off?!

Mhm, so the bug wasn't fixed? 😭 The original PR you linked to didn't fix this fully, which we did in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4765 — but apparently not. Unless you were still on an older version, which can happen if you 1) didn't load Excalidraw in a while 2) you had multiple tabs open which prevents loading the latest version (ServiceWorker).

> In Adobe products I am able to hold to Pan the canvas and optionally hold to Zoom with the pen. This works so well for navigating a canvas.
> 
> I would also love to be able to add a modifier key to the to drag which allows me to Zoom with the Pen as happens currently when I <CMD +/->.
> 
> I'm using the Dial/Wheel of my Logitech Keyboard to Zoom in conjunction with just now, but it's a bit awkward.

We could maybe add support for various pen buttons (though I'm not sure the buttons are standardized so without having access to them it might be hard to do properly), but it will not solve this for cases like touchscreen tablets (iPad, Galaxy Note, ...).
author: dwelle

body:
1) The 'bug' only occurred once.  I am using ED in Obsidian with Zolts plugin.

2) I don't think any Pen button support is required.  It's kind of difficult to describe what I mean.... Currently, when I hold the Space bar down and pan up and down with my Pen I can move around the canvas...all good.  What I am also able to do on Adobe products and Krita is to additionally press the <CMD> key so <SPACE> + <CMD> and the same Pen panning action Zooms me in and out of the Canvas.  Thus by holding <SPACE> down and coming on and off of the <CMD> key I am able to Zoom **and** Pan around my canvas - allowing me super-quick control of where I want to next focus.

As a side note, I notice that I can also achieve a Zoom In and Out by holding down <CTRL> and using my Magic Mouse scroll, but sadly holding <CTRL> and Panning the Pen does not do the same.
author: Pedroespecial

body: Is a pen-zoom feature planned for the future? Similar to the "space" to pan, but with zooming instead.
author: moonbyte13

body: Yeah, we need a analogous "space to pan" but with zooming. Concepts app does this with the "S" key, you can toggle it or hold it and then drag to zoom the canvas.
author: JanitorHead

body:
There is a PR #7802 for this feature, but it seems to have been abandoned since last year. I am willing to take it on, I will make a new PR and submit it.

I hope it is accepted, as this feature would be of great help to anyone who uses a graphics tablet.

body:
Hi. It should work regardless. Are you using a non-US keyboard layout/language by any chance? With many languages, you have to use the `Shift` modifier to use numbers with the numeric row.

I suppose we should use `keyCode` for the toolbar numeric shortcuts as most (if not all) language layouts have the numeric keys on the same position.

body: Yeah, we're aware of this. Apart from rendering in a worker ([https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/OffscreenCanvas](OffscreenCanvas) is sadly not support on FF and Safari), we'd have to split the image into tiles and glue the bitmaps together.

body:
That post doesn't describe how long live session URLs are alive for https://excalidraw.com/#room=b5db242f4fe07c826f6a,o95_Gq6x0eGk895A6kzILw

Is there no risk that this URL will be leaked? 

Ideally the room is perhaps cleared up after a period of inactivity?
author: kaihendry

body:
As long as you can keep the URL safe, it would not be possible to guess/access the url (as the entropy is quite high 16^22) and the keys are not saved on backend servers and decryption happens on client side.
The collaborations sessions are persisted so you can always come back and update your drawings

author: dwelle

body:
Hello - I made a PR for this, if there's anything I can / should do, would love any reviews or feedback!

For now, there's no visual icon for the hyperlink, I think that's something that can be done in a follow up PR if that is really wanted (personally I'd rather not have it, and it seems like there are some issues / contentions about how it should be presented) - also this was easier to do without it 😆.

I'm actually hoping to get this merged in for work purposes, specifically for building C4 diagrams, and this feature would be a huge win for making that happen!
author: JRJurman

body: @dwelle I wouldn't say so. I use excalidraw to create sketch notes in SVG format and clickable links would be pretty handy.
author: Shoggomo

body:
For my two cents, I think that there are other ways (such as color or font-decoration) that could be used to make linked objects obvious when they are exported to svg, and that can be done when editing in excalidraw.

I like the idea that SVG mimics the PNG export, rather than the editor experience. 

And finally, I think if an icon would be additional work / potentially a breaking change, I think it is worth it to release just the link functionality, and then follow up with additional icons.
author: JRJurman

body:
@JRJurman, I 100% agree.
If I want an image map on which links are obvious and highlighted, I will draw such a drawing. Else links should not be marked in the export with an icon.
author: zsviczian

body:
I think a visual presentation should be a separate discussion, that will only become relevant, when a basic export is available.
Your PR @zsviczian looks good to me. I would say, when this is approved, further discussions can start.
At least that's my view. I try to keep the scope of discussions small, before they get too cluttered.

body:
Yeah Im rly missing that one as well. 

The workaround with gradients or a lot of smaller forms is rly tedious and editing afterwards is also not that straight forward.

For me personally having 2 colors and maybe an option for the angle would be enough already. Maybe thats easier to implement. 

Im using the software on a daily basis, so I could for sure give feedback as soon as something breaks regarding this function.

Ofc it would be the dream to have full gradient support (multiple colors, percentage, different patterns), but the first variant would already add so much for:

- 3D objects (round surfaces)
- Lights
- (product) design

Right now most of the time I have to interrupt my workflow and use a different software to create an svg and if that needs to be changed, I have to go back into that file, export/import again. So also not great, but better than the system thats in there right now.
Its such a bummer, cuz I rly love excalidraw and everything else goes quite fast and smooth.

body:
I'm not a huge fan of the link icon either. It would be great If we could come up with a better way to 1) indicate an element is hyperlinked 2) make it easy to click through that link, and 3) show an indication where the link leads to (currently this works on hover of the link icon).

Miro puts the link icon inside the elements. It creates issues for text elements as it often significantly covers the text, but even for non-text elements it's not ideal.

Figma doesn't show an icon. It underlines the text. But that's not a clear indication that an element is hyperlinked (you can't distinguish  it from just an aesthetic element).  And AFAIK they don't support adding links to non-text elements.
author: dwelle

body:
For now, I am deploying the adaptive link icon and an option to set the link icon opacity to Excalidraw-Obsidian. I'll see how that will be received by the users. I think the adaptive approach (#4763) is a good balance between visibility and intrusion.

Maybe a context menu option to hide link icons? I often simply do a screenshot of my drawings instead of exporting to png/svg. Temporarily hiding links is already a big improvement.

body:
I have to disagree.  One of the key features of Excalidraw is the ease at which you can draw beautiful designs. These link icons undermine that beautiful look and feel...

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/14358394/153279636-d806a723-da6f-4173-9d77-74c6dea65047.png)
author: zsviczian

body:
> One of the key features of Excalidraw is the ease at which you can draw beautiful designs. These link icons undermine that beautiful look and feel...

I don't disagree, but drawing beautiful diagrams relate to the output (export), not the UI. But UI is of course important as well.

But, the purpose of the link icon is for it to be visible — an indication that an element is hyperlinked. In the screenshot above, it's almost invisible, thus goes counter to that objective.

I think the argument of this and related issues you've created is: diagrams that are very heavily hyperlinked are running into UI/UX issues with the link icon. We can discuss that in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4761#issuecomment-1034195123

body:
> However, I do not understand why you also do not want to attach it to excalidraw-container:

Yes, this shouldn't be conditional, I think we missed this.

> setting props.autoFocus = true; Why is this not true by default?

This is due to Accessibility since for users with screen readers its not a good experience if suddenly focus is shifted to something without them doing it

>changing handleCanvasPointerDown by adding this.focusContainer(); to the function. Again, I've seen no negative effects, but now if I touch the canvas it receives focus and all the shortcuts work as expected

I think this should be fine, feel free to open a PR

author: dwelle

body:
PS! I know it's _hard_ to remove something. According to [recent studies](https://www.nature.com/articles/d41586-021-00592-0), humans _prefer adding over subtracting_ in problem solving, even when subtraction gives the best result.

In this case, I really think removing the feature of arrow binding _completely_ is the best solution for the product, and in-line with the overall focus on low fidelity.
author: smyrman

body:
> In this case, I really think removing the feature of arrow binding completely is the best solution for the product, and in-line with the overall focus on low fidelity.

I agree. There are definitely plans to scale it back, or at worst make it easy to disable it.
author: dwelle

body: As a counterpoint, I would be very sad if arrows were just their own objects. e.g. If I move a box with an attached arrow, I want the arrow to move with the box.
author: DanTulovsky

body:
>  I would be very sad if arrows were just their own objects. e.g. If I move a box with an attached arrow, I want the arrow to move with the box.

@DanTulovsky don't worry, the arrow binding won't be removed. What we're considering is either 1) make it opt-in, or 2) make it opt-out. Both the opt-in/out would apply either to specific elements you toggle it for, or more likely to specific elements + future elements — similar to how it now works other properties such as when changing color.
author: dwelle

body:
> As a counterpoint, I would be very sad if arrows were just their own objects. e.g. If I move a box with an attached arrow, I want the arrow to move with the box.

@DanTulovsky, how often do you experience moving a box, and _not_ ending up adjusting the arrow manually afterwards anyway? And if you do end up adjusting the arrow afterwards, the question is, how much time did the automatic moving of the arrow save you?

Some products that specialize in drawing boxes with arrows between them, e.g. draw.io or various Office variants/clonse, have used a lot if time in specifying different _snap points_ along the objects' edge, and defining different types of curve adjustments, so that when you move the box, the arrow ends up "right" afterward. Excalidraw (rightfully IMO) haven't spent _as much_ effort on this.

This is my experience roughly 90% of the time if I want to move a box, and have the arrow follow:

![output](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/75205/154434707-8c16a9ea-ca89-438d-bbe7-e3bc571be6e8.gif)

author: DanTulovsky

body:
The snapping is driving me insane, and wastes a lot of time to work around. Whenever I have any text near an arrow, I cannot adjust the text without the arrow going insane.

I vote for opt-in for the feature, and also possibly to reduce the number of things arrows can snap to. Would it ever be useful to snap an arrow to an element of some text? Maybe removing snapping to text altogether would be good.

Another approach could be a feature where whenever a snapping possibility is detected, a little button shows up near the potential snap to allow the user to "lock" the elements together. This way it allows the feature to present itself whenever it could be useful, rather than having a global toggle that the user either leaves on and it causes problems, or leaves off and the user never remembers to use it.

body:
@smyrman wrote:
> > As a counterpoint, I would be very sad if arrows were just their own objects. e.g. If I move a box with an attached arrow, I want the arrow to move with the box.
> 
> @DanTulovsky, how often do you experience moving a box, and _not_ ending up adjusting the arrow manually afterwards anyway? And if you do end up adjusting the arrow afterwards, the question is, how much time did the automatic moving of the arrow save you?
> 
> Some products that specialize in drawing boxes with arrows between them, e.g. draw.io or various Office variants/clonse, have used a lot if time in specifying different _snap points_ along the objects' edge, and defining different types of curve adjustments, so that when you move the box, the arrow ends up "right" afterward. Excalidraw (rightfully IMO) haven't spent _as much_ effort on this.
> 
> This is my experience roughly 90% of the time if I want to move a box, and have the arrow follow:
> 
> ![output](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/75205/154434707-8c16a9ea-ca89-438d-bbe7-e3bc571be6e8.gif) 

I know your comment is pretty old and it seems the behaviour has changed a bit. But I don’t remember ever having this particular problem. What you’re basically doing is unbinding and rebinding the arrow when you attempt to move the right endpoint and then rebinding the left endpoint. This way you’re doing an unnecessary extra step. Here’s how it looks for me right now.

![excalidraw](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1823617/188412639-ec1ba9ee-5885-4509-aa3c-d104c24c742c.gif)

But I just want to add that I regularly work with big and complex diagrams with lots of boxes and multiple arrows connecting to them. I’d be devastated if arrow binding would not work anymore. It would just make everything so much worse. Even if the arrows don’t end up in the perfect place 100% of the time it’s still better to let the arrows follow the bound object.

Example with binding:
![excalidraw2](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1823617/188415078-e1cc8c5e-f6c1-4644-b0bd-e1481adefe70.gif)

Example without binding:
I forgot to move one of the boxes but you get the idea
I also see I forgot to move one of the arrows of a box I moved. This would not happen with binding.
![excalidraw3](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/1823617/188415122-bf177f64-ea96-49c3-a848-1e7165f33b47.gif)
author: baldurh

body:
Could the solution be one of the following?
A) Add a configuration. Enable/disable binding? Any arrow added/moved with this disabled would not bind to anything.
B) Add a configuration. Something like ‘Break binding when moving objects’ so that if you want to move something and ignore all bindings, this is something you could turn on.
C) Add a hotkey. Holding down some key while moving an object would ignore all bound arrows and just move the object. Or a hotkey for toggling option B
author: baldurh

body:
> Could the solution be one of the following? A) Add a configuration. Enable/disable binding? Any arrow added/moved with this disabled would not bind to anything. B) Add a configuration. Something like ‘Break binding when moving objects’ so that if you want to move something and ignore all bindings, this is something you could turn on. C) Add a hotkey. Holding down some key while moving an object would ignore all bound arrows and just move the object. Or a hotkey for toggling option B

- (A) is the idea, but still not the ideal UX as it requires explicit opt-out.
- (B) wouldn't make sense in the context of bindable containers (rectangles) since updating arrows when moving containers they're bound to is the whole point.
- (C) likely should work like that (also), yes — same as you can use `cmd/ctrl` today to disable _new_ bindings when moving elements. Though if we use the same key (which I think we must as there aren't any other left), there's an argument to be made that you may still want to not bind new elements while keep current ones that are already bound — which is the current behavior of `cmd/ctrl`.

---

Ad the comment discussing @smyrman's points — yes, I don't think binding should go away, but I agree we must alleviate the pain points of false positives (when you don't want to bind). And we should also improve the binding algorithm so that you don't have to manually reposition that much.

author: dwelle

body:
Currently there is no [File System Access](https://web.dev/file-system-access/) API support on Android, which means we fall back to `<a download>` (via [browser-fs-access](https://github.com/GoogleChromeLabs/browser-fs-access)). Getting proper support for the API on Android is tracked in https://crbug.com/1011535.

Proper [File Handling](https://web.dev/file-handling/) via file type association is currently only launched on desktop operating systems (still behind a flag). Your best bet is probably [Web Share Target](https://web.dev/web-share-target/), which Excalidraw implements.

body: Yes, this was an [intentional change](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4545) so that all tabs sync the data, and users never end up losing drawings due to getting overridden by the latest one. Earlier if there were multiple tabs, each with a separate drawing, and if you close a tab and reopen again (or open a new tab), it will contain last state from the other tab and no way to recover the lost drawing.
author: ad1992

body:
Oh I see.

The observed behaviour when attempting to open a scene in a new tab when there's already one open is quite surprising, as it simply opens the drawing from the existing tab. In my case the original window was blank, and I thought my drawings had broken!

It might be good to display a warning about what's going on, or even to provide the option to open it anyway with the localStorage sync disabled

In my experience I have relied far more on the behaviour of independent tabs than I have on the ability to close and re-open a tab. Generally my mental model has been that closing a tab will discard a scene (even though it doesn't) and I'm always slightly surprised when I go to start a new diagram and it resumes from whatever I worked on before.

Edit: to give a more concrete example, today while drawing a diagram I wanted to open another diagram I drew last week to copy some elements out of it - but clicking the link gave me the warning about clearing my current scene. Previously I could open the old one, copy stuff over to main, then close the old tab. As a workaround today I used a private browsing window to copy from.

body: We are really sorry but there is no way to recover those drawings since the tabs are now always synced so if you had different drawings in different tabs, it would now show the latest one in all tabs. As mentioned in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4837#issuecomment-1048479695 we started syncing tabs as users were losing work. Please export your drawings If you want to keep a copy of them. Since we don't support tabs so if you want to work on multiple drawings at the same time the only way would be to open it in different browsers/ incognito might work too.

body:
1. open a tab, go to excalidraw.com, drag-drop an excalidraw file into the canvas
-> the excalidraw drawing is shown, as is the file name for saving
2. open another tab, go to excalidraw.com, drag-drop an another excalidraw file into the canvas
-> the excalidraw drawing is shown, as is the file name for saving
3. Jump back and forth between the two tabs, the sync mechanism make the file save feature go away
4. Save file, the suggested filename is now Untitled-2022-xxxxx
-> The filename of the original drawing is lost

I think the mentioned PR introduced something with this sync feature which needs to be adjusted.

The use case for excalidraw now changed from copy-paste between open sessions in different tabs to copy-paste between different browsers and their incocnito mode.

As it was explained by @ad1992 at some point this was an intentional change. Since then this is how https://excalidraw.com/ behaves. Our premium offering offers to store multiple collections in the cloud https://plus.excalidraw.com/.

author: marcospgp

body:
> I believe you guys should make it clearer that having multiple projects open at once is a benefit of the premium plan.

It's mentioned in various places on our landing page, i.e. https://plus.excalidraw.com/pricing/. Though I agree it does not completely scream this information.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f7ebf969-a0ca-4933-9d12-238bc76c566f)

It might make sense to add it somewhere inside the editor (i.e. when trying to create a new scene / clean canvas), though we are trying not to upsell the premium plan in the editor too much as it creates additional friction - the open-source version, even with the one opened scene workflow, is perfectly fine for a lot of users.

> I did end up signing up for it, but I may have done so earlier if I knew the issue was an intended free plan limitation.

Awesome! Though to clarify, I think the intention was mainly to limit the loss of data, rather than to give the premium an advantage. Assuming we ended up with this as the only feasible solution, as attacking "tabs" without the intermediate step would be too much for one change.

body:
hey @alexeyzimarev. Sorry about that, we're getting a lot of issues on this repository, so some issues may get lost. For general help, you can always use one of our discord servers or our support email: 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/163224906-47a2e9be-87aa-45c8-b171-f98c4ddabb83.png)

Onto your question: I couldn't reproduce the problem.

In fact, I'm not even sure what you're describing is technically possible right now. If you're referring to this library: https://libraries.excalidraw.com/#ferminrp-post-it then those items are composed of line elements, which don't allow for binding text to them. So adding text using the text tool should just place the text over them, and it won't wrap nor be locked to the sticky note.

Could you maybe record a video, or explain in more detail what's happening?

If you're ok with not having the exact look of a sticky note, then I'd suggest to simply create a rectangle with a background color and adding text to it directly. It will be both bound to it, and the text will autowrap:

![excal-sticky](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/163224369-58b823ba-40dc-4c6f-bde7-46c49a8af338.gif)

body:
Thanks. Ok, it seems the text is indeed bound to the sticky note, even though that's not officially supported. There was a bug where under special circumstances it could have happened, and it was fixed only _after_ you've reported the issue (https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4655), so that's most likely how you ended up with it.

It should still be possible to edit the text by clicking on it with the text tool — at least it works for me. You can then either delete all the text and confirm to remove the text element, or you can take advantage of this bug which will at least autowrap the text to the sticky note :).

Alternative is to use workflow I suggested in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4840#issuecomment-1098243101, which is to create regular rectangles.

We might support binding to line(ar) shapes in the future, after we figure out how it should behave around double-clicking/enter, which as you've noticed right now it edits the sticky note line shape instead of editing the text.

Closing as there's nothing actionable — except maybe trying to recover from this bug on reload by fixing the bindings. But this seems enough of an edge case that it's probably better to leave it (and maybe now that you know you can edit it using the text tool, you may prefer the buggy behavior!).

body:
I have an idea how to implement tables using a subtype of `ExcalidrawFrameElement` and tying child elements to cells, with a custom action for in-cell distribution. My approach would allow configuring the number of rows/columns and their widths/heights, without limiting cell contents to text.

(Due to the technical specifics, such an implementation would not be available on https://excalidraw.com or Excalidraw Plus, until significant portions of #6037 were merged.  Until then, such an implementation would be available on https://math.preview.excalidraw.com.)

body:
In the meantime, for those looking for an alternative, I highly suggest writing tables in [markdown](https://www.markdownguide.org/).

This [website](https://www.tablesgenerator.com/markdown_tables#) is especially useful for generating the markdown code from a UI. Here's an example:

```md
# Person Table

| ID  | Name         | Age | Email                 | City        |
|-----|--------------|-----|-----------------------|-------------|
| 1   | John Doe     | 28  | john.doe@example.com  | New York    |
| 2   | Jane Smith   | 34  | jane.smith@example.com| Los Angeles |
| 3   | Alice Johnson| 22  | alice.j@example.com   | Chicago     |
| 4   | Bob Brown    | 45  | bob.brown@example.com | Houston     |
```

Then, you can wrap it in a rectangle to make it more familiar with excalidraw drawings:

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/f2014037-e8b0-446d-b889-a3213b3f6c12)

The advantage is that it's way easier to add or remove elements from a markdown table, and many tool can easily parse and recognize it.

body:
Hey folks,

I’ve built a utility app that solves this exact problem—Excalidraw’s lack of native table support.
I built (vibe coded) a utility app: [Excalidraw Table Maker](https://excalidraw-table-maker.vercel.app/) 

**What it does:**
- Converts HTML, Markdown, and CSV tables into Excalidraw-compatible diagrams.
- AI-powered table generation: Just give it a topic, and it creates a relevant table on the fly—perfect for brainstorming or quick documentation.


This tool aims to fill the gap until native table support is available. I would love feedback or ideas on how it could be improved or integrated more closely with Excalidraw!

body:
### Workaround 2

Another workaround would be to use a fallback font stack (available on Windows, macOS and Linux) for each of the two 

```diff
<text
  x="0"
  y="0"
- font-family="Cascadia, Segoe UI Emoji"
+ font-family="Cascadia, ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, 'SF Mono', Menlo, Consolas, 'Liberation Mono', monospace, Segoe UI Emoji"
  font-size="20px"
  fill="#ff7575"
  text-anchor="start"
  style="white-space: pre;"
  direction="ltr"
  dominant-baseline="text-before-edge"
>courses</text>
```

Maybe this should be a PR actually...

**Edit:** PRs open here:

- ~https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6479~
- #6550
- different approach: #8012
author: karlhorky

body:
### Workaround 3

Create a GitHub Actions workflow like this to find and replace all `font-family`s in all Excalidraw SVG files based on [these fallbacks](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6479) on every PR that is opened that adds or updates `*.excalidraw.svg` files (you'll need to set up a secret `EXCALIDRAW_SVG_FONT_FIX_TOKEN` with a token that has write access to the repository):

`.github/workflows/fix-excalidraw-svg-fonts.yml`

```yml
# Workaround to fix fonts in Excalidraw SVGs
# https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4855#issuecomment-1513014554
#
# Temporary workaround until the following PR is merged:
# https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6479
#
# TODO: If the PR above is merged, this file (and its token) can be removed
name: Compress PDFs
on:
  pull_request:
    # Run only when Excalidraw SVG files are added or changed
    paths:
      - '**.excalidraw.svg'

jobs:
  build:
    # Only run on Pull Requests within the same repository, and not from forks.
    if: github.event.pull_request.head.repo.full_name == github.repository
    name: Fix Excalidraw SVG Fonts
    runs-on: ubuntu-latest
    steps:
      - name: Checkout Repo
        uses: actions/checkout@v3
        with:
          ref: ${{ github.head_ref }}

      - name: Fix fonts in Excalidraw SVGs
        run: |
          find . -type f -iname '*.excalidraw.svg' | while read file; do
            echo "Fixing fonts in $file"
            sed -i 's/Virgil, Segoe UI Emoji/Virgil, '"'"'Comic Sans MS'"'"', '"'"'Segoe Print'"'"', '"'"'Bradley Hand'"'"', '"'"'Lucida Handwriting'"'"', '"'"'Marker Felt'"'"', cursive/g' "$file"
            sed -i 's/Helvetica, Segoe UI Emoji/Helvetica, -apple-system,BlinkMacSystemFont, '"'"'Segoe UI'"'"', '"'"'Noto Sans'"'"', Helvetica, Arial, sans-serif, '"'"'Apple Color Emoji'"'"', '"'"'Segoe UI Emoji'"'"'/g' "$file"
            sed -i 's/Cascadia, Segoe UI Emoji/Cascadia, ui-monospace, SFMono-Regular, '"'"'SF Mono'"'"', Menlo, Consolas, '"'"'Liberation Mono'"'"', monospace/g' "$file"
          done
      - name: Commit files
        run: |
          git config user.email github-actions[bot]@users.noreply.github.com
          git config user.name github-actions[bot]

          git add **/*.excalidraw.svg
          if [ -z "$(git status --porcelain)" ]; then
            exit 0
          fi
          git commit -m "Fix fonts in Excalidraw SVGs"
          git push origin HEAD:${{ github.head_ref }}
        env:
          GITHUB_TOKEN: ${{ secrets.EXCALIDRAW_SVG_FONT_FIX_TOKEN }}
```

Also added to `github-tricks` here:

- https://github.com/karlhorky/github-tricks#github-actions-only-run-when-certain-files-changed

body:
An initial hypothesis here, this dependency on React may have been introduced when exporting with [Dark Mode Export became available](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/3046). It looks like the app tries to create the SUN / MOON icons, and fails because React is not available.

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/326557/156684280-d7228dec-09b1-48df-8cfc-06f14bdb5655.png)

That being said, while that is where this code fails, it looks like there are other references to `React.createElement` in the bundle now as well :thinking:
author: JRJurman

body:
It is possible to have this work by including React, however the interface also appears to have changed, as exportToSvg now returns a promise? I was able to get it to work in the following example:

https://github.com/JRJurman/test-excalidraw-utils/blob/master/excalidraw-utils-0.1.1-with-react.html
http://jrjurman.com/test-excalidraw-utils/excalidraw-utils-0.1.1-with-react.html
author: JRJurman

body:
After doing a git bisect, it appears this is the first commit where React became a dependency: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/4b5270ab12bf13c2e8def10f5e264afea4e13bab @dwelle 

Additionally, it appears that this is the first commit where the interface for `exportToSvg` changes from being synchronous to asynchronous: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/f861a9fdd044e72e5b528c6e2d3da619e78cce05 @ad1992 

Honestly I think these can both be worked around with a documentation update. The React one seems odd since there's nothing clearly in the export flow that requires React, so ideally that one could be fixed. The promise one is most-likely fine since it's fairly trivial to work around, but it wasn't clear to me if that change was intentional or not.

body:
I feel that both operations are distinct enough that they shouldn’t be conflated.

The current eraser **deletes** everything it touches wholesale. It’s very useful and should not be replaced if/when the part-wise eraser is implemented. I also don’t think it should be hidden in the options panel.

I understand you might not want to clutter the UI but I’d put **„the deleter“** and **„the eraser“** next to each other as two dedicated buttons.

For the here proposed eraser, I like [how it works in browserboard](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/2052#issuecomment-965058960), if that is possible to implement.

![](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/23072548/141106948-aba7b3bb-27f6-43fc-997c-82f97c3a57f8.gif)
author: 0x53A

body:
> > Also, is it possible to set the eraser (or any other tool) to the double-tap for the Apple Pencil? I'm not sure how this works exactly.


> Tried, but sadly, no. To my knowledge it's not emitting any event we can subscribe to from javascript.

I don’t think it’s possible in the browser, that is, in Safari, but it might be possible if the browser is hosted inside an app. Then the app should be able to capture the events and forward them to the browser instance.

That would be relevant for, for example, Obsidian with the [excalidraw addon](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin).

So you could provide a hook that’s non-functional inside the browser but can be triggered from a host.

body:
To keep the editor offsets up to date, we query the closest scrollable container and listen on the `scroll` event:
https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/e92d1339732b8d5119ba8232fc904b0cbe7f0c0a/src/components/App.tsx#L1023-L1031

implem:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/e92d1339732b8d5119ba8232fc904b0cbe7f0c0a/src/utils.ts#L469-L490

If you scrollbars are not CSS-based, or you have nested scrolling containers, or something else is going on, you'll have to handle the offset updating yourself, by calling the `excalidrawApi.updateScene({ appState: { offsetTop, offsetLeft } })`, if I recall correctly.

body:
hey @abhaymahajanwh — the collaboration part consists of two parts: 1) the websocket server (excalidraw-room repo), and 2) Firebase Firestore (used for persistence). For pure collaboration without persistence, you don't need the Firebase part.

But if you do want persistence, then the thing you need to set up is:

1. initialize the Firestore database
2. get the config, stringify it and add it to `.env` as `REACT_APP_FIREBASE_CONFIG` key (see  https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/.env.production#L12)
3. make sure you deploy the Firestore rules (https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/firebase-project/firestore.rules) — you can also paste the rules manually in the Firestore dashboard

> Oops, you're right. The committed rules aren't up to date.
>
> Here's what you need:
>
> rules_version = '2';
> service firebase.storage {
>   match /b/{bucket}/o {
>     match /{files}/rooms/{room}/{file} {
>     	allow get, write: if true;
>     }
>     match /{files}/shareLinks/{shareLink}/{file} {
>     	allow get, write: if true;
>     }
>   }
> }
>
> Edit the if conditions to your needs (refer to
> https://firebase.google.com/docs/storage/security)

I wouldn't recommend detecting device type and auto enabling based on that for the simple reason that there are many other devices beyond iPads, and also detecting iPads is not that trivial as one would hope so... the ways to detect continually change with new versions of iPads being released, so such a code would require constant attention.

I am planing to spend some time with pen mode this weekend. My idea is to detect the pen already when you use click the toolbar with the pen. Perhaps that would improve the experience for you.

My own current routine is to touch the screen with the pen when the "Selection tool" is still active and then click the freedraw icon. This avoids the unintended line I sometimes create by placing my palm on the screen.
author: zsviczian

body: I am working on this PR (#4943) to resolve this issue.
author: zsviczian

body: @zsviczian ad your PR, I'm not against detecting on tool change, but maybe we should do that _on top_ of current canvas detection? But, neither solution will solve the issue @huntedman is facing. The only solution that comes to mind is preserving `penMode` across sessions.
author: dwelle
zsviczian

body:
@zsviczian #4943 is a really clever workaround! It does solve the issue of having to do 3 clicks in order to start drawing!

But this part makes me wonder:
> Without penDetected the pen-lock button will not be displayed
> Saving penDetected and penMode would mean that we shouldn't move penDetected to DeviceType context after all...

This behaviour seems more what I had in mind: when you open a drawing that was previously in penMode, it's automatically in penMode and the unlock button is also displayed. And whether or not #4943 sabotages the way I intended the behaviour?

On a slightly unrelated note, I noticed you released obsidian-excalidraw-plugin 1.6.20 Congrats 🥳 !
I'm a big fan of your work, and yesterdays update made me wonder, is there a way to set penMode and penDetected on by default in excalidraw? Perhaps similarly by using frontmatter tags like you did with excalidraw-export-dark, etc...

My IDEAL solution with the pen would be similar to "zen mode on by default" setting in obsidian-excalidraw-plugin. 

(Also let me know if this is discussion is off topic from the core excalidraw.)
author: huntedman

body:
> My IDEAL solution with the pen would be similar to "zen mode on by default" setting in obsidian-excalidraw-plugin.

This has crossed my mind as well.
But maybe the solution is to save penMode and penDetected with state. That would also be required to enable penMode by default similar to zenMode.

The point is, that DeviceType already holds information if the device is a mobile device, and if it has a touch screen. pen would logically fit this. But penMode is more like zen mode, view mode... which are app states.

I'll do another PR that will keep penDetected in appState, but will save it, it only takes few minutes, since there are not many changes required. Then We can try both and see which feels more natural.
author: zsviczian

body:
@dwelle #4955 feels more user friendly. The only drawback is penDetected remains in appState. However it also makes sense that it is in penState, since you can expect that next time the user uses the device she/he will still have a pen.

@huntedman, I think #4955 does exactly what you were looking for. I can use this to add an option to Obsidian-Excalidraw to enable penMode by default in the plugin...

body: I found a workaround that works fairly well. Use the [Set Grid](https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/blob/master/ea-scripts/Set%20Grid.md) script and make the Grid Size 2 and Major Grid Frequency 10. Only the Major Grid is visible and objects snap to the Minor Grid, and it's not noticeable.

author: emforem

body:
I've created fork with Custom elements. Work still in progress: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/2258a872960f36a5e868767564f3f56b244b518a

The change allows to create custom (temporary named peculiar) element, tool and action, which need to extend **ExcalidrawPeculiarElementImplementation**, **ExcalidrawPeculiarToolImplementation** from
`packages/excalidraw/element/peculiarElement.ts`
or **PeculiarAction** from `packages/excalidraw/actions/peculiarAction.tsx`

Elements, tools, or actions are declared outside of Excalidraw and registered with API: `excalidraw-app/App.tsx`. Examples of a new elements are in folder: `excalidraw-app/euclid/`. 

Remaining work: 
1.  Finish implementation of geometry related elements (to be close to feature parity with Geogebra)
      - needed to figure out which features needed for custom elements.
3. Migrate [feat: Support LaTeX and AsciiMath via MathJax on stem.excalidraw.com #6037](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6037) to the new custom (peculiar) element framework.
4. Refactoring current implementation:
     - rename peculiar to custom (and see if it is collide with current custom elements)
     - remove code from `excalidraw-app/` and move to own repository (geometry features aren't related to Excalidraw)
     - add test coverage
     - add documentation on how to implement own element, tool or action.

author: PappyEric

body:
In case someone needs this still.

You need to build your own docker image with either `ENV VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3002` the Dockerfile

OR

change it in `.env.production`


This is how i did it.

```shell 
mkdir excalidraw
cd excalidraw
git clone git clone https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw-room.git
git clone https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw.git
rm excalidraw/.dockerignore
touch docker-compose.yaml
```

docker-compose.yaml

```yaml
version: "3.8"

services:
  excalidraw:
    build:
      context: ./excalidraw
      args:
        - NODE_ENV=production
    container_name: excalidraw
    ports:
      - "3000:80"
    restart: on-failure
    stdin_open: true
    healthcheck:
      disable: true
    
  excalidraw-room:
    build:
      context: ./excalidraw-room
    environment:
      # Collaboration WebSocket
      PORT: 3002
    ports:
      - "3002:3002"
```

Go into `excalidraw/.env.production` and change `VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL=` to  
`VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3002`

Make sure that `VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL` url can be reached from your host. The browser is connecting to it.

```shell
docker compose up -d --build
```

Browse to http://127.0.0.1:3000 and start collaborating.
author: tiran133

body: Hi, tiran133. Thanks for your solution. It helps a lot. After deploying according to the way you gave me, I found that I can only access the collaboration feature on the local machine. author: Pradur241

body:
Since the docker compose file exposes the ports to the host via the ports section.
So there are available on localhost and on the host IP

```yaml
    ports:
      - "3000:80"
    
     ports:
      - "3002:80"
```

Instead of `VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL=http://127.0.0.1:3002` just replace the 127.0.0.1 with the hosts IP.
for ex. `VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL=http://192.168.1.42:3002`

Now you can connect to http://192.168.1.42:3000.
It should work from any computer within the same network.

author: Pradur241

body:
No this should be fine as long as you only change the first part of the port in the composer file

```yaml
 ports:
      - "10000:80"
```
If you want to change 3002 also you need to adjust the `VITE_APP_WS_SERVER_URL` accordingly.

author: madukan

body: Found the answer to my own [question above](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/4993#issuecomment-3704886633). Apparently it is a browser security issue (not a real issue) related. If I disable browser security My Chrome browser security was in "Standard Protection". Pushing that to "No Protection" helped eliminate this issue and test without proper SSL certificates (I was using self-signed). Here's the Chrome settings screenshot (NOTE: THIS IS **ABSOLUTELY NOT** RECOMMENDED, I used just for testing my own deployment locally only): Best way to solve this issue is to generate SSL certificates (or use a tool like `mkcert` to add a CA (Certificate Authority) to your OS (in MacOS, Mkcert adds the CA to Keychain automatically), while generating certificates for your Nginx proxy, or similar). So to sum up, this issue is nothing to do with Excalidraw nor NodeJS. Using appropriate SSL certificates to serve resources that Excalidraw use to encrypt the payload solves the issue.

body:
Hi. From the code it seems you're managing the video elements separately, so once you delete the element, only the element update itself is propagated to the other clients. They'll need to take care of removing the video from the DOM. Is my assessment correct?

(unrelated: you're deleting all elements on the canvas, not just target element, in your `.map()`).

Btw, would love to see how it works in practice if your app is public anywhere. I assume the video elements are on top of the canvas, so you won't be able to interleave it with other elements on canvas.

body:
Weird. Definitely not there when I do `npm install @excalidraw/excalidraw`

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/376842/162285217-047a87e1-b7e8-49d4-a984-39976ef619a5.png)

Who understands all this anyway though. 🤷 

At a guess, the issue is that `.d.ts` files are for declaring types that already exist, not for defining *new* types. For example you might have

    declare const window: Window;

But you wouldn't want Typescript to emit a type for that, so it doesn't. I *think* the right thing to do is to rename those two `.d.ts` files to just `.ts` and then import the types properly where you need to use them. Don't quote me on that though.
author: Timmmm

body: As mentioned in the [docs](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/packages/excalidraw/README.md#usage) currently we are exporting the types from `@excalidraw/excalidraw/types` path, we are working on improving the docs to improve the visbility and probably will move the types to `@types/excalidraw` soon as well.
author: ad1992

body:
I think that is unrelated. The whole `@excalidraw/excalidraw` has no definition of the types in `global.d.ts` (e.g. `MarkOptional`) yet they are used (e.g. in `types/actions/types.d.ts`.

I downloaded the Typescript example linked from the docs and get exactly the same error when I run `npx tsc`.

It did complain about some Typescript peer dependencies or something when I ran `npm install` so I had to use `npm install --force`, so *maybe* this error was silently ignored in earlier version of Typescript?

Anyway you can easily reproduce it like this if you don't believe me:

```
mkdir test
cd test
npm init
npm install @excalidraw/excalidraw
npm install @types/react
npm install typescript
```

And create `main.ts`:

```typescript
import * as Excalidraw from "@excalidraw/excalidraw";
Excalidraw;
```

And `tsconfig.json`:

```jsonc
{
  "compilerOptions": {
    "target": "es2016",                                  /* Set the JavaScript language version for emitted JavaScript and include compatible library declarations. */
    "module": "commonjs",                                /* Specify what module code is generated. */
    "esModuleInterop": true,                             /* Emit additional JavaScript to ease support for importing CommonJS modules. This enables `allowSyntheticDefaultImports` for type compatibility. */
    "forceConsistentCasingInFileNames": true,            /* Ensure that casing is correct in imports. */
    "strict": true,                                      /* Enable all strict type-checking options. */
    "skipLibCheck": false                                /* Skip type checking all .d.ts files. */
  }
}
```

Then

```
npx tsc
```

Note that it *does* work if you set `skipLibCheck: true`, but only because it doesn't bother checking for errors. And the Typescript example linked from the docs doesn't have that set so I'm still a bit confused about how this ever worked.
author: Timmmm

body:
Oh, I see. We _are_ using `skipLibCheck` as that's the default/recommended option (though I have not noticed any issues such as `any` types, while having the option enabled).

But since it's causing problems to people who have the option disabled, we should investigate. IMO the only way to solve this is to ensure the `d.ts` files are bundled/accounted for, because even if we move all the utility types into regular `.ts` files and import them as normal (which is the recommended way to do this anyway), there are some declarations which cannot be imported and must be in `d.ts` files.

By "Investigate sanitisation requirements" are you hoping to guard against [this sort of thing](https://book.hacktricks.xyz/pentesting-web/file-upload)?

Regarding the implementation, would the intention be to use [`imageCache`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/0ebe6292a310c19e7a4353fe9701a58bd7253896/src/components/App.tsx#L381) to create a zip file containing the scene data of the (selected?) elements, along with the associated image files?

I'm guessing the existing functionality - [encoded images as dataURLs before adding them to the scene](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7922ce129e569f0abed54158deba15506415af15/src/data/blob.ts#L251), etc. - could be left as it is while extending the library feature to allow for including images by refactoring the library feature to create a zip file (instead of JSON/excalidrawlib) using the `imageCache` as the source.

/cc @dwelle
author: null-hype

body:
> By "Investigate sanitisation requirements" are you hoping to guard against [this sort of thing](https://book.hacktricks.xyz/pentesting-web/file-upload)?

Yes.

> Regarding the implementation, would the intention be to use [`imageCache`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/0ebe6292a310c19e7a4353fe9701a58bd7253896/src/components/App.tsx#L381) to create a zip file containing the scene data of the (selected?) elements, along with the associated image files?
> 
> I'm guessing the existing functionality - [encoded images as dataURLs before adding them to the scene](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7922ce129e569f0abed54158deba15506415af15/src/data/blob.ts#L251), etc. - could be left as it is while extending the library feature to allow for including images by refactoring the library feature to create a zip file (instead of JSON/excalidrawlib) using the `imageCache` as the source.

The zip suggestion was mainly about use case, not implementation.

That said, it needs to account for all sorts of persistence models: browser (also moving from LS to IDB), library repository (database), filesystem (on export), host apps (make it extensible).

It's pretty much the same with canvas images, but the difference is that handling images separately is pretty annoying — as it already is with canvas images today. But with libraries, it'd be more so.

body:
Was looking into this and this looks like an issue with vite js using roll-up plugin and hasn't been fixed yet, the recommended solution to fix is to use the default export until it gets fixed in rollup plugin / vite itself. 
```js
const ExcalidrawComp = Excalidraw.default ? Excalidraw.default : Excalidraw;
```

and use <ExcalidrawComp/> in rendering.
https://github.com/vitejs/vite/issues/2139
https://github.com/rollup/plugins/issues/481

Sadly this affects a lot of popular libs as well like `react-select`, `react-switch` , `react-notification` and a lot more

Also here it like the issue is only with just default export,  as rollup is not able to identify the default export hence all libs using default export tend to fail so not using default export should fix this as well

author: scele

body:
This just hapend to me today. 
I found a work around if you have a previous backup. 

1. Export the (broken) drawing to `foo.excalidraw` file
2. Load a previous .excalidraw file from backup.
(Observe that the images are loaded correctly)
3. Re-Import the newly exported `foo.excalidraw` file
Observe that the images are restored. 

Granted you shouldn't have to export the file over and over like its 1995, and the chances that people have local exports of the file are slim. But if you do, then this will restore the images to the file.

author: svenn71

body:
Hi @svenn71 👋🏻, this was an [intentional change](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/4545) so that all tabs sync the data, and users never end up losing drawings due to getting overridden by the latest one. 
Earlier if there were multiple tabs, each with a separate drawing, and if you close a tab and reopen it again (or open a new tab), it will contain the last state from the other tab and no way to recover the lost drawing.

body:
Since you merged the PR back in January, I cannot have several independent excalidraw tabs open at the same time. The issue of "lost" drawings was something I discovered early and easily managed to work around by not doing it that way. For me the current implementation is even worse:

* I open a tab with excalidraw and drag-drop a file from file explorer to tab to edit
* I do some editing and discover that I need to look at another excalidraw drawing to copy some content
* I must now save my current drawing and open the other, I cannot have both on screen at the same time

Can you rationalize that the intentional change was so important to excalidraw that a change was introduced which hampered the ability to have several drawings open at the same time?

Do you see a solution to modify excalidraw in such a way that each tab actually could have a separate drawing?
author: svenn71

body:
Being forced to use just one Canvas at a time, then saving, the deleting the current one, and then creating/loading a new one is just bad UX. 

Let me create multiple independent canvases, even if we introduce an internal tab system inside the app, for switching between canvases. This way the browser tabs can still be synced, but Excalidraw can have multiple tabs/canvases available.

I believe this also relates to this issue (which is one of the most basic expected functionality, by users, in such tools): https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/1957

P.S.: I came to both of these issues from Google, looking for a way to create another canvas.
author: Zirafnik

body:
Sharing a workaround - running multiple instances of the browser should allow opening different excalidraw documents at the same time

Different tabs on the same instance do not work, as the local storage is still shared.

Easiest setup is one regular tab + one incognito tab, but more can be added by for example opening different profiles in chrome.
author: marcospgp

body:
I don't think the issue is solved from a UX perspective. I'll never click the _Reset canvas_ button, because I fundamentally don't want to reset the current canvas. I need separate canvases for things I discuss with software engineers, and for things I discuss with customers.

EDIT: The workaround I found is:
- I save the current canvas as an `.excalidraw` file.
- I make a copy of that file and name it for the new context I want.
- I open the copy.
- I erase the canvas of the new copy.

This way I am fairly sure that I don't accidentally erase and overwrite the other canvas.

author: cmheisel

body:
Thanks for running the test. Additional question: is this happening when the scene is very complex? Here is one theory:

1. The user clicks the **Save** button (this is the _user gesture_, which is valid a period of time `Δuser_gesture`).
2. Excalidraw prepares the to-be-saved data, which may take some time `Δprep`.
3. The browser tries to show the file save dialog, but if `Δprep > Δuser_gesture`, the user gesture is no longer good.

In `browser-fs-access`, the library used by Excalidraw under the hood, we now support a new programming model, where the to-be-saved data may be passed as a `Promise<Blob>` as well (apart from a pre-prepared `Blob` and streaming `Response`). 

```js
// `blobOrPromiseBlobOrResponse` is a `Blob`, a `Promise<Blob>`, or a `Response`.
await fileSave(
  blobOrResponseOrPromiseBlob,
  options,
  existingHandle,
  throwIfExistingHandleNotGood
);
```

Before we explore this, it would be helpful to know if this is the problem. Judging from your video (where apparently the data is available immediately), it's not, but I wanted to be sure.

Nevertheless, we can move to promise-based file saving. But I'm not seeing `fs-browser-access` supporting it yet?

body:
I found the problem.  It is very simple and it is not excalidraw issue.
I had two 4k monitors 32" within Windows OS.  Windows cannot handle the 4K in all situations nor all the other software.  Icons too small, fonts ridiculously small.  I like to use the native resolution because the  3840x2160 gives a lot of vertical real state.
So, I changed font sizes in many programs.  Firefox is one of those.  I had unclicked (not selected the allow pages to choose their own font.) 
I put it back on and the problem went away.
I can change fonts without any problem.
![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/44947400/166891185-9816a62e-afbc-4396-baf3-c13c984feffa.png)

@ad1992 it's not a bug?

author: dwelle

body:
Continuing the private chat discussion here -> 
As per UX, I feel it's not intuitive to disable selection and I am not sure why you want to disable selection, even when you right-click the element that gets selected. 
As per the same flow, I select an element -> right click, -> lock, hence I am expecting then when I click on an element again it will select it, and then I can either use the context menu/shortcut to unlock it.
Miro, and Figjam both allow the selection of lock elements and show a visual indicator to unlock as well which makes it more intuitive. 
Hence I would suggest we allow selection and show some indicator (eg lock icon) that makes it clear that the element is locked so the user needs to unlock it.

And this is most helpful during collaboration where users don't know which elements are locked and which aren't
And one more thing, disabling selection+ not showing indicator might look like a bug when someone tries to interact with the elements (eg move, resize) unless the user is aware of the lock functionality in Excalidraw

In case we want to disable selection then I suggest showing some indicator that makes it more clear how to unlock and why the selection is disabled but to align with other tools I think we should allow selection + some indicator to unlock when selected as well.

body:
I think I found the issue!

It seems to be a that `pendingImageElement` that is used when inserting images and the corresponding element in the scene are two different instances of the same element. It appears this is caused by [src/excalidraw-app/collab/Portal.tsx#L120](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/1ed1529f96eebbd744023463abebf5ee18292532/src/excalidraw-app/collab/Portal.tsx#L120) (It's even explained in the comment above!)

When I replace the line with `mutateElement(element, { status: "saved" })` I can't reproduce the issue anymore.

author: ychinghin

body:
It relates to the background shape simplification algo. If you draw a shape with fast strokes of your mouse/pen, it'll create a line with few points, and in turn the background shape will be simplified much more.

We should check if we can make the simplification less aggressive (thought it can affect performance).
author: dwelle

body:
Currently we use the first column which has all numeric values as the y axis, and the other as labels. And in this case we incorrectly parse the `424.` as a non-a-number (due to the trailing `.`).

We should fix this, and we should prefer the second column as values when both columns are numeric.

author: johtso

body:
Making everything immutable could be cool, and Immer/Immutable.js was on the table. The biggest hurdle is to ensure there's no regression in performance — which Immer is known for (or at least was in the past). For regular apps the Proxy things and magic that Immer does would not be noticeable, but for apps like Excalidraw, it could be (when moving elements over the canvas) — e.g., in many cases to make it super fast we just mutate elements + force rerender. We'd need to test.

There will also be cases where mutation is actually desirable not just for performance (https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/5143).
author: dwelle

body: Good point regarding performance.. with an application like this where a single user action can cause a large number of simultaneous updates, and the efficiently is reflected in the responsiveness of the interface, you can't always use the nice abstractions..

body:
Ok, so I dug around a bit and this might be doable, but heavily depends on the tablet/styles/OS. What tabled and OS are you using?

For example, on Wacom, the eraser on the other end of the stylus:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/167488685-2f6278cd-678f-43a4-829b-3d72898f1078.png)

emits `event.button === 5` which we can work with. 

But, on Windows it works only if the `Use Windows Ink` is enabled:

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/167492303-65aa282d-6504-4107-a95b-11653a35518a.png)

Otherwise it doesn't emit any events.

The stylus side buttons are by default mapped to generic buttons that we can't/don't want to use as an eraser (right click for opening contextmenu, and a double click). 

![image](https://user-images.githubusercontent.com/5153846/167488859-ba72d37c-d601-4e73-9771-a2a941579fe7.png)

Trying to remap them to something useful doesn't work on Windows _unless_ you disable the Windows Ink feature, which in turn makes the Eraser button not work :).

May work differently on MacOS.

body:
I think it's a combination of the stylus + Windows/OS driver or something interfering with it. But browsers may come into play too.

But the upshot is this: if we don't get the event, we can't do anything on our side. The only thing that can be done is tinkering with the stylus drivers or OS settings. But that's not very scalable since only a tiny fraction of users will do this (and learn that they need to do it in the first place).

body: Looks like this is fixed for well-behaving systems that report the eraser functionality correctly in #7496 by @dwelle. My One by Wacom on Linux in Firefox is not well-behaving, and I suppose there are quite a few other weird systems that aren't either, so I think the workaround suggested [before in this thread](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5166#issuecomment-1122528652) would still be nice to have: > As for somewhat of a **workaround suggestion**: Illustration how it could work: > > I feel like two different styles of erase button shortcuts would be good even without the Pen/remapped buttons. for better UX (less friction, since the switch would be much faster and without thinking). This is similar to how the spacebar temporarily switches to the Hand-tool, so it's not entirely out of place. Ideally this would be configurable to any input, so that no remapping is needed on the tablet-side and its functionality does not change in other applications, but maybe handling arbitrary configured inputs is a little out of scope for Excalidraw. In the meantime, I've found a workaround on Linux using [Input Remapper](https://github.com/sezanzeb/input-remapper/). Mapping the stylus button to `key(e).hold().key(e)` will switch to the eraser when pressing the chosen button and revert back to the previous tool on release. ![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/10365257/e7db45cf-026f-4450-baab-ab3cac3c72b5) With an erase-while-hold shortcut directly in Excalidraw, this would be simpler and more likely to work with simpler tools and in other situations as well.
