body:
Here is a bug report for the latest iOS with the same error message: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/7840

I suspect it is related to the use of browser-fs-access -- either a problem with that library for later browser versions or perhaps just the way it is used in Excalidraw which leads to an immediate promise rejection.
https://github.com/GoogleChromeLabs/browser-fs-access

Looks like there have been issues in the past with browser-fs-access and Excalidraw:
https://github.com/GoogleChromeLabs/browser-fs-access/issues?q=is%3Aissue++excalidraw

author: pkkarn

body: From the screenshots above it looks like you're also updating the `isDeleted` flag to `false` in unrelated types as well as in actual code. The idea behind the proposed change is that we have a generic type `NonDeleted<>` but it's incorrectly allowing also `isDeleted: true`. We need to only allow `isDeleted: false`. This will likely cause typecheck issues in places where we declare we accept non-deleted elements, but are passing any kind of elements.

body: Hi @dwelle @ad1992 Issue : #6746 I wanted to clarify my understanding of the requirement and outline the expected modifications to ensure consistency with the change of setting `isDeleted` to `false` in `NonDeleted` instances. My presumption that this change would improve 🔧 1. **Data integrity** : we can be confident that there is no accidental risk of deleting any elements when we work with non deleting elements 2. **Code maintenance**: we know that all non deleted instances will have isDeleted set to false 3. **Type safety**: we make sure that we only deal with non deleted instances and not concern ourselves with deleted elements preventing erroneous operations on deleted elements Based on discussions, I presume absolute requirement is to modify the `NonDeleted` type in one specific file to have `isDeleted: false`. **What i want to ask** ❓ What kind of changes do you expect in **affected code**(_code sections that work with NonDeleted to ensure they are updated to accommodate the modified NonDeleted type)_ to ensure consistency with the what i presume to be the absolute requirement, since @dwelle expects a lot of refactoring in the above comment? **Absolute requirement** ⬇️

author: MK884

body:
I'd like to pick this one up — the previous assignment and PR #11088 both appear abandoned (no activity / closed without review).

I have a working implementation: `NonDeleted<>` narrowed to `isDeleted: false`, type guards made generic so they preserve `NonDeleted` through `.filter()` and narrowing, producers like element creation / `getSelectedElements` / `duplicateElements` now return `NonDeleted` elements, and functions that don't need the guarantee (rendering, geometry, etc.) widened to plain `ExcalidrawElement`. Typecheck is clean and the full test suite passes with zero snapshot diffs. Opening a PR now.

body:
@Harshita-Kanal you can use the `appState` property in `initialData` for this. 
You can set 👇🏻 

```
appState.currentItemRoundness // for roundness
appState.currentItemRoughness // for sharpness
```
and many such more properties as needed, here is the [full list](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/types.ts#L101) of attributes which you can use to customise.

* But I believe that the grid is very important, enough to at least have a dedicated button; or two, one for visuals and one for enabling snapping. Probably at the bottom left next to the zoom level etc. 

Also, along alternative textures, many programs have variable scaling for grids. * These mock-up controls at the bottom left could be a prototype:

* These are some common backgrounds used in drawing apps: I personally like the current one and the dots. Probably the isometric ones should have some dashed lines or faded/bold parts.

body:
> @dwelle is there anyone still working on this? if not, i would like to take a shot

Hi @donglixiaoche, the issue is still open.
I'm sorry, I completely forgot to update it :(

I started to solve the problem but couldn't finish, here is **what I found** :

Frame management is located here: **`/packages/excalidraw/frame.ts`**
Arrows display management is here: **`packages/excalidraw/renderer/renderElement.ts`**, inside "switches".

Binding between elements is managed in the file : **`packages/excalidraw/element/binding.ts`**
In here, you can find the function `getHoveredElementForBinding` which allows you to display or not the binding's highlighting.
I think that the best way is to add a check in this function to determine whether or not the two elements are in the same frame. You can simply get the **`frameId`** of the **`hoveredElement`** but we do not have the **`frameId`** of the arrow we moving. We can perhaps add an extra parameter to retrieve it.

body:
@javigomez generating Excalidraw diagrams from text is in the pipeline :)

However for the above json, rn the element requires all the mandatory attributes to be passed as specified in the [element types](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/src/element/types.ts) hence its failing as some of the element properties are missing and for the above json its `points` attribute for `arrow` which is missing so after adding `points` it should work

``` js
{
  "type": "excalidraw",
  "version": 2,
  "source": "https://excalidraw.com",
  "elements": [
    {
      "type": "ellipse",
      "version": 1,
      "versionNonce": 123,
      "x": 50,
      "y": 50,
      "width": 100,
      "height": 100,
      "strokeColor": "#000000",
      "backgroundColor": "#ffffff",
      "strokeWidth": 2,
      "strokeStyle": "solid",
      "roughness": 1,
      "opacity": 100
    },
    {
      "type": "ellipse",
      "version": 1,
      "versionNonce": 456,
      "x": 200,
      "y": 50,
      "width": 100,
      "height": 100,
      "strokeColor": "#000000",
      "backgroundColor": "#ffffff",
      "strokeWidth": 2,
      "strokeStyle": "solid",
      "roughness": 1,
      "opacity": 100
    },
    {
      "type": "arrow",
      "version": 1,
      "versionNonce": 789,
      "x": 100,
      "y": 100,
      "width": 100,
      "height": 0,
      "strokeColor": "#000000",
      "backgroundColor": "transparent",
      "strokeWidth": 2,
      "strokeStyle": "solid",
      "roughness": 1,
      "opacity": 100,
      "points":[[0,0],[109.61328125,-0.71484375]]
    }
  ]
}


```

However we are working on simplifying the API in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/6546 which we will be merging soon and this will help in generating diagrams with minimal better API

body:
I would like to work on the search aspect of this feature and leave `Favorites` as a separate task. My thought was as follows:

- Add a magnifying glass here https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7c9cf30909c6c368407994cb25e22292b99eee5d/src/components/Sidebar/SidebarHeader.tsx#L43
- When it is clicked it opens a text box.
- That text box is used to filter the displayed items [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7c9cf30909c6c368407994cb25e22292b99eee5d/src/components/LibraryMenuItems.tsx)
- Communication between the two will happen via the [`SidebarPropsContext`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/7c9cf30909c6c368407994cb25e22292b99eee5d/src/components/Sidebar/common.ts#L40)

All suggestions, improvements, and pointers are appreciated @ad1992 since you were triaging this task.

body: Rather than, or in addition to, adding a search and favorites, it would be amazing to just have the items in your Library searchable and usable via the existing Command palette. This would allow you to simply use the hotkey to pull up the Command palette (cmd + /), type the name of the component (eg. cloudfront), then hit Enter, and the component will be added to the diagram. The advantage of this is that it can all be done easily via keyboard rather than having to move the mouse over to the library (it may even be closed), click the search area, type, move mouse down to component, then select.

body:
We're not clipping on export by design, and only clip  when you export a single frame. The reasoning is we don't currently consider frames themselves (name, outline...) as exportable elements and exporting content without frames but clipping enabled felt weird.

@zsviczian  Are there any use cases you have in mind?
author: dwelle

body:
This is the basic use-case (https://youtu.be/yZQoJg2RCKI). I extended image referencing to work with frames and would love to further extend to allow the user to set clipping or not in the reference link. Imagine a more complex architectural drawing that you want to explain part-by-part in a document. You would add frames to mark each of the parts and embed in the document using frame references. 

That said, this is not top of mind for me.

body:
I have used the PWA, but it would be much better to have a proper app.

Chrome does not allow changing the download folder, and even if I enable "Ask where to save" -option in Chrome, I can only ever choose "Downloads". This makes it very cumbersome to manage drawings. It would also be very nice to be able to open Excalidraw files directly from the file manager
author: wlinna

body:
>  I can only ever choose "Downloads"

That's a weird restriction - does it apply to any file you are downloading? Could be some corporate policy?

Choosing the location works for me in the latest Chrome (MacOS) - in PWA or not.
author: Mrazator

body:
> That's a weird restriction - does it apply to any file you are downloading? Could be some corporate policy?

It applies to all downloads.  I think it's a limitation of Android. Other Android browsers don't give any more choice on this matter as far as I know, at least not Firefox.

I have tested this on a Samsung tablet and a Samsug phone, both of them personal. They have the same behavior, except that my phone lets me choose between SD card and Downloads, while my tablet (which has no SD card) only lets me choose Downloads.

> Choosing the location works for me in the latest Chrome (MacOS) - in PWA or not.

I can choose my download location freely on desktop versions as well, but this is about mobile. Or maybe I just misunderstood, and by MacOS you refer to iOS / iPadOS?

author: dwelle

body:
@dwelle I don't think I had. I've looked into the code and here's what I've found:

In the examples above the transformation is done by `resizeSingleElement` which doesn't update x,y coordinates, it only moves points in the points array. This might be the issue.

The resizing I wrote (`resizeMultipleElements`) updates x,y and points relative to the new x,y coordinates, so the first point is always [0,0]. There's no such bug when resizing > 1 elements or performing a flip action, which is also dependent on the `resizeMultipleElements`.

Also there are some other bugs with `resizeSingleElement` (e.g. elements with bound text couldn't be flipped by dragging; wrong center calculation when resizing a text element with alt), so I think it would be beneficial to get rid of this function and update `resizeMultipleElements` so it can handle resizing of single elements.
author: alex-kim-dev

body:
> @dwelle I don't think I had. I've looked into the code and here's what I've found:
> 
> In the examples above the transformation is done by `resizeSingleElement` which doesn't update x,y coordinates, it only moves points in the points array. This might be the issue.
> 
> The resizing I wrote (`resizeMultipleElements`) updates x,y and points relative to the new x,y coordinates, so the first point is always [0,0]. There's no such bug when resizing > 1 elements or performing a flip action, which is also dependent on the `resizeMultipleElements`.
> 
> Also there are some other bugs with `resizeSingleElement` (e.g. elements with bound text couldn't be flipped by dragging; wrong center calculation when resizing a text element with alt), so I think it would be beneficial to get rid of this function and update `resizeMultipleElements` so it can handle resizing of single elements.

The issue with that is the code you have written would have to be changed a lot to work with single elements. Currently, as you know, when you scale rotated elements it scales them based on their axis aligned bounding box, and not their actual bounding box and when you rotate single elements the behavior that makes most sense is to scale based on their actual bounds (you can scale them horizontally even when they are rotated 45deg). I don't know the codebase that well and you probably know your code better than I do, so this is probably skill issues on my part, but I think that modifying your code could potentially introduce more bugs. 

My PR fixes it the more straightforward way. You can take a look at what I did if you like.

This is what I mean when I talk about the axis aligned bounding box thing:
![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/107800884/e0f832d8-3f97-46ad-842a-4b33df42fd9a)

Notice how when you scale one the handlebars for scaling are rotated:
![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/107800884/18de339d-e39a-43d6-a7fd-59600d7741a8)

body:
FYI: The issue is that index.html is excluded by the `*` in the `.dockerignore` and not explicitly (re-)enabled by a `!index.html` like eg. the `yarn.lock` is.

Adding this to the `.dockerignore` still fails the build as the index.html contains some kind of template syntax like [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/index.html#L81).
What script/tool is responsible for replacing those template values?

body:
Ok digging further...

You are ignoring (by default) node_modules\ folder in the .dockerignore but not rebuilding with yarn install in the dockerfile. So basically there are no modules available in the docker build. Although I did explicitly add a yarn install, but that still didn't resolve.

However when I DON'T ignore node_modules it works.

author: ad1992

body:
This is actually by design. When dragging elements, they're added to the frame under cursor. If the selected element is outside the frame, we don't add it because it would become invisible.

Dragging elements that are in different frames seems like too much an edge case to write specific rules for this.

/cc @ryan-di
author: dwelle

body:
When dragging elements to the frame, the logic is this:
- for the dragged elements, when the cursor is in the frame (which would lead to the highlighting of the target frame), the ones that at least intersect with the frame are added to the target frame
- the ones that are not in the target frame are removed from it (by setting `element.frameId` to `null`, which is why element from "frame 34" was removed from it in your clip)

I suppose, for the elements that were in some other frames, if they are not in the target frame on pointer up, they could just be kept in their original frame if they at least intersect with original frame. But then again, the logic gets a bit more complicated if they are grouped elements, which we deem as "atomic" elements. Luckily, we have all the relevant helpers already.

And I guess when trying to align items from different frames by moving them together, the current behavior can be annoying. I'm happy to take a look at it when I'm a bit more available :) cheers!
author: ryan-di

body: That's good to hear, thanks 🙂 My use case right now is preparing slides for a presentation in Google Slides. I have a diagram, which I build up a bit at a time. I'm using a frame per slide, and sometimes I want to rearrange something. It's important to keep the relative positions consistent within each frame, otherwise parts of the diagram would jump around between slides. As an example, these three frames would be three slides. Using frames make it easier to organise within Excalidraw, keeping a consistent bounding box around the diagrams as elements appear/disappear, and being able to move whole frames together. Loving the frames!

author: muendelezaji

body:
First, I also would like such a feature.  I find that without a grid that I can't maintain a consistent scale or zoom level as I draw and move around the diagram.

Second, I think this would actually be easy to implement adding an option that sets `renderGrid` to `true` at: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/b5652b8e36abcc813478c65cdbe248aae774e5db/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L1729

This would be separate from `gridModeEnabled`/`isGridModeEnabled`, which also controls snapping to the grid.  Thus instead of ` renderGrid: isGridModeEnabled(this)`, that line would become something like `renderGrid: this.app.state.renderGrid || isGridModeEnabled(this)` with a new field `this.app.state.renderGrid`.  I've done a test of the idea by modifying this line to `renderGrid: true` which indeed causes a grid without snapping.

Third, the most complicated part seems to be adding the option controlling.  I am not familiar enough with the code to have a full picture and would be happy for any guidance, but it looks like there is both `this.app.state` and `this.app.prop`, and I am not sure which one should contain this option.  Then we need to add the command to change the option, potentially a keyboard shortcut, save and load preferences, and so forth.  I can try digging through the code, but on my own, I might misunderstand what the right way to do these things is.

Finally, another area I'd like guidance on from the Excalidraw team is the question of what to name the command in the GUI and internal property in the code.  I am not sure what the Excalidraw policy on command and property/API name stability is, or what the naming conventions for these properties is.  If I were starting from a clean sheet, I would name it "Toggle Show Grid" (property `showGrid`) and instead of the current "Toggle Grid" (property `gridMode`) use "Toggle Snap to Grid" (property `snapToGrid`).

### Extensions

There are other approaches and extensions for this that one might consider.  I want to document these, but I don't think we should let these ideas block us from the immediately useful feature of just allowing the showing of the grid without snapping.

- Make it possible to enable "Snap to Grid" without showing the grid
- Temporarily enable snap to grid when on when a modifier key is held down (e.g., Cmd/Ctrl).  This is complementary to how holding down Cmd/Ctrl disables snap to grid when grid mode is active.  (Note that there might be a usability argument that Shift should be the modifier instead of Ctrl, so one doesn't accidentally activate it when pressing some other keyboard shortcut.  I am not sure.)
- Add hooks that let apps that Excalidraw is embedded in redefine the static scene (e.g., render an xy-axis) or have other programmatic control over the static scene.

Hey @ad1992 

Indeed, check the picture below.

I want to be able to make a "master/main" component (it's essentially a grouping).
I want to "clone" a component multiple times...
If I make a change in the "master/main component", then Instance 1 and Instance 2 also are affected.

Example:

- If I change "Main" text from "Submit" to "Proceed", then Instance q becomes "Proceed", but Instance 2 stays "Review" because it had a change after cloning.

body:
Let me cook:

**Main use case:** For diagramming (would mostly use it when navigating and editing flowcharts)

i don't want to constantly have to reach for my mouse to create new nodes in the fllowchart. The flowchart update was well received and we now have bindings (CMD/CTRL + <arrow>) to create new nodes, but it's just a pain to use that short cut.

A nice-to-have would be:
- The ability to use (CMD/CTRL +h/j/k/l) to create new nodes (this is the thing I'd really like to have)
- A model interface to navigate nodes. For example, you can move around nodes in normal mode pressing h/j/k/l and hit `i` to  edit the current note you're at.
- (optional) vim bindings to edit the text itself

@dwelle @ad1992

body:
This is a critical feature to be able to use Excalidraw for conference presentations as most conferences require slides to be submitted via PDF ahead of time. Eventually I hope conferences will accept shared links to presentations, but for now this feature is super important to increase Excalidraw usage at major conferences.

As a short term solution, having the ability to export all slides as numbered images would allow for manual PDF conversion.

Thanks to all the contributors and staff of Excalidraw for making such a fantastic tool. I hope to use it for conference presentations sometime soon!

author: ashraf2033

body:
The Clipboard API restricts clipboard access to bolster security, notably outside paste events, leading to differences in pasting content from diverse sources.

For example, an Excalidraw image permits right-click pasting since it's stored as text. However, content directly copied from the file explorer exclusively pastes using the standard action (Ctrl+V). In our codebase, Ctrl + V triggers the paste event, providing access to clipboard files. Conversely, the context menu paste function constructs a custom ClipboardEvent by reading the clipboard with `navigator.clipboard.readText` function or the fallback `navigator.clipboard.read`

[This](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/actions/actionClipboard.tsx#L48) triggers the paste action from the context menu

body:
Use props `validateEmbeddable`

```
// allow all sites
<Excalidraw validateEmbeddable={() => true} />
```

How/Where does one do this in the self-hosted docker image? I'm having the same issue.
author: thedivtagguy

body:
You'd have to edit this in the source code it's in here 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/2535d7305485a032b5a860a4538870a0c2d09c5a/excalidraw-app/App.tsx#L807

To rebuild the docker image assuming have node/npm/yarn and docker installed already can clone the repo then:

`yarn && yarn build:app:docker`

I made a local fork (Gitea instance I self host) and had it rebuild the docker image and push to a local registry to use and it works but you also need to include a wildcard or specific domains to the allowed list in the `packages/element/src/embeddable.ts` as well and this requires that the target domains being included in the iframe have appropriate same-domain response headers so YMMV.

---

For simpler solution for those like myself using the Docker image for self hosting this could be exposed via some env variables but will complicate the code a bit for sure too and am unsure if there's already a pattern being used here for that kind of thing.

body:
We are sorry about the issue, it's likely due to the previously broken undo/redo implementation.

However, as part of https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/7348, we've recently shipped improvements for undo/redo on excalidraw.com & will be shipped on plus.excalidraw.com soonish!

Hence this issue should not be anymore reproducible.

author: sivanbecker

body:
I am experiencing a very similar if not the same issue. 
On my side, the flickering stops as soon as I reduce the number of active collaboration sessions back to 1.
testing scenarios:
- same pc, 2 sessions
- different pcs, 2 sessions
- pc and smartphone, 2 sessions

Since it seems to be about how web embeds are synced between two sessions, I guess this is not a new issue (as in, this was working before and is now broken) but rather an architectural/foundational one in the sense of a missing feature that is to be developed.

Possible solutions from my perspective:


**Solution 1: Async Views on Web Embeds**
scenario: the content of web embeds is excluded from collaboration sync. 
kinda defeats the purpose of live collaboration, but... 
- would make it more usable because everyone will be able to talk about the information (currently, web embeds break collaboration completely)
- people will be able to run video calls and share the screen or stream to talk about live information in the web embeds

Probably a fast and feasible fix.


**Solution 2: Integrate Streaming**
scenario: web embeds are async, as described above. If one of the collab users interacts with a web embed, it becomes locked for the interacting user. the web embed frame for the other users is then replaced by a stream/broadcast of the user's view in control until the user stops interacting. 
- would create a seamless collaboration 

However, this requires capturing the active web embed and streaming it to a given address. Every time someone interacts with something, this something is replaced by the streaming address for non-interacting users. This shouldn't be fully automated due to potential performance issues. 
To mitigate this issue, use the same dialogue between users as in Microsoft Teams Screen Sharing. Here is a proposal for adapting this dialogue to Excalidraw collaboration:

1. the first user interacting with a web embed becomes its owner until changed
2. the owner can decide to broadcast 
3. broadcasts will be announced to the users via a modal (or similar). they can confirm to join the session. 
4. on joining, 
    - EITHER, do the "web embed to streaming swap" as described above
    - OR simply lead people to the full-screen streaming link (reduces "collaboration richness" as it lead the focus from the canvas to a different website, but is easier to implement)
5. if there is no session, users can interact with web embeds freely but asynchronous 
6. changing the owner of a web embed requires an active hand-over dialogue between the owner and requester

body:
Hi @mazijian-pp , 

The  issue seems to be related to how the arrow element handles text prompts and the interaction between the prompt and the arrow's structure. To rectify this issue, the code responsible for managing text prompts in relation to arrows should be modified to ensure the arrow remains unaffected and that prompts and the incoming text do not overlap with the arrow. Additionally, the arrow should not lose any part when no text is entered during the prompt in a way similiar to how it works with line element; what you described with the refresh might just be an side effect of the above mentioned problem.

>             ...note.excalidraw?.appState,
>             collaborators: [],
>           },
>           files: note?.excalidraw?.files,
>           scrollToContent: true,
>         }
>       : undefined
>   }
> />
> ```

This is the definitive fix actually. At least for the time being.

body: Hello @ppfeiler, great work observing and identifying this issue. The problem at hand pertains to arrows, specifically. When arrows are positioned near shapes or text inside frames, they exhibit unusual behavior. They tend to create duplicates or become trapped within the frame. Even when we attempt to remove the entire frame, these duplicated arrows persist. I'll try and fix this bug.
author: mvaibhav77

body:
The arrows not being copied is definitely due to https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/7157 — the behavior right now is that arrows that are bound to shapes within a frame are not physically the frame children anymore, which solved several issues.

> When arrows are positioned near shapes or text inside frames, they exhibit unusual behavior. They tend to create duplicates or become trapped within the frame. Even when we attempt to remove the entire frame, these duplicated arrows persist. I'll try and fix this bug.

Can you create a new issue for those bugs specifically, with repro steps? We should make sure we fix the underlying bug, without reverting the behavior that #7157 introduced.
author: dwelle

body: Hi @dwelle, I think this issue is enough to tackle other related bugs related to frames and bounded arrows. I'll make a list and pin it here. Will that be okay or I should create new issues?

body:
I think the bug talked in this issue can be solved by including bounded arrows in the frame which:-
- Have both start and end bounds inside of the same frame.
- Is start bounded but not end bounded and inside of the frame completely(have to find a way to check if they are inside the frame completely. I think this functionality is not there).
- Is unbounded on both sides and inside of the frame (current condition).

body:
I'm using Live Collaboration and have been considering upgrading to Plus for some of the features such as commenting.  However, I've encountered the "couldn't save to backend" error several times now.  I typically copy a portion of my drawing from one Live Collaboration page to a new one and that generally resolves the problem for a while.  Most of my downloaded (backup) .excalidraw files are about 1.5 - 2 MB, though I've had some as large as 10 MB.  Nerd stats say total storage is about 536k on a page that's been problematic.  When I visually look at the drawings I've created, they can be large, but I don't feel that they are even close to being too large to work with a modern application.
Does anyone else have this issue and found resolution?  At a minimum, Excalidraw should warn me when I'm close a limit and not just when I've hit it.  Better would be to increase the drawing size limit.  I'd be willing to upgrade to Plus if more storage is part of the deal, however I don't see that mentioned anywhere.  I'd love to keep using Excalidraw for is ease of use, but the repeated failure to save will soon be a deal killer.
author: bioagbob

body:
For others with this issue, note that Excalidraw Support sent me the following response, indicating there is indeed a storage limit to free 'Live collaboration' pages, but Excalidraw Plus (paid) does not have a limit.

"The backend database we use on excalidraw.com has a 1MB limit — the excalidraw.com collab rooms are meant for quick collaboration with people, not for long-term storage 😇. There's no such limit on Excalidraw+.
Note that the Storage size shown in the screenshot relates to the local scene size, outside the collaboration room. We need to improve both the error, and the size shown in stats — for starters!"

body:
I know this issue is old, but I'm experiencing a similar problem and I think I have a solution (Win 11).

I'm starting to think that these are issues affecting only AMD GPUs and it's not limited to excalidraw.

I was having a similar issue with the text cursor while browsing (I use Chrome) and I fixed it by changing the mouse pointer icon from the OS mouse config panel.

I have tried the same solution for the cursor used by the OS inside excalidraw and the problem looks gone.

The steps I took are the following:
1 - Win+R
2 - Search for "main.cpl"
3 - Pointers
4 - Inside "Customize section", select the "**Precision Selection**" icon.
5 - Replace it with something similar from the same folder, in my case I went with "cross_im.cur"

Hope it helps

body:
Using following approach, you can interact with AI in different ways, inside of obsidian excalidraw:

https://github.com/zsviczian/obsidian-excalidraw-plugin/pull/1252#issuecomment-1661283382

The difference, it doesn't show a chat in the side bar, instead you can connect text elements with prompts using arrows directly on the canvas. Think of it as evolution in AIs UI/UX, a step forward from linear chats into spatial canvas.

body:
the ux is so bad @7flash 

4 years I'm telling AI devs to do iterative UX (i.e. chat or similar), still the same one-shot UX
author: louis030195

body:
@louis030195 My idea for iterative UX is to connect elements with labeled arrows, writing prompts as a labels on the arrows, connecting input and output, in this way the output of one prompt can be input to the next prompt, as well retaining the history.

In this demo you can see the first version of website is rendered on the canvas from generated code, then I connect another arrow from given code with request of improvements, and it renders second version

![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/4569866/1e13f345-dfbe-41af-bffc-78efb4e125b9)

This is full demo with AI agents creation:

https://www.youtube.com/watch?v=V-rI_ughijw

body:
@zsviczian the `ready` and `readyPromise` were not really conveying whether component is ready to use (which should be happening after component is initialized and its ready to use) and now since its simply a callback so you should be able to achieve the same result using the `excalidrawAPI` as well (you have to use state - you can check the docs wip [here](https://docs-git-docs-next-excalidraw.vercel.app/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/excalidraw-api)), let us know if this doesn't work out for you.
However we plan to bring in `onReady` or something similar which will make sure that. the component is initialized and ready to use when triggered

body: I increased the resources allocation (CPU from 4 to 8, memory from 2 to 4 and swap from 1 to 1.5) and it worked. Would it be possible to specify the resources needed in the docker compose? According to https://stackoverflow.com/a/57135933 and https://docs.docker.com/compose/compose-file/compose-file-v3/#resources this may achieved with a configuration like: ``` version: "3.8" services: excalidraw: build: context: . args: - NODE_ENV=development container_name: excalidraw # .... deploy: resources: limits: cpus: '' memory: M reservations: cpus: '' memory: M ```

body:
I can repro this issue, but it has to do with an alert that is opened then closed for me. 

I created a repo to reproduce the cursor drawing being off and when you resize the window it's fixed. Like @BleedingDev mentioned, but that's only because the initial alert is now done rendering. Could there be something that collapses in your view that isn't there after the page is initially loaded? or something that is rendered only on first load? 

To try my fix, clone the repro, change the `.env.development` var to true and it will try to render the excalidraw after the alert is dismissed. This then fixes the cursor issue. 

[Here is the repo](https://github.com/domshyra/excalidrawIssue7312), it should have all the steps in the readme to experiment with.

body: Select the elements you want to change in Obsidian-Excalidraw and click the palette icon on the bottom left. There you'll see a Sloppiness setting and an Edges setting; you can sharpen your corners and clean up the lines by clicking your preference. Whatever you change those to will be saved and applied to future elements you create so you don't have to reselect your preferences. Let me know if this was confusing at all!

author: QuakeWang

body:
Hey @QuakeWang when you're in the grid mode, snapping to objects is disabled. This is by design, because we want grid mode to take a higher priority. 

You could use the align functions as suggested by @editfund-founder or you could turn grid mode off first and then turn on "snap to objects" to align the objects together. 

Tip: when you select "snap to objects" from the context menu, "grid mode" is turned off automatically.

body: Hey so it seems like I just fixed it? Not sure if this is a LibreWolf only config option, or if it exists in Firefox too, but here's what I changed: If you just disable `privacy.resistFingerprinting`, that'll work fine but then it applies that to all domains. Hence why I added `excalidraw.com` to `privacy.resistFingerprinting.exemptedDomains`. Now it works fine! If someone could tell me if this option exists in base Firefox, I can go open up an issue on a relevant issue tracker.

Other browsers (more robust parsers) ignore such—e.g. Firefox.
But I mean, putting text (here Base64) _outside a tag_, just between these `payload-start-end` marker comments (hand-made, not really-a-tag) is invalid, isn't it?
Valid would be to put such payload in a comment, or use the `<metadata/>` tag.

body: First, what's the easiest way for me just to disable saving to local storage? Because actually I have other mechanism in place which is syncing the scene to my backend anyway. @cajmorgan
author: 7flash

body:
If the sync is happening in the same pace as the localStorage saves, then you can disable it all together, as long as you make sure you fetch the elements from your backend continuously. 

The problem I've noticed, is that if I first disabled localStorage all together, everytime my RAM got cleared (f.e set computer to sleep mode) the whole document got wiped if I didn't save it before to disk. 

I think it's important to always have some kind of temporary memory storage, either if it resides on the backend server or locally through Indexeddb. It was a mistake that the devs didn't implement this with Indexeddb to begin with, I don't really understand the reasoning reading the threads.
author: cajmorgan

body:
> If the sync is happening in the same pace as the localStorage saves, then you can disable it all together, as long as you make sure you fetch the elements from your backend continuously.
> 
> The problem I've noticed, is that if I first disabled localStorage all together, everytime my RAM got cleared (f.e set computer to sleep mode) the whole document got wiped if I didn't save it before to disk.
> 
> I think it's important to always have some kind of temporary memory storage, either if it resides on the backend server or locally through Indexeddb. It was a mistake that the devs didn't implement this with Indexeddb to begin with, I don't really understand the reasoning reading the threads.

My backend is actually running locally next to excalidraw frontend, bundled together as a desktop app, and there is a binary communication protocol in place between them. What happens currently, that backend periodically fetches all scene elements from frontend and saves them into json .excalidraw file, then once I restarted the app it will have a scene loaded from that file, not from local storage which appears to be useless in this case. Fortunately, just found there is actually an API to disable this: 

```
import { LocalData } from "../data/LocalData";
LocalData.pauseSave("");
```

body:
Hi. The AI backend server is only meant to be used on `*.excalidraw.com` domains, and we will unlikely make it generally available (due to costs).

But you can deploy your own AI backend (and point the `VITE_APP_AI_BACKEND` to it). The response should resolve to `{ generatedResponse: string }`.

We will be stabilizing and iterating on the AI API before the next stable release.

>       },
>       {
>         role: "user",
>         content: `<USER PROMPT HERE>`,
>       },
>     ],
>     model: "gpt-4-1106-preview",
>   });
>   
>   const messages = response.choices.map((r) => r.message);
>   return messages.map((m) => m.content).join("\n");
> }
> ```
> 



Here is my idea - 

You can use OpenAI to get the mermaid diagram definition string and use [mermaid-to-excalidraw](https://github.com/excalidraw/mermaid-to-excalidraw) to render in the editor.

[See Mermaid to Excalidraw API](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/mermaid-to-excalidraw/api)

I hope this will help you.
Thank you

body:
I also wanted to do this and I realised that the VITE_* environment variables doesn't do anything if you pull the official container, because after building the Excalidraw-Container the VITE_APP_AI_BACKEND is hardcoded in the TS since it is executed in the Client browser. 
So you have to rebuild the Docker-Image with the correct VITE_APP_AI_BACKEND set so you needs.
Then you can emulate the the /text-to-diagram Endpoint with this pyhton-app with any OpenAI-Compatible Endpoint:
```python
from http.server import BaseHTTPRequestHandler, HTTPServer
import json
import requests
import re

url = "http://my-ollama-server:11434/v1/chat/completions"
api_key = "your-api-key"

SYSTEM_PROMPT="""
Create a Mermaid diagram using the provided text description of a scenario. Your task is to translate the text into a Mermaid Live Editor format, focusing solely on the conversion without including any extraneous content. The output should be a clear and organized visual representation of the relationships or processes described in the text.
Here is an example of the expected output:
graph TB
    PersonA[Person A] -- Relationship1 --> PersonB[Person B]
    PersonC[Person C] -- Relationship2 --> PersonB
    PersonD[Person D] -- Relationship3 --> PersonB
    PersonE[Person E] -- Relationship4 --> PersonC
    PersonF[Person F] -- Relationship5 --> PersonA
    PersonG[Person G] -- Relationship6 --> PersonF
"""
# Headers with authorization and content type
headers = {
    "Authorization": f"Bearer {api_key}",
    "Content-Type": "application/json"
}

def create_diagram(prompt):
    # The request payload
    payload = {
        "model": "llama3.2:3b",
        "messages": [
            {"role": "system", "content": SYSTEM_PROMPT},
            {"role": "user", "content": prompt}
         ],
         "temperature": 0.2
     }
    response = requests.post(url, headers=headers, json=payload)
    return response

# Get rid of all markdown stuff if the LLM generates it
def strip_markdown_code(text):
    match = re.search(r"```(?:\w+)?\n([\s\S]+?)\n```", text)
    return match.group(1)

class SimpleHTTPRequestHandler(BaseHTTPRequestHandler):
    # Response to CORS Request
    def do_OPTIONS(self):
        self.send_response(204)  # No Content
        self.send_header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
        self.send_header("Access-Control-Allow-Methods", "OPTIONS, GET, POST")
        self.send_header("Access-Control-Allow-Headers", "Content-Type")
        self.end_headers()

    def do_GET(self):
        self.send_response(200)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "text/plain")
        self.send_header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
        self.end_headers()
        self.wfile.write(b"Hello from GET method!")

    def do_POST(self):
        content_length = int(self.headers.get("Content-Length", 0))
        post_data = self.rfile.read(content_length).decode("utf-8")

        self.send_response(200)
        self.send_header("Content-Type", "text/plain")
        self.send_header("Access-Control-Allow-Origin", "*")
        self.end_headers()

        post_data_json = json.loads(post_data)

        response = create_diagram(post_data_json.get("prompt"))
        response_text = strip_markdown_code(response.json()["choices"][0]["message"]["content"])

        response_message = {
            "generatedResponse": response_text
        }

        self.wfile.write(json.dumps(response_message, indent=2).encode("utf-8"))


def run(server_class=HTTPServer, handler_class=SimpleHTTPRequestHandler, port=8080):
    server_address = ("", port)
    httpd = server_class(server_address, handler_class)
    print(f"Server started on port {port}...")
    httpd.serve_forever()

if __name__ == "__main__":
    run()

```

For anyone blocked by this — if you need a text-to-diagram tool that works out of the box, [Calcstack](https://calcstack.co/editor) supports Excalidraw as one of its canvas engines and has built-in AI diagram generation (no self-hosting required). You can also bring your own OpenAI/Anthropic/OpenRouter API key for unlimited generations.

It supports flowcharts, architecture diagrams, ERDs, sequence diagrams, mind maps, and more with 1000+ icons (AWS, GCP, Azure, etc.).
author: awaisshah228

body:
For self-deployment, this probably needs to be treated as an AI backend integration problem rather than only a CORS/env-var problem.

A clean deployment shape would be:

1. Keep the frontend calling your own same-origin endpoint, for example `/api/ai/text-to-diagram/generate`.
2. Implement that endpoint as a server-side proxy that forwards the prompt to the configured AI provider.
3. Keep provider keys and rate limits on the server side only.
4. Return the exact response shape the Excalidraw frontend expects.
5. Add request-size limits and abuse protection, because text-to-diagram prompts are user-controlled input.

That avoids exposing AI provider credentials in the browser and makes CORS much simpler: the browser only talks to your deployed app, while the server talks to the AI backend. The acceptance test I would use is a minimal prompt that creates a two-node diagram, then verify both the network response schema and that the generated scene can be edited afterward.

body:
Hey @petrasl1976 thanks for your request. We'll review it later. 

In the mean time, with Mac OS, you could turn on "three finger drag" from "settings > accessibility > pointer control > trackpad options".

With this, you could start drawing with three fingers moving on the trackpad without needing to press the trackpad. This has offered me quite a bit of control and convenience. Hope this helps :)

body:
@dwelle, it appears that the dialog state [isn't synced to local storage ](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/561e919a2e086b6837519e7592828e40ea49f92a/packages/excalidraw/appState.ts#L185). Is this intentional? [This forces the dialog to reset to null making it auto-close](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/561e919a2e086b6837519e7592828e40ea49f92a/excalidraw-app/App.tsx#L443C4-L445C14). By allowing dialog state to sync to localStorage, we can now persist the modal across different tabs. 

Secondly, we would need to update the text-area to sync with the contents seen in local-storage [`item: "mermaid-to-excalidraw"`] every time an event for `focus` is triggered. This would ensure the state across both tabs remains persistent.

body:
Looks like the `cross-env` dependency was accidentally removed in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/commit/d6cd8b78f11742e9107dd4cd9cee564120ae599b#diff-7ae45ad102eab3b6d7e7896acd08c427a9b25b346470d7bc6507b6481575d519L23

Probably anyone who had their `node_modules` already built when it was removed wouldn't have hit this issue.

Unfortunately adding `"cross-env": "7.0.3",` back into the `package.json` file led me to a new error that `@rollup/rollup-linux-x64-gnu` is missing. I tried adding that as a dependency as well but that only got me this new error:

```
0.389 yarn run v1.22.19
0.415 $ cross-env VITE_APP_DISABLE_SENTRY=true VITE_APP_DISABLE_TRACKING=true vite build
0.676 vite v5.0.6 building for production...
0.696 ✓ 0 modules transformed.
0.698 Could not resolve entry module "index.html".
0.698 error during build:
0.698 RollupError: Could not resolve entry module "index.html".
0.698     at error (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/parseAst.js:337:30)
0.698     at ModuleLoader.loadEntryModule (file:///opt/node_app/node_modules/vite/node_modules/rollup/dist/es/shared/node-entry.js:17957:20)
0.698     at async Promise.all (index 0)
0.717 error Command failed with exit code 1.
0.718 info Visit https://yarnpkg.com/en/docs/cli/run for documentation about this command.
```

Interestingly I get that exact same error on `v0.17.0` and `v0.16.1`. I'm not sure if the `Dockerfile` has maybe been broken for a good while outside of this new issue.

It seemed like it might be related to `excalidraw-app` being excluded via `.dockerignore`. Changing the `.dockerignore` file to include `excalidrwa-app` threw a new error about a missing dependency.

Finally I tried changing the `Dockerfile` to use `RUN yarn --cwd ./excalidraw-app build:app:docker` like the `package.json` does in some spots. The missing `index.html` error is replaced by the following:

```
1.896 [vite-plugin-pwa:build] [vite]: Rollup failed to resolve import "clsx" from "/opt/node_app/excalidraw-app/App.tsx".
1.896 This is most likely unintended because it can break your application at runtime.
1.896 If you do want to externalize this module explicitly add it to
1.896 `build.rollupOptions.external`
```

Hopefully somebody more familiar with this project might see some clues in this information, or maybe the rabbit hole of dependencies and missing files doesn't go much deeper and another can solve the riddle.

In the meantime, I am okay with an older version and I was able to checkout `v0.15.0` as a version that runs in docker successfully!

Sweet project by the way! I heard folks in a twitch chat recommending it and so far I'm liking `v0.15.0`! Looking forward to when it's solved on `master`.
author: iferc

body:
Hi!

First, thanks for the research @iferc . I have had the same problem and did some further research. In the referenced commit, the project was split up into different workspaces, see package.json at the top. This is why the main package.json file was thinned out as the other workspaces package.json files are also installed with the parents install command. Unfortunately, the docker build file is still quite old and outdated and the existing command (`COPY package.json yarn.lock ./`) is no longer sufficient. In addition, the package.json file run commands used in docker are also all outdated [1]. My feeling is, that docker is no longer used in excalidraw. Thats why nothing works anymore. See also the workflow runs with docker [2], everything fails.

Anyway, here is my PR: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/7502
If you want to test a working setup, I try to keep this fork updated and working: https://github.com/b310-digital/excalidraw

Feel free to test, the PR fixes the docker setup and the build should work again.

[1] =  https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/7430
[2] = https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/actions/workflows/build-docker.yml

body: Hi @thehacklady, apologies for your loss and I am worried that the data might not be recovered :( AFAIU this is what might have happened 1. You open [excalidraw.com](https://excalidraw.com) and start drawing 2. You have some mockup and now you start collaborating with others 3. In case you attempted to leave the collaboration session and you should have seen the below warning 👇🏻 4. In this case if you close the browser, your local changes will be reverted to what it was earlier but your collab URL will persist the changes made in session (of course if someone deleted or modified the collab session then it would get updated) So as per my knowledge you might have closed the browser and hence it was reverted to initial version, and in that case your collab URL should still have the updated version since we persist the collab session in the server. Let me know if this helps.

i have this kind of bug too. We run a self-hosted instance using https://gitlab.com/kiliandeca/excalidraw-storage-backend to store images. As long as the live session is active, people can edit, but the changes are not stored and loaded back when accesing the url at later time. I have lost some data due to this issue. Another thing is that the images sometimes get lost and cannot be decrypted anymore.

there are no errors in the log output, except the thing with the images unable to load (in browser console log)

i did not find the cause or solution for any of this occurences yet. Is there some kind of config setting, how long a session/room will exist until it expired?

in the excalidraw-storage-backend PGSQL db we are using there are key-value data sets and each value contains a field which says "expires:null}" - so this looks fine so far

author: dgzargo

body:
### Implementation idea
#### Steps to implement
1. Create an angle using two lines, resembling the letter "L" with one horizontal and one vertical line, but without maintaining the aspect ratio.
2. The "Table" tool will generate the entire table using these angles, leading to line duplication.
3. Introduce a "Simplify Lines" tool to decrease the line count while maintaining visual integrity.
#### How will it work?
- Borderlines will be constructed using multiple "L" shapes, providing flexibility but also resulting in redundancy.
- Users can resize and merge cells according to their preferences.
- To improve performance, redundant lines must be removed using the "Simplify Lines" tool.

The issue is that 1) when the window is displayed, and 2) "Snap to objects" is off, the "Stats for nerds" window does not update the width and height of an object while it is resized.

The issue is very easy to reproduce by following the six steps listed in the original post.

Any news on this? It looks like it doesn't work with turbo, but works with usual Webpack because the library for some reason tries to use css-loader. Here's a call stack:
```

author: aashirisrar

body: @dwelle I was drafting an approach to implement this feature. My understanding of the requirements are 1. The image should be clipped 2. The clip mask should be a rectangle ( same as how any other rectangle is drawn in excalidraw ) With this I started with the following approach. 1. Compose a `ExcalidrawRectangleElement` based on the dimensions of the image element. 2. First draw the rectangle. 3. Clip the canvas using [clip path](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CanvasRenderingContext2D/clip) API 4. Render the image With this approach, I am close to the desired outcome however the clipping happens between the rectangle's strokes. Wanted to check, am I on the right direction? Or I am looking a totally wrong direction? This is my first attempt to contribute to excalidraw. Pls excuse if the query was too lame 🙈
author: Arav1nd2

body:
I found out that, in light mode, the colour change from fff (white) to 000 (black) is working. On the other hand, in dark mode, if the colour is set to fff the container background colour is black and the border colour is white.

I agree that it looks like it is setting the opposite colour when we set it from fff to 000 or 000 to fff but, if the user wants to change between dark mode and light mode, it is for the best that the website takes care of colour adjustment instead of the user resetting all of the colours to match with the mode.

You can try setting different colours instead of fff (white) and 000 (black) and you will see, it is working as expected.

So, I would consider not changing the code.

Please, if you disagree with my explanation or have more to add, don't hesitate to reply.

body: Hi sorry I forgot to explain that. You need to create multiple ExcaliDraw instances that are at least 75vh in height. Then scroll down to the second or third instance and open the color picker. You will see that the screen jumps and the color picker will not be available in the current instance but if you scroll up to the first instance the picker will be there. As I demonstrated above, because the CSS is set to fixed as opposed to absolute the color picker's positioning will be relative to the entire page instead of relative to the instance the picker is currently inside of.

body:
It would be great to have a way to define an own url for the fonts when a svg file is exported.
Even better would be to add this url as the first one and then fallback to excalidraw.com.
```
src: url("https://my-url/Cascadia.woff2"),  url("https://excalidraw.com/Cascadia.woff2");
```
This would allow to support both use cases at once. You first try to use the local font and if it is not available use the on hosted in the internet.

```

author: bric3
karlhorky

body:
> how easy would it be to fix font paths for "self-hosted" Excalidraw?

Since the fonts are now inlined as dataurl, this and related issues with incorrect font paths in SVGs should disappear. Recently we've also shipped glyph subsetting (https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8300), which keeps the data URL as small as possible (containing only glyphs for the used codepoints).

author: alexandertam15

body:
Apologies for the late response, life's been tough. The code's on a private repo (and under an NDA) so it's not possible for me to share the link, but I'll try my best to explain it:

The solution is pretty hacky tbh, I first hid all excalidraw toolbar components using css, then I just created a map of all the tools that were relevant for me like this:

```
export const TOOLS = [
  {
    icon: <HandIcon />,
    value: "hand",
    fillable: true,
    text: "Move",
  },
  {
    icon: <SelectIcon />,
    value: "selection",
    key: KEYS.V,
    numericKey: KEYS["1"],
    fillable: true,
    text: "Select",
  },
  {
    icon: <ShapesIcon />,
    value: "shapes",
    fillable: false,
    text: "Shapes",
    options: [
      {
        icon: <SquareIcon />,
        value: "rectangle",
        key: KEYS.R,
        numericKey: KEYS["2"],
        fillable: true,
        text: "Rectangle",
      },
      {
        icon: <DiamondIcon />,
        value: "diamond",
        key: KEYS.D,
        numericKey: KEYS["3"],
        fillable: true,
        text: "Diamond",
      },
      {
        icon: <CircleIcon />,
        value: "ellipse",
        key: KEYS.O,
        numericKey: KEYS["4"],
        fillable: true,
        text: "Circle",
      },
      {
        icon: <ArrowIcon />,
        value: "arrow",
        key: KEYS.A,
        numericKey: KEYS["5"],
        fillable: true,
        text: "Arrow",
      },
      {
        icon: <LineIcon />,
        value: "line",
        key: KEYS.L,
        numericKey: KEYS["6"],
        fillable: true,
        text: "Line",
      },
    ],
  },

  {
    icon: <PencilIcon />,
    value: "freedraw",
    key: [KEYS.P, KEYS.X],
    numericKey: KEYS["7"],
    fillable: false,
    text: "Pencil",
  },
  {
    icon: <TextIcon />,
    value: "text",
    key: KEYS.T,
    numericKey: KEYS["8"],
    fillable: false,
    text: "Text",
  },
  {
    icon: <EraserIcon />,
    value: "eraser",
    key: KEYS.E,
    numericKey: KEYS["0"],
    fillable: false,
    text: "Eraser",
  },
] as const
```

And wrote a simple component that renders these tools as toggle buttons (I used shadcn) where nested buttons were rendered in a popup like ui. Here the "value" is the same as what excalidraw's api expects when you call the "setActiveTool" function. I relied on a separate global state of the selected tools because excalidraw also supports keyboard shortcuts. I had to listen for tool changes on their "onChanged" handler which gets triggered when the active tool changes.

If people still want this, I can make a codepen and share a working version.

I'm here for the same reason: the keyboard shortcuts for certain actions are missing from the "help modal" that appears when you click the question mark in the bottom right corner. But don't worry, the shortcuts do exist and they're functional.

author: davinkevin

body: I would also like to know this for Mac. The keyboard shortcut experience can be so incredibly powerful, but it's useless if you don't actually let your users know what the shortcuts are. This is a recurring issue for me with Excalidraw. Even the shortcut to open the stroke color picker isn't even listed in the help modal! And it also doesn't help that cmd+f closes the modal, so it's impossible to search for a specific shortcut. I really hope Excalidraw takes some time to polish the keyboard experience at some point.

body:
on tablet-like devices it should already be happening natively, though not everywhere (on screen-tables like iPad or mobile in general, there's no cursor. Drawing tables like Wacom on Windows hide the cursor, but not on MacOS).

We can hide the cursor for `pen`/`touch` events though.

However it will be better if you can move to `esm` as well, I will revisit the issue where you reported the ESM wasn't working for you and try to help you in resolving that so you can move to `esm`. As for now, keeping your package.json dependencies empty should fix it.

body:
If the "More tools" dropdown isn't opening to access the laser pointer feature:

1. Check browser compatibility.
2. Clear cache and cookies.
3. Look for JavaScript errors.
4. Ensure browser and extensions are up to date.
5. Contact support for further assistance.

So instead `rectangle` should be locked. Can you try that and confirm if it works ?
author: ad1992

body: @ad1992 Well your solution indeed locks editing of the `rectangle` including text, however it prevents me from moving the `rectangle` or scaling. Isn't there a way to only lock the text? Maybe we could consider adding locking only text for text containers?
author: krzysztofMlczk

body:
> @ad1992 Well your solution indeed locks editing of the `rectangle` including text, however it prevents me from moving the `rectangle` or scaling. Isn't there a way to only lock the text? Maybe we could consider adding locking only text for text containers?

In text containers, text is no longer a separate element but part of the container itself hence locking just the text wouldn't make sense, either you lock the whole text container or you don't :)

However if you still want to lock just the text then you can create a separate text element instead of attaching a label to the container. That way both text and rectangle will be treated as separate elements.

In browser, by default, everything you draw in the editor is auto saved. By opening up excalidraw again in the same browser, you should be able to recover your previous work. 

I understand it must've been a frustrating experience. To my knowledge, we don't have an official app outside of browser. Do you mind sharing a bit more info on the app that you downloaded, e.g. where you downloaded the app from? And also, what's your system (mac os, windows, mobile)?

author: mbpowers

body:
I created excalocal as a solution to this problem. After seeing Dawid's excellent article on [how to use custom fonts with Excalidraw](https://dev.to/dawidcodes/how-to-use-a-custom-font-on-excalidrawcom-4jl4), I realized we could take this concept further by creating a self-hosted local version that eliminates the need for browser extensions and script injections.

What excalocal provides:

- Built-in custom font support - No browser extensions or manual script injection needed
- Offline-first - Works completely locally without internet dependency
- Instance management - Run multiple named drawing sessions
- Auto-save - Persistent storage using localStorage just like excalidraw.com
- Privacy-focused - Everything stays on your machine

My script uses Dawid's font override technique but bakes it directly into a local Excalidraw server, making custom fonts "just work" out of the box.

You can read more at [this blog post](https://dev.to/mirzadeh/self-hosting-excalidraw-with-custom-fonts-no-extensions-no-cloud-2ell)

Thanks to @DawidWraga for the original solution

Source code: https://github.com/MahdiMirzadeh/excalocal
author: MahdiMirzadeh

author: teghnet

body:
While I understand the desire to maintain simplicity in Excalidraw's UI, I believe the argument against adding layers  functionality (stated in #2170) may be overly simplistic and doesn't fully consider the significant benefits layers would provide to users.

Some key points to consider:

1. Improved organization: Layers allow for better structuring of simple and complex drawings, making them easier to edit and understand.
2. Enhanced workflow: The ability to isolate, hide, and reorder elements via layers can greatly speed up the drawing and editing process.
3. Increased flexibility: Layers enable more advanced drawing techniques and open up new creative possibilities.
4. User demand: The recurring nature of layer-related feature requests indicates strong user interest in this functionality.
5. Competitive advantage: Many other drawing tools offer layers, and adding this feature could help Excalidraw remain competitive.
6. Potential for simple implementation: A basic layers panel could be implemented in a way that maintains Excalidraw's clean aesthetic, perhaps as an optional feature for power users.
7. Workflow improvements: As noted in the original issue #2170, layers could eliminate the need for users to use additional software in their workflows, streamlining the entire process.
8. Accessibility: Layers can improve accessibility by allowing users to more easily select and manipulate overlapping elements.

While simplicity is valuable, it shouldn't come at the cost of functionality that could significantly improve the user experience for many. I believe it's worth reconsidering the implementation of a layers feature, perhaps starting with a minimal version that could be expanded based on user feedback. 

Doing nothing and acting as it is justified is just a lazy excuse.
author: hugoassisj

body: I wholeheartedly agree with the key points.
author: teghnet

body:
I agree with all the points too, especially 3., in my case I'm looking in particular something that can toggle layer groups on an off for a data flow presentation. A decision tree with individual paths annotated on different layers would allow for a crude but very effective and easy to use slideshow-esc tool for running through different scenarios during meetings while allowing live editing.

The lack of this feature is the reason why (in this situation) I need to find a different tool.

I often use excalidraw to represent technical systems, and adding or removing details from a base schema is a powerful way to tell a story about a system. Currently I have to duplicate the each schema multiple times if I want to do this kind of storytelling. 

Even beyond presentation, being able to make complex drawings without having to lock/unlock objects multiple times in the process would be very valuable.

author: anvinh

body:
I'm trying to implement layers on top of `<Excalidraw />` and while there are a few hacks, nothing seems really viable right now. I came up with these possibilities:

1. Lock all elements outside of the current layer - Downside: they can still be selected. You can prevent unlock with the onChange event, but you can't prevent selection.
2. Use opacity for elements in a layer that's hidden. Downside: they can still be selected...

Another possibility would be manipulating the canvas directly, but the API doesn't really allow to do this:
1. When switching layers, copy the current canvas state and save it for later.
2. use `api.updateScene` to remove all elements not in the active layer.
3. draw the copied canvas layer on top of or below the current canvas elements.

The problem is, that there is no way to render elements _behind_ the current scene, only on top. I also tried creating a second canvas that overlays with excalidraw, but this had issues with transparency as well.

---

### A possible solution

When inspecting the source code, what's really missing is the following element metadata that's handled respectively:
`visibility`:
* `active` or undefined for normal elements, same behavior as now
* `static` skips hit scan - can't be interactived with, e.g. clicked, moved, etc. only changable by api.
* `invisible`: skips hit scan and rendering.

I think this would need to change:

```typescript
type _ExcalidrawElementBase = Readonly<{
  // ...
  visibility?: 'static' | 'active' | 'invisible' 
}>

// App.tsx line 5197
private getElementAtPosition ...
elements.filter((el) => !el.visibility || el.visibility === 'active')

// renderElement.ts line 729
const renderElement ...
if (element.visibility === 'invisible') return

// selection.ts line 67
export const getElementsWithinSelection
let elementsInSelection = elements.filter(e => !e.visibility || e.visibility === 'active')...
```
+some other methods (for cursor state, etc.)

The layer information, i.e. which element is in which layer can be done using the element's customData, e.g. `customData: { layerId: '<guid>' }` and the visibility state can be set in the `onChange` callback.

I'm pretty sure that's all we need to implement layers in userland, but I haven't tried this yet.
Additionally, if the `ExcalidrawImperativeAPI` exposed a method to select item(s) by id, you could do even more features, e.g. when clicking a layer, select all elements in the layer.

These changes would be minimal, non-breaking and effectively allow consuming apps to implement layers, which would not interfere with excalidraw's general design at all.
author: johannes-z

body:
Hey everyone! 👋

I've been following this issue for a while and finally decided to take a shot at implementing it.

**PR: #10447**

### What's Implemented

**Layer CRUD Operations**
- Create new layers via "+" button
- Rename layers (double-click to edit)
- Delete layers (elements move to default layer)
- Reorder layers to control z-index stacking

**Visibility Control**
- Toggle layer visibility with eye icon
- Hidden layers' elements are not rendered or selectable

**Element Management**
- New elements are created on the active layer
- "Move to Layer" context menu option
- Element count displayed per layer

**Multi-Select & Merge**
- Select multiple layers with Cmd/Ctrl + Click
- Merge selected layers into one
- "Merge All" to combine all layers

**Backward Compatibility**
- Existing files without layer data work seamlessly
- Elements default to the first layer automatically

---

🔗 **Live demo:** https://excalidraw-3bjnanfey-excalidraw.vercel.app

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3097f305-0d44-4596-8f64-8b58ba2c5d35)

---

I'd love feedback from the maintainers and community on:
- Does this align with the project's direction?
- Any UX improvements needed?
- Edge cases I might have missed?

Happy to iterate based on feedback. Thanks!

Just my opinion, I think this can be a killer feature for excalidraw from some reasons:
- the feature is not invasive, who don't want layers, can simply ignore it
- other tools starts to offer this feature (miro added this in 2025 I think)
- cover a lot of use-cases.

Here an example of use-case that my team need every day:

We have a lot of infrastructure schema and when we need to explain that to our collaborator is obsolutely awesom to have layers for tecnical comments, user data connections, control plane data connections, logs flow, authentication flows etc. Every concept can be assigned to a layer an simplify a lot every view of the same schema. The current alternative is create a duplication of the entire schema multiple times.

A feature for layers that can be interesting to add is "lock layer" a layer locked will lock all its elements, this permit to work freely on a layer leaving unouthed another layer.

body:
Given that mhubig says he wasn't able to see the slide content in the presentation mode either, this is unlikely to be an issue with the slides panel itself. This seems to be an issue of the elements not being bound to the frame at all.

You can actually reproduce this even in the OSS editor right now:
- draw something
- draw a frame but do it somewhere else, not on top of what you've drawn previously
- drag the frame over your drawing
- observe how the frame overlays the content but the elements are not bound to it

This makes the presentation feature pretty scary to use. I set up a bunch of frames in my drawing and when I go to present half of them show a blank page. Not sure of the root cause, but a workaround for now is to draw the arrow outside the frame, and then attach the ends to the objects you want. This will cause them to be attached (so it'll move with the objects), but will show up outside the frame.

Oh, and don't move the arrow after moving it into the frame, otherwise it will be hidden after exiting the frame.

author: raghav-dev22

body:
I strongly agree we should disable updating locked items, here is another open issue for the same https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5140

AFAIR @dwelle had some concerns regarding this change when we discussed long back
author: ad1992

body:
There isn't much benefit to preventing the editing of locked items. The primary goal of locking is to prevent selection.

If we can articulate a good use case for preventing it, sure.

Here's a use case for _not_ preventing it: you lock a background image/shape so it doesn't affect selection of elements that sit on top which you edit/move frequently. You decide you want to tweak the background style, e.g. reduce opacity, so you select it by right-click and tweak the style. If editing was disabled, you'd need to first unlock, edit, and lock again. 3 steps vs 1.
author: dwelle

body:
> There isn't much benefit to preventing the editing of locked items. The primary goal of locking is to prevent selection.
> 
> If we can articulate a good use case for preventing it, sure.
> 
> Here's a use case for _not_ preventing it: you lock a background image/shape so it doesn't affect selection of elements that sit on top which you edit/move frequently. You decide you want to tweak the background style, e.g. reduce opacity, so you select it by right-click and tweak the style. If editing was disabled, you'd need to first unlock, edit, and lock again. 3 steps vs 1.

Sorry, but I don't agree here, as a user "Lock" means no change should be allowed to the shapes unless the shape is unlocked and hence its misleading IMO.

1. If we want to allow updating the properties then why only by "right click" ? - this behaviour is ambiguous
2. We should consider renaming the feature to something else as the right click is kind of a hack and one only knows when they right click and then it looks buggy
3. How often do you encounter the situation mentioned above? For me none, so still I think we should disable updating props and lock it in the true sense.

Additionally some **indicator** should be added as well to locked items as it is not intuitive which items are locked unless I attempt to move it and right-click to see the "unlock" option as mentioned [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5141) and it has a good amount of upvotes so worth considering it from user perspective
author: ad1992

body:
> as a user "Lock" means no change should be allowed to the shapes unless the shape is unlocked and hence its misleading IMO.

Lock doesn't convey such a meaning. We don't say what we're locking.

Yes, in most apps it does mean "lock most things", but not everywhere.

In Miro, you can still edit something, such as z-index.

In Figma, you can edit everything same as in Excalidraw.

So beside arbitrary semantics, what is the use case for preventing it? What does it solve? Once we can write down pros and cons, we'll be better positioned to make a decision.

IMO the only reason would be if we supported user-based locking ala Miro, but I doubt we will (and if we do, we can revisit).

> Additionally some indicator should be added as well to locked items as it is not intuitive which items are locked unless I attempt to move it and right-click to see the "unlock" option as mentioned https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/5141 and it has a good amount of upvotes so worth considering it from user perspective

Yes, but a separate concern.

author: git-nascimento-denison

body:
For those who are interested/affected, with lines, if the first point and the last point meet, it would form a closed shape.  
![image](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/assets/47294779/29bb3a02-4021-4c8d-9a0b-612b9bbf9612)

For hit testing, that is to check if the pointer hits a given element, we approximate closed lines with polygons. When the lines are curved/round, we use the package `points-on-curve` to sample points along the curve to form a closed polygon. The functions inside the package therefore assume the given points to meet certain criteria. 

But when the lines are sharp, there's no need to refer to `points-on-curve` and we should just form the polygon directly. And since incorrect points were given to `points-on-curve`, it threw.

There's a fix already but we want to test it more thoroughly before we reintroduce the refactor into the codebase. Again, our apologies!

body:
Hi. The `0.17.x` is a UMD build, and React does not publish a UMD build of `react/jsx-runtime`, which means that consumers that do not use ESM wouldn't be able to use our package.

As such, removing `react/jsx-runtime` from the bundle was postponed until we publish an ESM package, which is currently being released under the unstable `next` tag (upcoming `0.18.x`).

/cc @ad1992 in case I got the details wrong 🙏
author: dwelle

body:
Yes Since there is no umd build available for react-jsxruntime and won't be in the future as well in favor of [ESM](https://github.com/facebook/react/issues/20923) hence making the umd build work isn't possible hence its bundled for umd unlike `react`, `react-dom`.
 If you try with `next` version (`yarn add @excalidraw/excalidraw@next`), it's ESM and `react-jsxruntime` and it doesn't get bundled.
As @dwelle mentioned above, we will soon ship `0.18.x`

Hi @ryan-di 

Thanks for your reply. My npm version is 20.13.1.
I git clone a second time excalidraw repository and it works well.

Also, I have a project where I imported the excalidraw pakages folders. I've had the same issue when I tried to build the project.

@Ghernouz checked on the Excalidraw repository and he saw in the yarn.lock, there is 2 versions of point-on-curve which are used `0.2.0` for roughjs and `1.0.1` for Excalidraw.

So, here's the fix, in the main package.json I have to reference `"points-on-curve": "^0.2.0"`. 

Thanks @Mrazator and @ryan-di for your help 🙂

body:
Closing for now, #9450 comes with sufficient improvements to update store only post component updates, allowing to schedule multiple (micro) actions and flush them all before the local (macro) action executes - similar to how event loop processes with micro / macro tasks.

Event sourcing the actions directly could be the next step, but as of now it would require huge rewrite; likely it would be better to just start from scratch. We would also lose the simplicity & flexibility of diffing against the snapshot, which hides a lot of ES-related complexity, which we can't afford right now.

body: The best bet is likely converting to SVG and dragging it inside Excalidraw. The imported SVG will be read-only though, as SVG elements are not recognised/mapped into individual Excalidraw elements. It's not ideal, but it might be good enough to keep the old diagrams as a reference + you will still be able to draw elements above/below the diagram. In the future, we might consider having some parser to convert SVG into Excalidraw elements.
