At the moment you can do two things that may help:

- if you select the objects you want to export only, then the export dialog will only export the selection by default (can be toggled)
- you can also create a frame. Selecting the frame before exporting will crop the output exactly to the frame's dimensions.
author: dwelle

body: @dwelle I think your two suggestions perfectly solve the original problem. However, the fact that I had to post this issue here to find out about them is the **_hidden_** problem here. I have renamed the issue title to reveal this hidden problem. I suggest that the "Only selected" checkbox is **_always_** shown but disabled if it's not applicable for the current state. It should **_always_** have a hint text to guide the user on how to enable and use it. Here's a mockup: --- > [!TIP] > Generally I'm noticing that options that are irrelevant to current state are being _hidden entirely_ instead of being disabled but still shown. In UX terms, this decreases visibility and exposure, as the user will not be aware that such options exist in the first place. Thus the user remains uneducated on them, leading to them opening issues such as this, or simply feeling that Excalidraw is inferior and lacking desired features when in reality, the user is not aware how to enable and use the features correctly.

body:
We'll likely support this, but not through a modifier because all of them are pretty much taken. The `Ctrl` is for deep select which in itself is useful (and likely will be more so soon).

> alternatively when a selection starting point is on the right of a selection ending point (i.e. a selection from right to left would have it activated by default, while a selection from left to right not - CTRL could be still used to alternate the mode in both cases).

Was suggested, but it's too implicit/confusing behavior.

So we'd need enable it through a toggleable setting (selection mode).

---

We're also working on a lasso selection that will most likely use partial selection as well. That one will work via Alt+drag.
author: dwelle

body:
I agree with this. The  current behaviour is weird for a whiteboard app. When I draw a square or a circle, I want them to stay a square or a circle, and not turn into a rectangle or an ellipse because I entered text inside. 

My proposal would be to make this "keep shape > change text size" approach the default behaviour. 
The user would still have the ability to use the text size buttons to _force a text size_ and then either:
* Have the excess text hidden inside the shape. This is how Miro does it, expecting the user to resize the shape to their will if they want to show all the text.
* Scale the shape or selected shapess to fit all the text inside them. Note that I say "scale", meaning that the proportions are respected, instead of distorting or elongating the shape. This would be a worse option in my opinion.

An "auto text size" button could be added to the text settings in order to turn a fixed size text into a flexible one.

This would also make any rectangle effectively work as a sticky note, and thus mostly solve #1428 without the need of a new tool.

body: I'm able to reproduce this issue in Safari on iPad and Mac. > [!NOTE] > **TL;DR** Safari doesn't support the filters that are used for the dark mode export. The **Export Image** menu has a separate **Dark mode** toggle that is separate to the current theme used by the rest of the app. Here's a screenshot from **Firefox** on Mac: This, however, **doesn't show up in Safari on Mac or iPad**, because we're checking for browser support of the `CanvasRenderingContext2D.filter` method: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/a18b139a60a50f1db73444140443254961655a55/packages/excalidraw/components/ImageExportDialog.tsx#L233-L239 As we can see from [the MDN docs]( https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/API/CanvasRenderingContext2D/filter), it's not supported, although in the compatibility table it links the corresponding **WebKit bug** that was apparently fixed a year ago: https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=198416 so maybe it'll be working in the near future. ### Enabling canvas filters In the mean time, it is actually possible to **enable canvas filters** via an experimental **feature flag** in Safari: Settings > Apps > Safari > Advanced > Feature Flags > **Canvas Filters** ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/a8e52712-ec0d-4987-894d-7c235a406ff8) Once enabled: * the MDN filter examples do work ✅ * and the Dark mode toggle appears in Excalidraw ✅ * but it unfortunately toggling it does nothing ❌ https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/a18b139a60a50f1db73444140443254961655a55/packages/element/src/renderElement.ts#L80-L81 👆Also, the image invert filter from the code seems to work fine with the MDN hippo example, so perhaps something else could be breaking compatibility?
author: dangerman

body: ### Workaround Update: When **canvas filters** are enabled: * The preview and the PNG export are still in light mode. * But the SVG export actually works ✅ And you can convert this to PNG in **Files**: ### Canvas filter WebKit compatibility At runtime, when the `filter` for dark mode is updated, it doesn't throw any errors and the value is just `'none'`. https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/4dfb8a3f8eaf7722f5e133e270927e297d7aaa0e/packages/excalidraw/renderer/helpers.ts#L74-L75 And this seems to be related to a WebKit bug where the filters don't work with **detached canvases** that haven't been appended to the DOM. This is the case for the exports, and for the preview the filter is set **before** it's appended. * https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=275436 * https://bugs.webkit.org/show_bug.cgi?id=277638

body:
It seems that tooling is not there yet to support our raw ES modules out-of-the-box, without using a bundler. CDNs like `esm.run` and `esm.sh` with their auto-bundling and CommonJS -> ESM interoperability seem to be almost there, but neither of them quite work. Also, the investigation is time-consuming, as individual issues (mostly with deeply nested dependencies) need to be resolved one-by-one.

Another option is in theory relying on [import maps](https://web.dev/blog/import-maps-in-all-modern-browsers), but specifying the whole dependency tree seems tedious, and generating the import maps with tools like [jspm cli](https://jspm.org/docs/jspm-cli/stable/) currently also fails on broken nested dependencies.

Perhaps @tomayac might have some ideas? :)

Otherwise, we will likely need to go with a separate `@excalidraw/browser` bundle for a simple script import.
author: Mrazator

body:
> Perhaps @tomayac might have some ideas? :)

Sorry, but this is not one of my areas of expertise…
author: tomayac

body: Alright, thanks! I though it might be, since your post, which I linked above, was one of the first result for "import maps" in my google search.
author: Mrazator

body:
>  I though it might be, since your post, which I linked above, was one of the first result for "import maps" in my google search.

I know about this…

> and generating the import maps with tools like [jspm cli](https://jspm.org/docs/jspm-cli/stable/) currently also fails on broken nested dependencies.

…but I don't know about that `:-)`.
author: tomayac

body:
Alright, got it working with `esm.sh` CDN, marking `react` & `react-dom` as external and including import maps for both. Otherwise react & react-dom get included multiple times through (nested) dependencies: 

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/9190c729-ecd5-4ead-b0f5-4431e63b3272)

Hence we won't need another separate browser bundle.

Examples will be updated as part of the [#8771](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8771).

Playground: https://jsfiddle.net/yL13h6kv/6/

author: mtolmacs

body:
Hello ^^

I investigated a little and found that :

When you press "Escape" instead of click outside the shape, it has the behavior you're looking for.

--
I tried to fix the bug but I only managed to fix it partially so I'll put here my investigation results if someone else wants to take the issue (as I have no more time for this issue + I'm a newbie to this voluminous codebase ^^"):

In the code, when pressing escape, the `onKeyDown` event listener of the editable textarea is called first here :

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/21ffaf4d76bf8554c3ba5561ab8ff41ce89b55fa/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L391-L394

which calls `handleSubmit`.

After calling the event listener on the editable textarea, the event listener `this.onKeyDown` bound on `document` here :

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/30983d801a12b658d4f6b3ee4bd7471186291c78/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L2585-L2589

calls the action manager here:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/30983d801a12b658d4f6b3ee4bd7471186291c78/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L4298

In the [handleKeyDown](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/30983d801a12b658d4f6b3ee4bd7471186291c78/packages/excalidraw/actions/manager.tsx#L85), we get the `actionFinalize` action thanks to `action.keyTest` which matches the event key `Escape`.

This action is then executed by the manager's updater function :

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/30983d801a12b658d4f6b3ee4bd7471186291c78/packages/excalidraw/actions/manager.tsx#L124

Notice we get the `actionFinalize` action from `data[0]` so I guess we are always sure to always get one action which is  `actionFinalise` as this piece of code is called only by the event listener on `document`. I'll base myself on this assertion to do my test below.

As the action should be captured immediately: 

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/30983d801a12b658d4f6b3ee4bd7471186291c78/packages/excalidraw/actions/actionFinalize.tsx#L205

the action manager's updater function [pointing](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/99d8bff1751b0bcfaacd1b0f32689df63ab42f86/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L704-L705) to `syncActionResult` is then called (https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/21ffaf4d76bf8554c3ba5561ab8ff41ce89b55fa/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx#L2139) which I guess triggers all the history logic (among other things)

--

On the other hand, when clicking outside the shape instead of pressing Escape key, the workflow is different.

It is the [`onPointerDown`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/21ffaf4d76bf8554c3ba5561ab8ff41ce89b55fa/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L637) bound [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/21ffaf4d76bf8554c3ba5561ab8ff41ce89b55fa/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L726) which is called.

And this event listener also calls `handleSubmit`

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/21ffaf4d76bf8554c3ba5561ab8ff41ce89b55fa/packages/excalidraw/element/textWysiwyg.tsx#L677-L679

And that's it. All the logic to call the actionFinalize doesn't exist hence the behavior you mentioned.

There is this a function `executeAction` in the action manager that does the same as what is done in the editable event listener `handleKeyDown` so I tried to call it after the `handleSubmit` in the `pointDown` event listener like so :

```ts
      requestAnimationFrame(() => {
        handleSubmit();
        app.actionManager.executeAction(actionFinalize); // <= added this line here
      });
```

This fix seems to fix the problem you mentioned but adds some bugs when doing some undos and some shapes.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7bdf438d-2ae9-4a52-8350-50d6294a071e

Hope it's helps !

body:
> but there's no way to make the text large enough to view at this zoom level.

There are two ways to scale the text A) resize text element, B) set custom size (in px) in the properties panel

> 
> Just reference a image.
> 
> ![https://www.google.com/url?sa=i&url=https%3A%2F%2Fforum.effectivealtruism.org%2Fs%2Fdr9sjmfd6vB2ztXMt%2Fp%2FiKes4JjvpLM8LKMyT&psig=AOvVaw0ZoTAP9bwN9dAPgz03TwTO&ust=1743151631346000&source=images&cd=vfe&opi=89978449&ved=0CBQQjRxqFwoTCOCS0YHwqYwDFQAAAAAdAAAAABA5](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/534cc043-6cc0-4197-a182-084ccf5261e4)

I think for something like this, a three step conversion can be simpler :- 
1. Use a LLM vision model to convert image -> mermaid diagram output (non-determinstic step but LLMs are good at generating mermaid code)
2. Mermaid code validator and re-run through LLM until correct output.
3. Use the excalidraw mermaid->excalidraw package to get the diagram (deterministic step and flowcharts are supported).

author: AyushRajgor

body: ### Image → Diagram via AI (AWS Bedrock, Gemini, OpenAI, Ollama) for #9244 The PR for the feature is here: https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/pull/10388 This PR implements an “Image to diagram” feature for Excalidraw, addressing [#9244](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/9244?utm_source=chatgpt.com) It allows users to paste or import an image (e.g., a screenshot of a UI, a flow, or a diagram) and use an AI model to convert it into editable Excalidraw elements. The feature is fully client-side configurable and supports multiple AI providers: - AWS Bedrock - Google Gemini - OpenAI - Ollama (local models) Users can configure their own API keys under Settings → Configure AI, and then use the tool via More tools → Image to diagram. ### High-Level Overview What this PR adds: New AI configuration UI Adds a “Configure AI” section under the Settings dialog. Allows the user to: - Choose an AI provider (AWS Bedrock, Gemini, OpenAI, Ollama). - Enter the provider’s API key (and, where applicable, optional model / endpoint fields). - Enable / disable the “Image to diagram” feature. - New “Image to diagram” tool - Adds a menu item under More tools → Image to diagram. When triggered: - Takes the currently selected image element. - Sends the image content (e.g., as a data URL / base64-encoded image) to the configured AI provider. - Receives a structured response describing shapes / connectors. - Converts that response into Excalidraw elements and inserts them into the scene. Multi-provider abstraction Introduces a small abstraction layer so each provider implements a common interface for: - Accepting an image input. - Returning a normalized set of “diagram elements” (shapes, text, lines, arrows, positions). - This keeps the UI and core logic provider-agnostic and makes future providers easy to add. Client-side key storage with encryption: User-supplied API keys are: - Never sent to any Excalidraw backend. - Encrypted and stored only in the browser’s session/application storage. - On page load, the app: - Decrypts the stored keys (if present) in memory. - Rehydrates the AI configuration so the user does not need to re-enter keys for that session. UX & Workflow Details 1. Configuring AI (Settings → Configure AI) Path: Settings → Configure AI UI / Behavior: Provider selection Dropdown to select provider: - AWS Bedrock - Google Gemini - OpenAI - Ollama (local) The user can select Model to be used eg: aws claude 4.5 or gemini3 etc. Region in Bedrock can be configured. On “Save”, the config object is: Serialized. Encrypted. Stored in session/application storage. On “Cancel”, no changes are persisted. 2. Using the “Image to diagram” Tool (More tools → Image to diagram) Path: More tools → Image to diagram Flow: User selects an image element on the canvas. User opens More tools → Image to diagram. The tool: Reads the current AI configuration. If no valid configuration exists: Shows a message guiding the user to Settings → Configure AI. If configuration exists: Extracts the selected image content. Sends it to the configured provider with the necessary request payload. The provider: Analyzes the image (UI, flow chart, diagram, etc.). Returns a structured representation (e.g., rectangles, text nodes, connectors). Excalidraw: Maps this representation into Excalidraw elements (rectangles, text, arrows, etc.). Inserts them into the scene near the original image (typically overlapping or slightly offset). The user can then: Edit the generated shapes as normal Excalidraw elements. Optionally delete or hide the original raster image. Edge cases handled: No image selected: user gets a prompt/toast indicating they need to select an image first. No AI provider configured: user is redirected (via UI message) to Configure AI in Settings. Provider/network errors: user sees an error notification with a generic message and suggestion to check the console for details. Security & Privacy API keys are never transmitted to Excalidraw servers. All requests go directly from the user’s browser to the chosen AI provider endpoint. Client-side encryption Before saving to session/application storage, the config (including keys) is encrypted via browser-side crypto. Only decrypted in memory when needed for a request. Session-scoped storage Keys are stored only for the current browser context (session/application storage depending on environment). Users can clear them at any time via: The Configure AI UI (e.g., “Clear configuration”). Their browser’s storage clearing tools. No logging of secrets API keys are never logged to the console. Network debugging requires the user to inspect their own requests; Excalidraw code does not emit logs containing keys. For example we have tested out with one of the above images in this issue. we were able to acheive the above. and below is a gif explaining the same ![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c9e54ed4-97b0-4451-957f-8cb48a88a9e9)

author: tech-dipesh

body:
@tech-dipesh The fix/workaround from the other issue (which came from an issue on the Brave repo) involved using the following styling on SVG elements to just disable the auto dark feature:

```css
color-scheme: only light
```

It's not really a standard browser feature, so I think disabling it is reasonable - especially as you can still use Excalidraw's dark mode.

If we check the [**Mozilla documentation for `color-scheme`**](https://developer.mozilla.org/en-US/docs/Web/CSS/color-scheme#only) it does explicitly say that:
> Can be used to turn off color overrides caused by Chrome's [Auto Dark Theme](https://developer.chrome.com/blog/auto-dark-theme/#per-element-opt-out), by applying color-scheme: only light; on a specific element or :root.

☝️So perhaps this could be a place to start?

body:
May I ask that this be labeled as a bug report? cc @Mrazator 
Rationale is that, when copying to the clipboard, the "embed scene" toggle is completely ignored.
This was reported back in 2021: see #3467.

Pair this with #6648 (loading a scene overwrites the current one with no way to restore it), and it becomes very easy to accidentally lose a diagram which you thought was safe in (the image you pasted from) your clipboard.
author: baioc

body: Thanks for noticing! Makes sense, though I am not sure if we are going to be able to include scene data in the clipboard, as discussed in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/3467.
author: Mrazator

body:
I think this comment from #3467 is relevant:

> Just may need to make it clearer in the UI that "copy to clipboard" cannot use the embedded scene?

So if this re-encoding can't be avoided by the webapp, I agree that a "fix" would be to change the UI so as to make it obvious to the user that "Copy to clipboard" and "Embed scene" are mutually exclusive things

body: @hewerlin @Mrazator Could you please clarify whether this request is for embedding ThreatPad diagrams, allowing access due to security restrictions, or integrating it with the Threat Model editor?
author: MridulTi

body:
Hi.

Threat Modeling in really simple:
1) What are we working on? Draw system diagram for target scope 
2) What can go wrong? Capture threats 
3) What are we going to do about it? Capture mitigations 
4) Did we do a good Job?

ThreatPad allows to capture threats and mitigations. (2, 3)

If it worked as a web embed, people could draw a system diagram with excalidraw (1), then add a ThreatPad embed as a threat+mitigation notepad (2, 3)

Hope that clarifies the use case. 🙂

You can test the app really easy. It has anonymous, end-to-end encrypted use driven by capability URL approach.

author: tech-dipesh

body:
Same thing happens in the library sidebar now. 

It used to let me use Ctrl + F to find specific library units based off of what text they had. This was happening with the Chrome's built in find functionality. 

For example using the AWS serverless library I could open the side bar, Ctrl + F and search for "lambda" and it would highlight the icon for lambda. When you have hundreds of icons to sift through this search feature was a god send. 

Current functionality just closes the library sidebar when you press Ctrl + F and only searches for text on the canvas. 

---

\* I was able to find a work around while writing out this comment - if you click the URL bar in Chrome while in excalidraw then press Ctrl + F it uses the browsers find functionality and you can search the library sidebar again!

author: 8rianhoang

body: I'd like to work on this issue. The root cause is in the \onKeyDown\ handler in \App.tsx\ — when the event target is a writable element (e.g. contenteditable text), the handler bails out early without calling \e.preventDefault()\ for Ctrl+S, so the browser's default save-html dialog fires instead. The fix is to handle Ctrl+S before the writable-element bail-out check so \e.preventDefault()\ is always called.
author: MD-Mushfiqur123

body: Submitted a fix for this in #11363 — added an exception in the keyboard handler to allow Ctrl/Cmd+S to pass through when editing text, so the save shortcut works correctly instead of triggering the browser's native save dialog.
author: algojogacor

body: Hi team! I'd like to work on this — it's still reproducible on the latest main. My plan is to intercept the `keydown` event in the text editor context and call `event.preventDefault()` when CTRL+S is detected while an input/textarea is focused, so Excalidraw's own save handler fires instead of the browser's. Happy to open a draft PR first if you'd like to review the approach before I go further. Is this still open for contribution?

author: Mrazator

body:
Thank you for your response!

I’m currently using a CRA-based React app, which doesn’t expose the Webpack configuration out of the box. While using craco is a good alternative, I’m unable to go that route. I’m hoping that Excalidraw can address this issue in a future release so that manual configuration changes are no longer required.

Thanks again!
author: abhishekayu

body:
Hi. Our current stance on this is that since CRA is deprecated, we've decided to not focus on backwards compatibility with it. While it _may_ affect other build systems, we haven't gotten any feedback about anything other than that, yet.

That said, we _might_ accept a PR if it doesn't introduce too much complexity and maintenance burden. We'd also need to test it thoroughly against various use cases and build systems to ensure the fix works with those (we don't have automated tests for that, though).

body: The bundle has changed in master with v0.18.0, there is no `excalidraw.development.js` anymore - I would recommend to use the latest version The structure of the package now looks like this, you can generate it by running `yarn build:package` in the root. . Here is a browser example on how to import the package without a bundler https://jsfiddle.net/vfn6dm14/3/. Another example is how to use it with Vite https://codesandbox.io/p/sandbox/github/excalidraw/excalidraw/tree/master/examples/with-script-in-browser. Other changes in v0.18.0 are described here (you might not need it, just fyi) https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/releases/tag/v0.18.0.

body: @eliandoran is it actually an http is an issue? as we are running http in dev as well (vite) The `subset-worker.chunk.js` seems to be served from a file system instead of the `localhost:8080` server, so to me that seems to violate the security policy. Anyway is this only reproducible locally? Could it be that your dev server needs additional configuration for serving dynamically imported chunks? Also note that we are relying on `import.meta.url` to locale the worker chunk, which might be undefined in your environment.
author: Mrazator

body:
In `TriliumNext`  we use webpack to serve a "bundled" Excalidraw.
So potentially Webpack is not correctly packing that file automatically?

However quick question: is the path to that file adjustable (like e.g. it is possible with `EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH` for other assets) or does it need to be `node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/dev/subset-worker.chunk.js`

I'll do a test this evening and report back
author: pano9000

body:
so, I did a quick test, where I used our webpack bundled Excalidraw but did NOT "prune" the `node_modules/@excalidraw` folder as we usually do (hint: we have added the excalidraw package as devDependency and prune devDeps after our build to keep our distributables size small)

⇒ The error does not appear

so it really is actually looking for that file in that specific path and fails, if does not find it:
`node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/dev/subset-worker.chunk.js`

looking at the **bundled JS** and grepping for that file also shows that path
```
new URL("file:///home/pano/TriliumNextNotes/node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/prod/subset-worker.chunk.js"))
```

this is also interesting, because the path points to our project's "root" node_module folder **instead** of the node_module folder inside our  "build" or "dist" folder…
author: pano9000

body:
> However quick question: is the path to that file adjustable (like e.g. it is possible with EXCALIDRAW_ASSET_PATH for other assets) or does it need to be node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/dev/subset-worker.chunk.js

There is just a dynamic, relative, ESM import like `import("./subset-worker.chunk.js")`, so it's not hardcoded but also not adjustable.

> looking at the bundled JS and grepping for that file also shows that path `new URL("file:///home/pano/TriliumNextNotes/node_modules/@excalidraw/excalidraw/dist/prod/subset-worker.chunk.js"))`

Does it mean that webpack does not "copy" this chunk into your bundle?

Btw. even bundling the worker chunk inside the main chunk would be wrong, as the worker does not have access to a ton of APIs which are in the `index.js` (web apis, react, etc.) - would still lead to ` "Failed to use workers for subsetting, falling back to the main thread.", `.

body:
I wanted to share a couple of references I looked at while thinking about moving bound text along an arrow's path: **tldraw** and **FigJam**.

In tldraw, bound text appears to be positioned using a normalized path parameter over the full arrow path `[0,1]`, combined with orthogonal projection to keep the text aligned to the path. One thing worth noting about this approach is that when points are moved, the bound text also shifts its position along the overall path. While this maintains relative position along the entire path, the behavior can feel a little unintuitive in practice, any time you move a point the text will shift as well.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/bfe8ffeb-d1ef-463d-b4c2-e267a7c9ebb8

FigJam handles a similar case with elbow arrows and bound text. It appears they still use an overall path parameter, but attempt to preserve the text’s position relative to its current segment when possible. When applied to their curve connector, however, segment-relative constraints become stricter. In my testing, this behavior feels more intuitive, since the text stays anchored locally to the segment or point the user associated it with. Noting that they do not have the ability to add or remove control points like in Excalidraw.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7fe20cb8-db41-4dec-8a98-9e2594cbd639

After exploring both approaches a bit, the segment-relative behavior seen in FigJam’s curve connector I believe can fit quite naturally with Excalidraw’s existing arrow structure and types and extend across `n` segments. Below is an example of this behavior applied across the different arrow types in Excalidraw.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/3453f7dc-f212-4e99-908e-d890e4fc4717

Curious to see if the community finds this behavior fitting for Excalidraw or if there are any other alternatives or suggestions?
author: cTangonan123

author: sachintha-lk

body:
I'm also able to reproduce this issue on Windows, and I noticed that the `∞` renders correctly if we remove the **font styling** on the `textarea` when editing:
<video src="https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1f8ab38a-4b86-4f86-a71f-1235f7bcdde7"></video>

@sachintha-lk perhaps you could start by looking into how the custom **fonts are handled** in [`Font.ts`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/fonts/Fonts.ts)?

For context:
 * In our [`App.tsx`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/components/App.tsx) there's a `Scene` that contains `ExcalidrawElement`s that are rendered to an HTML `canvas` - [and for `TextElements`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/element/src/renderElement.ts#L478), we're using `context.FillText()`
 * When text being edited, the textbox is implemented as an HTML `textarea` in [`TextWysiwyg.tsx`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/wysiwyg/textWysiwyg.tsx)

And In both cases we're using a **font family** corresponding to the fonts loaded in [`Font.ts`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/fonts/Fonts.ts), which is called from our App.
author: dangerman

body:
Hi @dangerman thanks for the detailed context and pointer!

I was exploring the issue and narrowed down it to be a possible bug with the font `Segoe UI Emoji`. It doesn't let a fallback happen for the `∞` and instead displays an incorrect symbol.  `∫` and  `≠` are also affected in a similar fashion. 

 I confirmed it with a small test by simply rendering on a html canvas.

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/58a26c5b-93ce-4440-98e3-00c30bd06f73)

I'll look in to the [Font.ts](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/packages/excalidraw/fonts/Fonts.ts) and other files and will try to submit a PR soon with a suitable fix.

Again thanks for the help!

body:
Thank you for your feedback, I will try that ; )On 4 May 2025, at 22:50, John Harrington ***@***.***> wrote:﻿
johnha left a comment (excalidraw/excalidraw#9361)
I have seen the issue of placeholders being displayed for images but were painted when I resized the browser.  The issue appears to be after addFiles() method.  Under the cover, addFiles() sticks the dataURL into its internal cache and kicks of the browsers normal image decoding pipeline asynchronously.   You can test if this is the issue for yourself by adding a simple delay after addFiles(): e.g.
await new Promise((resolve) => setTimeout(resolve, 500))
Unfortunately addFiles() is a fire and forget request - you cannot await the response from what I understand.
As an FYI - if the images are being updated after load, I also had to generate a unique FileId for the update (it leaves orphans in the cache) in order for the update to apply.   Something like the following:
const versionedFileId = ${n.nodeID}-${updateVersionOrTimestamp} as FileId // <-- Key change
const nodeImageAsFile: BinaryFileData = {
id: versionedFileId, // Use the unique, versioned ID
....
This helped resolve my issues so a possible temporary fix if your problem is the same.

—Reply to this email directly, view it on GitHub, or unsubscribe.You are receiving this because you authored the thread.Message ID: ***@***.***>

body:
> This is a common placement for these essential functions in many design and drawing applications for a reason.

Could you provide list of examples which application use it there? If it's really an "industry standard" used by most of the apps, then we might move it there.
author: Mrazator

body:
> > This is a common placement for these essential functions in many design and drawing applications for a reason.
> 
> Could you provide list of examples which application use it there? If it's really an "industry standard" used by most of the apps, then we might move it there.

The fact that we're even arguing about something so self-evident is rather telling. Nevertheless, in the interest of progressing this discussion, I'll address your request. Here is a brief, illustrative list of common applications where the undo/redo buttons are typically found near the top of the user interface:

- Office Suites: Word, Docs, Excel, Sheets, PowerPoint, Slides.
- File Managers: Windows File Explorer, macOS Finder - Default system file management applications.
- Code Editors: Visual Studio Code, Sublime Text.
- Web Browsers: Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Brave, Opera, Safari.

Now, I ask you to provide a comparable list of applications where the undo/redo buttons are primarily located at the bottom of the UI, so we can proceed with a proper comparison.
author: cos4ni2s

body:
There is no argument, I am genuinely curious since from my experience, modern editing software, including whiteboards, does not expose dedicated undo / redo buttons in the UI - take figma, notion or even the mentioned vscode as examples. 

Though it seems when they do, it's often close to the their toolbar - will need to do a proper research.

Office suites is a fair point, but for the rest seems that you are mixing undo / redo with navigating the history. Unless you are talking about the "OS-level" toolbar, when undo / redo is often hidden behind a submenu (i.e. Edit > Undo). In that case it does not satisfy the frequent access requirement.

> for the rest seems that you are mixing undo / redo with navigating the history

@cos4ni2s is this true or am I missing the dedicated undo / redo buttons in every single example you mentioned?
author: Mrazator

body:
It seems to me that it only takes a little common sense, or brief experience with basic computer use, to recognize that the Undo/Redo function in applications like whiteboarding, image/video editing, and note-taking is the equivalent of the Back/Forward function found in file managers, browsers, etc. And the irony is that they are all situated in the top section.
I can't really picture Safari having dedicated Undo/Redo buttons, any more than I can imagine Microsoft Word having dedicated Back/Forward buttons.
I hope this short discussion has actually exposed a more significant malfunction with the Excalidraw staff than simply an inconvenient button position for those reading along.
author: cos4ni2s

body:
Back / forward is not equivalent to undo / redo, in fact most of the examples you mentioned (browsers, file managers, code editors, etc.) implement both, with undo / redo buttons often not being exposed in desktop versions.

We will consider adding undo / redo to the toolbar at one point (perhaps configurable), but at the moment it does not align with our unified design across the stack - especially for tablet / mobile, where there is not enough place for additional buttons in the toolbar.

For the time being, please consider using shortcuts (ctrl/cmd + z and ctrl/cmd + shift + z) for frequent Undo / Redo usage in the desktop mode.
author: Mrazator

body:
Backward/Forward and Undo/Redo fundamentally serve the same user need within their respective contexts: navigating sequential states. Their similar icons, which are not coincidental, highlight this point, justifying their consistent UI positions (mostly in the top section) across different applications users routinely interact with and are familiar with.

Therefore, the distinction you're raising (browse history vs. action history) is irrelevant to this core point and serves more as a deflection than a valid argument.

A minimum degree of intelligence, common sense, or at least some sincerity would have made this discussion much more productive. I have been met with stubbornness, perhaps even maliciousness.

After all, this is a UI design decision that shouldn't be left to staff responsible for issues tracking and sorting.

> I hope this short discussion has actually exposed a more significant malfunction with the Excalidraw management than simply an inconvenient button position, for those reading along.

body:
Our goal is the simplicity, in order to remove the decision paralysis when choosing a font, so we won't be likely a lot of similarly looking fonts in the future.

I.e. at the moment we have these categories "hand-drawn", "normal", "code" and "display". In this case both Poppins and Roboto fall into the "normal" category, for which we have Nunito already. Though, I agree Nunito looks a bit more handdrawn than both, so one of those might be more useful for "professional" looking diagrams (less handrawn).

Additionally, we are planning to support adding custom fonts in plus.excalidraw.com, no ETA yet.

body: Is this a valid bug though? When a frame wraps some elements should it be handled the same as if the elements were grouped?
author: hazemkrimi

body:
Actually, we can leave it, though I am not sure how useful it is (in which scenarios) to move just the frame, without moving the elements inside. At least there is no other way to do it than with the properties panel.

However, we would need to fix the resulting bug, which keeps the elements visible and bound to the frame, when the elements leave the frame:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/ca992316-2bae-4b4c-a20e-b7e859a683a5

Instead, they should get unbound when leaving the frame fully, similar to this case:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/61a2c637-6961-4c26-b6b3-6ee14f31da74
author: Mrazator

body:
> Is this a valid bug though? When a frame wraps some elements should it be handled the same as if the elements were grouped?

Frame shouldn't interfere in any function of its elements like resizing. It should just keep them together while in movements and let's the user to access elements for selecting and manipulating them effortlessly.

body:
I managed to fix moving the frame children along the frame when dragging the x and y coords [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/compare/master...hazemKrimi:excalidraw:fix-frame-coords):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/30c54801-d374-4ef4-ad68-6e8bf0036781

But updating the coords through typing in the x or y rather than dragging causes the child element(s) to jump further than the frame:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/b97da372-9a07-4223-88fc-966e068760ca

Should I continue to fix this issue along with detaching elements that go out of a frame boundary or do you suggest another approach? @Mrazator @imanzaker

body: Hi! This is a limitation of the current web embeds implementation. It gets pretty tricky, as we can't draw them directly inside the canvas, and can't make holes inside the canvas either. Perhaps we could find a better solution in the future, perhaps a specific (SVG) layer for "annotations" drawn above all of these elements, including web embeds.
author: Mrazator

body: If they can't coexist in the same canvas, would it be possible to provide a toggle that simply moves the drawing canvas above all web embeds? It is not the perfect solution, but it would make annotating web embeds actually possible.
author: Alb-O

body:
Hey @AMC-Albert I ran into this as well, however i don't think this is something that can be resolved in it's present state, the reason is that if you move the drawing canvas above, then you wouldn't be able to interact with the embedded content.  Additionally if your shape had a background set, it would cover/hide the embedded contents.

There might be other ways, like @Mrazator said, with adding an annotation layer, but I have not dug into that at all myself.

author: Mrazator

body:
This is intended behavior. You aligned an arrow segment with another segment close enough that the segment simplifier unified the two segments. However this also resulted in removing the manually fixed segment 2 segments (one by size being imperceptibly small, the other via alignment with another segment) -> no fixed segments remained -> rerouting accordingly.

@dwelle if you think this should behave differently, let's reopen this ticket and come up with a set of rules how the simplifier should work.

body:
@mtolmacs is this issue resolved or is it up for grabs? as I have a solution for this 

**why this is happening?**
This is due to the proximity-based binding heuristic inside excalidraw's arrow binding logic
specifically `bindToElement(...)` here

so we can solve it by adjusting binding sensitivity based on zoom level
or by disabling binding when converting type

Hi @elenniko, are you in collab mode by any chance? To fix this, I'd
- exit the collab mode if you're in it
- hard refresh (shift + cmd + r)

Why this is happening: when you delete an element, the element is not removed right away, instead, we set the flag "isDeleted" to true, thus resulting in the same file size too large error.

author: Destreyf

body:
Yeah, it's a valid concern.

You don't have to store the "files" property with all the base64 content as part of your MongoDB document, but upload it somewhere else i.e. S3 or similar blob storage.  

> Could I set up excalidraw so that when I add an image, I create a URL

In that case your `fileId` would be the id, which would be used to retrieve the file from the blob storage.
author: Mrazator
Destreyf

body:
> do you have any guidance/examples on how we could handle this if someone drops or pastes an image?

There is [onPaste](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/#onpaste), but I am guessing that won't help you much.

Right now you will need some kind of a cache to track the image elements and status of their files (loading, saving, saved), as we are doing (check `FileManager`) in https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/master/excalidraw-app/collab/Portal.tsx#L104.

In the future, you will be able to subscribe to `onIncrement` API, which will allow you to find out whether an image was just added / updated / deleted (API is already on master, but this bug needs to be fixed first https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/issues/8321).

> ideally it would never get encoded as base64 into the excalidraw document
> so being able to just reference the image rather than embedding it would be a massive boon for us on that front.

Understood, though the base64 encoded files need to be part of the `files` App property, as pure link won't just work. Files are not really part of the document, just loaded in memory, as only the ids will make it to `elements`, which you would store in the DB. Files, on the other hand, will be part of the copy event or included in the exported scene - which works offline, compared to links.

In fact, we are doing this in OSS as well, you can check file upload and download, respectively:

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/6c0ff7fc5c45719a7a659f613be169ce8bf295da/excalidraw-app/collab/Portal.tsx#L104

https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/6c0ff7fc5c45719a7a659f613be169ce8bf295da/excalidraw-app/collab/Collab.tsx#L415

Btw. it's possibly to override the `fileId`, in case you need some custom one, with [generateIdForFile](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/#generateidforfile).
author: Mrazator

body:
I have been digging (not very far yet) into the way that stuff's handled, I see that you're using blob urls, which are being constructed from the image base64, I guess what I would want to be able to do is intercept this process somewhat, reading the image to a blob url would be fairly easy to do, just need to be able to capture the incoming image data.

It sounds like the onIncrement might get most of the way there.  The main reason I want to avoid the base64 is in our use case we'll be doing a lot with collaboration, and having to download the base64 content for every client makes it hard to implement caching strategies for network transfer, and we're working on integrating this with yjs via y-sweet, so we want to avoid having large documents as we don't really control the end users who will be using this in a collaborative setup.
author: Destreyf

body:
> The main reason I want to avoid the base64 is in our use case we'll be doing a lot with collaboration, and having to download the base64 content for every client makes it hard to implement caching strategies for network transfer

Couldn't you fetch the images from a CDN (relying on caching) and then convert them into base64?

> we're working on integrating this with yjs via y-sweet, so we want to avoid having large documents

Yeah, that's another reason not to include the binary files in the yjs document, as the IDs are enough.
author: Mrazator

body:
I was talking about if it was inside of the y Sweet document it would be hard to cache.

The main need is to capture the paste/drop so that I can extract them before they ever get to the document which would then be synced over yjs.
author: Destreyf

body: I've been using image urls from my s3 bucket in my excalidraw setup for a while now. What i do is that when an image element gets added to the doc by a participant, i let the "local" participant use the base64 initially, but before it's sent to other peers or my database, i upload the image to s3 and send them the element with the file_id the same as the image's id that is also present on s3. So it becomes easy to fetch the urls by just doing something like `${S3_BASE_URL}/${file_id}` and then using addFiles with that id as the file_id, and the s3 url as the DataURL. This works in the sense that it's working in collaboration pretty well. However, as soon as I try to export the canvas as an image (important for me as a store canvas snapshots at times as thumbnails), the export fails as it marks the canvas as tainted. According to some research online all I really need to do is add "crossOrigin=anonymous" to the image before it gets added to the canvas but of course I can't do that since i'm using the addFiles method which doesn't allow me to mark certain files as "crossOrigin=anonymous". Does anyone think that could work?

body:
It turned out that our elements were sometimes missing a `lineHeight` field, and the field was not being given a default when we called `updateScene`, because we were not using the `convertToExcalidrawElements` helper.

author: Cheapstar

body:
According to my understanding, the problem seems to be:
- `segmentMidPointHoveredCoords` is outdated because it isn't updated by `handlePointDragging()` when a point isn't being dragged.
- After moving a point, `handlePointDragging()` updates `hoverPointIndex` or `segmentMidPointHoveredCoords` — hence point highlight rendering logic will fire on any pointer-down later (this includes moving the line)

Removing the `hoverPointIndex` and `segmentMidPointHoveredCoords` [updates in `handlePointDragging()`](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/a8ebe514dae890a68512961cd52ec14d405b6f7d/packages/element/src/linearElementEditor.ts#L414) should fix it, but these updates might be there for other reasons.

A possible hack is to make sure `editor.hoverPoint !== -1` [here](https://github.com/excalidraw/excalidraw/blob/a8ebe514dae890a68512961cd52ec14d405b6f7d/packages/excalidraw/renderer/interactiveScene.ts#L823). This avoids rendering midpoint highlights unless hovered. It will still render non-midpoint highlights while moving, but these are synced anyway. EDIT: realized it wouldn't render highlights on not-yet-created midpoints.

body:
Hi! 👋 I’d like to work on this issue.

From the discussion, it looks like the search results currently reorder based on the elements’ y-axis, but the intended improvement is to keep the ordering stable even when elements move on the canvas.

I’ll start by reviewing how the search results are generated and explore adding a stable key (likely the element’s id) so that dragging or repositioning items won’t affect the order. Once I confirm a clean approach, I’ll share an update here before opening a PR.

If there are edge cases or scenarios I should pay special attention to, please let me know!

author: Himanshu197200

author: themavik

body:
## Bug Analysis

**Root Cause:**

The issue is in `packages/excalidraw/actions/manager.tsx` at line 109.

The `handleKeyDown()` method collects ALL actions whose `keyTest()` 
returns true for the same keyboard event. If more than one action matches, it logs:
"Canceling as multiple actions match this shortcut"and cancels without executing any action.

For `Ctrl+]` and `Ctrl+Shift+]`, there are two potential sources of conflict:

1. **Multiple actions matching the same shortcut** — both `actionBringForward` (`actionZindex.tsx` line 67) and potentially another action have overlapping `keyTest` conditions for `CTRL_OR_CMD + BracketRight`.

2. **WYSIWYG text editor interception** — in `packages/excalidraw/wysiwyg/textWysiwyg.tsx` at line 661-666, `Ctrl+]` is used for indent/outdent inside text editing mode and calls`preventDefault()`, consuming the event before it reaches the action manager.

**Why it's hard to notice:**
The conflict only cancels silently from the user's perspective — no visual feedback, nothing happens. The only evidence is the console warning which most users never see.

**Suggested Fix:**
Audit all `keyTest` implementations that use `CODES.BRACKET_RIGHT` with `CTRL_OR_CMD` to find which actions overlap, then disambiguate their conditions.

The locking/selection behavior we implement is pretty standard (Miro, FigJam, Whimsical, Mural, Lucid...) and makes sense if you think about it.

Take the case for example which is more obvious than yours above for why clicking on the locked element should select it instead of the unlocked element below:

https://excalidraw-ip53eu05x-excalidraw.vercel.app/#json=QVIqXruOBB0SQBykMqIgs,cMmB1s4qCBWNpins-NbQDA

Now consider the locked red rectangle completely covering the green one, it would be even more confusing if the green one got selected instead.

---

IMO to achieve the workflow you want, there should be another way to do so:

- use lasso selection (cmd-alt, or lasso tool)
- implement a way to cycle through shapes underneath cursor. I wanted to do that by cmd-clicking, but don't remember why I didn't yet (I dimly recall there was some clash with other behavior for that modifier, not sure).

author: zsviczian

body:
Why not move the image below the text and other elements?

Either way, I think we need to solve precision selection / highlighting of hovered elements first, and make it easier to select elements behind other elements.

I don't think locking elements to accomplish this is a good and intuitive workflow that most people do or we'd wanna promote as "the way to do this". Given that, tailoring the lock-selection behavior to this use case would be counterproductive.

Of course if we think the lock-selection behavior is bad with respect to other, more common workflows, that's another matter.
author: dwelle

body: I use locking in many situations. Layers are important in terms of how overlaps are rendered, thus moving items to the top is not always a desirable solution. In my mind, the whole point of locking is to get items out of the way. Now, with the "better unlock", locked items get in the way. An alternative solution that allows the user to select locked elements (maybe like the slide sorter for presentations) where you can select which ones to unlock would be better.

body:
Hi @Mrazator 
I wanted to ask for clarification on the intended behavior for the align and distribute tools in relation to groups. Are you envisioning support similar to how applications like Illustrator and Figma handle it–where:
- Grouped elements can be aligned/distributed with non-grouped elements or other groups, and
- While in "group edit mode"(double-click on the group), any selected elements within the group (as long as more than one is selected) can also be aligned/distributed(requires > 2)?

I've been referencing the deep wiki(great resource) to get a better understanding of how state changes and actions are implemented, and would love to help out with this if the direction is clear.
Thanks!
author: cTangonan123

body:
Hey @cTangonan123 

> Grouped elements can be aligned/distributed with non-grouped elements or other groups, and
> While in "group edit mode"(double-click on the group), any selected elements within the group (as long as more than one is selected) can also be aligned/distributed(requires > 2)?

Yeah, that would be nice!
author: Mrazator

body:
Awesome @Mrazator , 
I had a chance to dig into the issue, and here’s what I’ve found so far:
- The first bullet point (align/distribute working with grouped and non-grouped elements) seems to already be handled in `align.ts` and `distribute.ts`.
- For the second point (align/distribute while in group edit mode), I made a quick fix by passing in `appState` to detect when we're in "edit group mode." With that, I was able to get align/distribute working for selected elements within a group, and the tests are passing

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/8ae31d6d-c3c2-48c2-bdca-d053a2bc517d

However, I don't think that is a permanent fix, as it'd need to respect the condition of nested groupings, which is where odd behaviors seem to occur.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/69113a7c-8493-440f-8331-289a889a4625

### What I'm thinking next:
- Going to dive into `group.ts` to see what helper methods are available and how grouping hierarchy is managed.
- Are there any actions or features that already account for group hierarchy that I could use as a reference? I thought of the boolean operations, but I don’t believe they’ve been implemented yet (possible future feature?).

Would love any guidance or suggestions if you have ideas on how you’d like to see this handled structurally!

body:
Hi @imanzaker Thanks for taking the time for creating this issue

> We can't add an entity to an entire group. If we select both and hit the ctrl/g, it will create an unwanted new group and add all entities to that. Perhaps an option should exist that allows the user to drag entities into an entire group, causing the bundle box of the group to expand and wrap around the entity(s). By dropping them, the entities will be added to that group without creating a new group.

Could you share with us why creating a new group is undesired? 

> We can't remove an entity from a group without ungrouping all the group members. The user should be able to select a member of a group and hit a button to extract that entity from its entire group.

True, we probably could/should do something about this. 

> There is no way to select multiple entities in a group.

Select a group => double clicking on a subgroup or item would select that subgroup/item => "shift + click" would allow you to select multiple "entities"
author: ryan-di

body:
Hello @ryan-di, you're welcome

> Could you share with us why creating a new group is undesired?

Obviously creating a new group with any single addition to the group will increase the complexity and difficulty of editing your drawing.  Sometimes we end up in chaos.

> Select a group => double clicking on a subgroup or item would select that subgroup/item => "shift + click" would allow you to select multiple "entities"

 I mean, selecting multiple entities inside a group to apply some changes to them that there is still no way to do that.

A solution to solve all these problems could be defining a key like Ctrl. By holding that key and dragging members of a group, all the group bundle boxes will stand still so we can drop the selected items into any other groups to change their group, or outside of all groups to ungroup them.
author: imanzaker

body:
Hey @imanzaker,

I came across this issue and wanted to share some input. Currently, it is possible to ungroup either single elements or multiple elements from a group using cut and paste while in group edit mode, as @ryan-di mentioned earlier. You can do this by:
- Double-clicking the group to enter edit mode.
- Selecting the element(s) you want to remove.
- Then, cutting and pasting the selected elements—this effectively removes them from the group.

Here's a quick visual reference for that behavior:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/0e4505a8-67f2-49d6-887f-8453c2e21c2c

This also applies to nested groups, and seems consistent with how other drawing applications handle similar actions.

However, something that’s missing compared to other tools is the ability to paste new elements into an existing group while in group edit mode. Currently, Excalidraw doesn’t support that:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/6f191c73-6497-4c20-85fc-cf4ec8eeb0e3

Would supporting paste actions while in group edit mode be a potential solution? That way, users could both add to and remove from groups more fluidly, similar to how other design tools handle group editing.
author: cTangonan123

body:
Hi @cTangonan123 
If extracting an entity from a group involves cutting it, the reasonable way to add it is to select the target and paste the entity. The problem in this scenario is that EX pastes where the mouse spots, not where you selected.
author: imanzaker

body:
Great point @imanzaker — really glad you brought it up!

One possible solution could be adding a “Paste in Place” option (hotkey: `Shift + Ctrl + V`), similar to what’s available in other design tools like Adobe Illustrator. It’s intuitive and would add a lot of flexibility to Excalidraw.

The below video shows the removal from group the addition to group with the "paste in place" option in Adobe Illustrator.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/7cd25478-ca3a-4d38-9d63-e57ae277cd5b

If this option were introduced — alongside support for pasting while editing a group — I think entity additions and removals to and from groups could be handled much more seamlessly.
author: cTangonan123

body:
it could be the shortest way to fulfill this purpose, although is not as user-friendly as drag and drop. Due to the priority is accomplishing the circle of editing a group, I support your idea. 
Also we should pay attention that adding entities involves the exact level of group and subgroup which the user has selected.

You highlight the challenge in the task quite well. If within a nested group pasting should inherit the editingGroupId along with its parent groupId.

Will make it a point to look more into it in the coming week, and keep you posted.
author: cTangonan123

body: Groups here don't work with the groupId. You gotta trace the order of groupmates' IDs in any object.
author: imanzaker

body:
Yes, I may have described the situation a bit too abstractly.

From what I understand so far, group hierarchy is enforced through the order of an element’s `groupIds` array. Identifying the correct `groupIds` to assign will be key.

The tricky part, I think, is determining which `groupIds` should be inherited — especially since not every element within a group shares the same number or depth of `groupIds`. For example, in the case of nested groups, we likely don’t want the pasted element to inherit the nested group’s ID — just the outer one, depending on context.
author: cTangonan123

body: It is operationally implementable. Just in case you get a deep perception of the saving groups routine.
author: imanzaker

body:
> It is operationally implementable. Just in case you get a deep perception of the saving groups routine.

Thanks — that makes sense.

From what I’ve gathered, the intended behavior is that when pasting into a group, the new element should inherit all `groupIds` that come after the `editingGroupId` in the source element’s `groupIds` array. In other words, the inheritance is based on the position of `editingGroupId` — everything to the right of it is preserved.

I’ll double-check though on the saving logic to make sure this holds true, but let me know if I’m misinterpreting anything.
author: cTangonan123

body: You are going well. Look at how it records the IDs in the hierarchical structure of groups inside any object, and watch the changes happen alongside in the JSON with moving elements between tiers. It will take less time than others describe it.

body:
> only items that are fully encloused should be part of the selection

The argument for not aligning was that we want to make the lasso selection a first party citizen on the mobile, and there you don't have a lot of room for selecting, so partial selection makes more sense.

Also, I'm nowadays using lasso selection something like 90% of the time (using cmd-alt-drag) as it turned out way more useful than box selection is, and I think part of the reason is the non-enclosure behavior.

It's a personal preference of course. We'll likely make the selection behavior customizable.

> once you have selected something, you should be able to interact with it without switching modes first (stay in lasso mode but move resize selection).

Yes, we'll try to do something like with the box selection, at least on mobile.

For now, you can start using the cmd-alt-drag while staying in the selection tool. Best of both worlds.

> one other selection mode that I would love to see implemented is line selection. It is similar to lasso selection except that it only selects what the line touches. Should be easier to implement than lasso selection, right?

We could. We might. But we don't want to overwhelm with selection tools. In the above screenshot, you could achieve this with 4 strokes with the lasso tool and IMO it'd still be faster/safe than using the mode you're suggesting because it's easier to do 3 straight lines than a loop like that where you need to be slower to not make a mistake (but YYMV).

![Image](https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/c0dbdfa0-506b-44a3-aecf-798e13ffaa12)

author: maielo

body:
Hi everyone! I saw this feature request and decided to build the "Inline Calculation" portion of it for a fork I am working on. 

I successfully implemented a real-time text parser that intercepts standard arithmetic and trig functions (like `sin(90)=`) and injects the answer on the fly. 

**Here is a quick video demo of the implementation in action:**
https://www.loom.com/share/f82584cceabe4906999dd11a0d5e0ab5

**A few technical details on how I handled the edge cases:**
* **Regex Parsing:** It safely extracts formulas ending in `=` without evaluating incomplete strings.
* **Multi-line Safety:** It restricts evaluation to the current line so previous answers don't accidentally get dragged into the execution layer.
* **Trigonometry Mappings:** It maps human-readable strings like `sin()` and `^` to `Math.sin()` and `**`, including automatic Degree-to-Radian conversion under the hood.
* **Backspace Loop Prevention:** It tracks the previous length of the string before the state update to ensure the injection only fires on text *addition*, completely preventing the React infinite-render loop when a user presses backspace.

I haven't built the 'Unit Conversion' portion of this request, but I have the core math engine working cleanly. Would the maintainers be open to me submitting a Pull Request for this foundational part, or would you prefer I package it as an external community script? 

Let me know!

author: dwelle

body:
Hi, I've been looking into this and traced the root cause.

**Proposed approach:** 1. Comment on the issue first per contributing guidelines. 2. Study PR #4742 (the 2022 fix for mobile link click) and PR #4735 (original desktop feature) to understand the expected behavior. 3. Trace the regression: find what changed in the touch/click event handling path since PR #4742 that broke mobile link-open behavior. 4. The fix likely involves ensuring the tap/touch handler in view mode triggers the same link-open logic as the desktop click handler — probably in the event handling code that distinguishes pointer types. 5. Test using browser mobile emulation. Base branch: master.

I'll open a draft PR shortly. Happy to adjust based on your preference.

body:
This is actually by design. We could change it of course, if we think it adds value. Sadly there's no precedent for this beside tldraw, which does what we're doing.

IMO the idea is that eraser should make it easy to remove everything under cursor. We'd need to try out on real world scenarios if the proposed change keeps that UX, while enabling some more use cases (which I assume is the goal of this change).

Note that the proposed change _doesn't_ really solve the user's issue from the Discord, which is about removing annotations (mainly handdrawn) above images. This change would still end up deleting the image as well.
author: dwelle

body:
> This is actually by design

Understood, otherwise it would be a bug :p

> Note that the proposed change doesn't really solve the user's issue from the Discord, which is about removing annotations (mainly hand-drawn) above images. This change would still end up deleting the image as well.

Yeah, although if you would be super careful and hit only freedraw, it would delete just that or the yellow diamond shape in the example, which is easier to hit.

Other option is to make the eraser work only for freedraw, similar to FigJam is doing, so that it won't delete other shapes, which could be deleted with selection -> delete.

> IMO the idea is that eraser should make it easy to remove everything under cursor. We'd need to try out on real world scenarios if the proposed change keeps that UX, while enabling some more use cases (which I assume is the goal of this change).

Subjectively I find it unintuitive as is, due to the concept of layers - you can't select something that is behind a shape, but suddenly you could delete it - which to me is unexpected.

As there seem to be quite a number of issues for touch device related support, but seemingly nothing tying them together I created this issue that collects them so it will be easier to discover the current state for touch device support and which issues that are still open.

I did not see if collection/meta issues are supported from the [contribution guide](https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/introduction/contributing), additionally I did not find touch related issues on the [roadmap](https://github.com/orgs/excalidraw/projects/3/views/1). body:
> We should probably add this dialog, instead of reconciling the local & remote data.

Local data should be safe. It is stored in local storage, and saving to local storage is paused during collab.

We probably want the local data to be moved to the room only on room creation.

But I'm not sure if the reconciling is there to solve other issues.
author: Brikaa

body: > Local data should be safe. It is stored in local storage, and saving to local storage is paused during collab. True, but once you stop the collab, you'll face the same issue. We show this dialog at the moment, but I don't think people actually read it, so it's still pretty dangerous and somewhat unintuitive for external users. --- I was thinking it would be more intuitive to force the user to make the decision in the beginning - backup or replace the existing content (similar to sharable links), but now I am second guessing whether it's not actual worse than what we currently have. > But I'm not sure if the reconciling is there to solve other issues. author: mtolmacs

body:
Hey @Ricksanchez000 ,
Thanks for raising this! I'm still relatively new to the codebase but recently worked on a fix for alignment functionality and can share some insight.

Currently, Excalidraw aligns elements relative to the bounding box extremes of the current selection:
- Align Left aligns all selected objects to the left-most object's edge.
- Align Top aligns all objects to the top-most object's edge, and so on.

At the moment, there isn't a way to manually choose a "reference" or "key" object (the one that stays fixed), like you can in some design tools (e.g., Adobe products where you click again to designate a key object after the initial selection). I agree that adding this could make the alignment workflow much more intuitive and flexible.

Makes me curious to know if something like this happens to be on the roadmap?
cc @dwelle
author: cTangonan123

body:
@dwelle Does this RCA sound about right? ⏬ 

Issue: Mobile arrow endpoint drift was caused by floating-point precision errors in the _updatePoints method (linearElementEditor.ts:1612-1630).

What happens: When dragging one arrow endpoint, the system:
- Recalculates the element's bounding box center using floating-point division
- Applies position compensation to maintain element positioning
- Accumulates tiny precision errors that shift ALL points globally, causing the non-dragged endpoint to drift.

Why worse on mobile: Touch coordinates have different precision characteristics than mouse coordinates, and high-DPI screens add coordinate complexity that compounds the floating-point errors.

author: Solidatary

body:
I think it is because the collision calculations (calculating the bounds of the element, and calculating the intersection with the eraser) use the points representing the path that the mouse took, rather than what was actually drawn (by perfect-freehand):

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/24d82545-2502-4519-8b77-7094269eb27f

The pink points here show the curves with which the eraser should intersect. Notice how that curve is almost non-existent in case of a dot. Also, if we visualize the bounds in case of the dot, they would be squashed in the center of the dot.

In the previous versions, this was probably mitigated due to approximations.

This is also probably why the selection bounding box is inaccurate on freedraw elements sometimes.
author: Brikaa

body:
This video shows the eraser and selection bounding box working correctly after the actual drawn points are used in bounds and intersection checking:

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/1fa721aa-2844-4beb-b873-3140c0f0172e

But probably not the most performant solution.
author: Brikaa

body: @mtolmacs thoughts?
author: dwelle
cTangonan123

body: Yes, this is indeed an issue and it is caused by my faulty assumption that freehand collision checks can be done by intersection testing the eraser path with the freehand path. Instead the freehand outline should be tested against the eraser path, but that might not be very performant, but the outline could be simplified for hit testing.
author: mtolmacs

body: I think tldraw does something similar to @cTangonan123's idea. They treat dots as circles and check the intersection with the circle, while still using the points path for larger freedraws.

author: Hell1213

body: The main issue is that actionToggleEraserTool behaves differently from regular shape tools: it tracks lastActiveTool so the user can return to their previous tool using event.key == "E". However, it wasn't accounting for active LinearElementEditor states. Specifically, when a linear element is being edited (isEditing) or its points are being dragged (isDragging), pressing E would trigger unintended eraser behavior. Adding guards for these two states, as well as !appState.newElement, to the keyTest prevents the inconsistent behavior reported by @Brikaa.

author: levi42x

body:
> Hi [@Ayushjhawar8](https://github.com/Ayushjhawar8), could you clarify if you're looking for a persistent laser like on iPad, or more of a highlighter-style tool?

Hello @levi42x, What I had in mind is something closer to the iPad’s persistent laser. Essentially, it would work like this: you can draw freely (similar to the current laser), but the strokes remain visible until you click/tap again to clear all of them at once.

So rather than being a highlighter-style tool, it’s more of a laser/annotation mode where the drawings stay until explicitly cleared. This way, presenters can keep emphasis on specific areas as long as needed, and then remove everything in one action when done.

body:
Hi! I'd like to help implement this persistent laser option.

I understand the scope:
- Add a toggle/checkbox in the laser tool menu
- 'Temporary' (default, current behavior) → laser fades after a few seconds
- 'Persistent' (new) → laser stays until manually cleared/toggled off
- Backward compatibility preserved

My approach would be:
1. Add state for the persistence toggle
2. Modify laser tool UI to show the checkbox
3. Adjust the fade behavior based on toggle state
4. Include tests and documentation update

Would you be open to a PR for this? I can start with a draft implementation if this aligns with your vision.

body: Hello, local storage has a limit and when this is exceeded the elements won't be saved. Is this an issue that shall be fixed? If so a proposed fix would just to communicate to the user that a maximum number of elements have been used and not allow the paste at all.  Right now the error is caught and logged but not communicated to the user.
author: ericvannunen

body:
The problem is that localstorage has limited amount of storage, so this error is not really "fixable". However, when this error occurs, an error is raised: "DOMException: The quota has been exceeded."

Suggested solution: catch error and display to the user that storage quota has been exceeded. I could work on this if desired 👍

Update:
- Chrome: `QuotaExceededError: Failed to execute 'setItem'` 
- Firefox: `DOMException: The quota has been exceeded.`

Other browser may raise different errors, would need to be investigated further.
author: h0lm1

body:
In chrome the limit, at least from my testing is around 10800 elements. Not sure if it is a common use case to have close to this many elements in a canvas. To support more elements, a solution could be to store elements in IndexedDB instead of localStorage. However, depending on how common this issue is, maybe it is sufficient to let the user know that the localstorage quota has exceeded. 

Changing from localStorage to IndexedDB may cause problems with already existing canvases though, I'm not too sure how that would be handled, any other thoughts on this?

body:
- Do not use the pre-built container image (whoever wrote the README on Dockerhub lied or someone pushed the wrong build, as it definitely has tracking and external dependencies as of the time I'm writing this)
- If using the provided `docker-compose.yml` comment out the entire `volumes:` sections and all items in them, as they are not needed, and, in fact, bind mounting the entire source directory with write permissions is a terrible practice. 
- Set environment variable `VITE_APP_ENABLE_TRACKING=False` either in your compose or .env file
- Manually create a network with internal flag set `[podman|docker] network create --internal excalidraw`
- Edit compose or run behavior to use that network
- Edit the file `scripts/woff2/woff2-vite-plugins.js` , at the top change `const OSS_FONTS_CDN = "https://excalidraw.nyc3.cdn.digitaloceanspaces.com/oss/";` to `const OSS_FONTS_CDN = "/";`
- I encountered an error during build relating to the file size of something being too big, so I edited file `excalidraw-app/vite.config.mts` on line 142 (workbox section) and added line `maximumFileSizeToCacheInBytes: 3000000,`

There are many problems with this project but it provides a nice canvas for freely sketching stuff. I hope the contributors have time to clean it up and bring the collaboration features to self-hosted like they said.

body:
Thank you! I'll revisit this as soon as I can. 
FYI It took the best part of two days to get it stable and behaving properly... but it's now fully integrated into a Svelte 5 app, loads drawings at the correct zoom and centered, saves to a sqlite database and even stores the library icons in the database for convenience and adapts to reflect whichever theme my app is running. It also blocks network requests. 

For anyone struggling... the biggest issue was I used Claude Code to generate most of the code. 

It turns Claude's default is to guess the names of function etc. and lie about reading the manual. 

Having Excalidraw embedded this way is brilliant. Well worth the trouble...

Thanks again.

body:
Hi maintainers,

I’m exploring the idea of adding auto-correct for freehand strokes, for example, converting rough lines or circles into clean shapes.
I noticed that Excalidraw already has a shortcut where users can press a number key to insert precise shapes, which works as a workaround.

Before investing time in implementing this feature, I wanted to ask: do you think an auto-correct freehand option would add significant value to users, or would it be mostly redundant given the existing shortcut?

I’d love to hear your thoughts to avoid duplicating functionality unnecessarily.

Thanks for your guidance!

@excalidraw/core

body:
Though there are third party solutions to this problem like dAIgram.app : ( Upload your PNG flowchart image (supports hand-drawn sketches, whiteboards, or screenshots). The tool uses OCR for text extraction and GPT-4o (OpenAI) for detecting shapes, arrows, and connections. It analyzes the image to reconstruct the diagram structure, then generates an editable version where you can add/remove elements, adjust colors, and refine layouts._) 

Here's what i think my approach to this looks like : 
1. Leverage Existing AI Diagram Tools -off-the-shelf AI tool (like dAIgram.app or DiagrammingAI.com) that converts PNG flowcharts to editable diagrams, then adapt their output to Excalidraw’s JSON format. ( it wouldnt work though ) 
2. Build a dedicated backend pipeline using AI models to process PNGs, extract elements, and output Excalidraw JSON. Inspired by multi-agent systems like Mastra.ai’s whiteboard-to-Excalidraw converter. -> 
[Frontend Upload (PNG)] 
    ↓ (POST /api/import-diagram)
[Backend API Endpoint]
    ↓ (Preprocess Image)
[Multi-Agent Workflow]
    ├── Agent 1: Image → Structured Data (CSV/JSON)
    ├── Agent 2: Validation & Refinement Loop
    ├── Agent 3: Conversion to Excalidraw Schema
    └── Agent 4: Final JSON Validation Loop
    ↓
[Output: Excalidraw JSON Response]
    ↓ (Return to Frontend for Canvas Load)

author: RisabhKedai

body:
Hi @dwelle ,

I looked into the freedraw fill issues (#7333, #10039) and here’s what I found.

### Problem

1. **Loop detection is too simplistic**

   ```ts
   isPathALoop(element.points)
   ```

   This only checks if start and end points match, which isn’t a reliable way to detect a closed shape. It doesn’t handle self-intersections or multiple loops.

2. **RoughJS doesn’t always see a closed shape**
   Even when the above returns true, the `simplifiedPoints` passed to RoughJS may not form a valid closed polygon. In those cases, no `fillPath` is generated → fill breaks.

3. **Self-intersecting paths**
   Shapes like stars are treated as a single path instead of multiple enclosed regions, so fill behaves incorrectly.

---

### Proposed approach

* Replace `isPathALoop` with logic that detects **actual enclosed regions**, not just endpoint equality.
* Detect intersections and split the path into **individual loops**.
* In `generateElementShape`, create **one drawable per loop** instead of assuming a single shape.
* Ensure each loop passed to RoughJS is a properly closed polygon so fill works consistently.

---

### Next steps

Planning to prototype loop detection + integrate it into `generateElementShape`, then test against self-intersecting and multi-loop cases.

Happy to take this up if this direction makes sense 👍
