body:
Thanks a lot everyone for engaging with this issue 🙌

I agree with @prateekbisht23 that a simpler solution would be better for end users instead of needing LaTeX input.

As @sachintha-lk mentioned, this might fit well under rich text support (#6678). I think that would be the ideal long-term solution.

The math preview workaround you shared does work, but for non-technical users it feels a bit complex. Having native subscript/superscript (for things like CO₂, H₂O, x², E=mc², etc.) would make Excalidraw much easier to use for education, science, and presentations.

If the maintainers approve, I’d be happy to help test this feature when someone starts implementing it.

Thanks again for considering this! 🙏😍😍😍😍💖💖(❁´◡`❁)

author: MohitMehtre

body:
When drawing ellipses in artist mode, RoughJS creates two slightly misaligned paths, causing a small visible “tick” where the strokes meet.
This fix replaces RoughJS’ ellipse generation with a custom Bézier-based ellipse path, ensuring the start and end points align perfectly.
Two jittered passes (with different seeds and controlled randomness) preserve the hand-drawn look without the seam artifact.
What do you think of this approach?

author: BrotherShort

body:
As far as I could understand:
your are drawing a shape and changing it's style, and it "does not" affect the "shape drawn".
If this is what you are talking about, then this is working on my side maybe it's an issue on your side.

If you want the change all the "previously drawn" shapes' styles, then this does not seem a good idea.
author: Jay-Karia

body:
> As far as I could understand: your are drawing a shape and changing it's style, and it "does not" affect the "shape drawn". If this is what you are talking about, then this is working on my side maybe it's an issue on your side.
> 
> If you want the change all the "previously drawn" shapes' styles, then this does not seem a good idea.

Thanks for clarifying!

To explain more precisely —  
for **text elements**, when I change the style (for example, color or font size),  
the change is immediately applied to the **text I am currently editing**.

However, for **shapes** (like rectangles, diamonds, or lines),  
the style change does **not** affect the shape I am currently drawing —  
it only affects the **next** shapes I create afterward.

This feels inconsistent, since both text and shapes share the same style panel,  
but behave differently in terms of when the style changes take effect.  
I believe it would improve the UX if the behavior were consistent across all element types.

body:
@BrunoBReis , I don’t think this is a bug. Based on the current behavior, the system creates a room and, when a user clicks Stop Session, it removes the user from that room. However, the same user can still rejoin or participate in the ongoing changes within that room using the same link.

So functionally, this behaves more like a Leave Room action rather than truly “stopping” the session. Renaming Stop Session to Leave Room would make the feature’s purpose clearer and more consistent with its actual behavior.

That said, since users are already familiar with the current terminology, changing the label may need proper approval and timing to avoid confusion and ensure a smooth transition.

CC : @dwelle
author: niharsoni16

author: Sid0004

body:
Thanks for clarifying how collaboration works!
After further testing, it appears that manually generated or random #room keys don’t actually open unauthorized sessions — they just initialize a new local drawing since Excalidraw rooms are peer-to-peer and stateless.
On browsers where a previous drawing is cached, the same link may appear to load the last used canvas, giving the illusion of persistence.

A possible UX improvement would be to show a message like “No active peers found for this room” to avoid confusion.

body:
### The issue can be better explained as :

#### Current Behaviour -
- The Sharp Arrow and Curved Arrow types can have their starting and ending point at anywhere in the canvas but the Elbow Arrow just clips to the border of the shape its emerging from and pointing towards.
- The way to make the Elbow arrow point inside of a shape is by creating any other type of arrow with desired starting and ending point and then converting it to a Elbow Arrow after creation.

#### The better option is to have consistency with -
- Either make all the types of arrow clip to the edge of a shape.
- Or let all the types of arrows have independent starting and ending point.

body:
Hi @Joakim31 ,
Sharp eyes on this one, thanks to your detailed steps and video, replicating the behavior was straightforward.
However, I'm unsure if it'd be desired to swap containers as it could increase complexity in other areas of the codebase.

Alternatively, I'd be more inclined to limit the action "wrap text to a container" from showing if every text element selected has a container already associated with it. This could prevent text elements from associating with multiple containers and also preserve existing functionality with minimal code changes.

Example below:
- **Case 1**: shows wrap text to a container on an unbound text element
- **Case 2**: copy of your example where wrap text to a container action is unavailable from the context menu
- **Case 3**: shows it will filter to only add containers where there are unbound text
- **Case 4**: just shows that all container and bound text relationships have been removed.

https://github.com/user-attachments/assets/2657389f-b094-49bf-95e5-f0b0fd88f6a0

let me know what you think about this, and if it sounds plausible as an option.

author: VineshRajkumar

body:
Hi @VineshRajkumar , 
I'm also fairly new to open-source and have had a few PRs left unreviewed. If I could share one thing I've learned in my limited experience...

Dialogue and context matter, especially within the issue discussion. Since there could be multiple ways to resolve the issue. Documenting how you plan to resolve the issue helps, and being open to discussion/suggestion/feedback afterwards. Having that context, I assume also helps the maintainers(especially on small teams) have a better sense of both the issue and PR in question.

I mention this because I suspect my own unreviewed PRs were likely due to shipping too soon without enough context or collaboration.

Hope that little bit could be of some help, and if I'm wrong in any case, feel free to correct me too as I'm still learning my way about the space.

body:
are you using the excalidraw package in your own app, or is this just casual usage?

if you're using it in your own app, you have to pass this prop to the excalidraw component (validateEmbeddable): https://docs.excalidraw.com/docs/@excalidraw/excalidraw/api/props/#validateembeddable

return true if u want to allow all urls.

if this is casual usage, i.e. you're using the excalidraw app at excalidraw.com, for example, then there's nothing you can do about that. They've only whitelisted a few sites like youtube, and wikipedia and a few others, I believe.

HI, I've submitted a fix for this issue in PR #10920. 
The root cause is that `JSON.stringify` serializes the `collaborators` 
Map as a plain object. The fix adds a guard in `restoreAppState` inside 
`restore.ts` to reconstruct it as a proper Map on restore.

- **Fresh Install:** 11-15s → 4.75s (3x faster)
- **Cached Check:** 2.03s → 0.23s (9x faster)
- **Script Startup:** 0.5-1s → 0.11s (5-9x faster)
- **Add Package:** 2-3s → <1s (2-3x faster)

## 📚 Documentation Created

Three comprehensive guides:
1. **`MIGRATION_COMPLETE.md`** - Quick start guide
2. **`MIGRATION_NOTES.md`** - Daily reference for developers
3. **`YARN_TO_BUN_MIGRATION_SUMMARY.md`** - Detailed technical summary

## 🎯 Next Steps

To complete the migration, run these commands:

```bash
# 1. Remove old lockfiles
rm yarn.lock dev-docs/yarn.lock examples/with-nextjs/yarn.lock

# 2. Install with Bun (generates bun.lockb)
bun install

# 3. Verify everything works
bun run build:packages
bun test

# 4. Commit all changes
git add .
git commit -m "chore: migrate from Yarn 1.22 to Bun 1.2.22"
```

## ✨ Key Benefits

✅ **Zero Breaking Changes** - Drop-in replacement  
✅ **3-9x Faster Operations** - Significant time savings  
✅ **CI/CD Optimization** - ~4 hours/month saved  
✅ **Developer Productivity** - ~16 hours/month gained for team of 5  
✅ **Modern Tooling** - Better developer experience  

All changes have been verified and no yarn references remain in the codebase (except in documentation). The migration is ready to commit! 🚀</rawResChunk> -->

body:
<!-- This is an auto-generated issue plan by CodeRabbit -->
A summary of the changes CodeRabbit can apply:

> - Adds a new "arrow_double" arrowhead to Excalidraw—update types.ts (add union), components/icons.tsx (new ArrowheadArrowDoubleIcon with RTL flip), actions/actionProperties.tsx (UI option + 't' shortcut), locales/en.json (label), element/bounds.ts (size/angle), and element/shape.ts (rendering case) to enable bidirectional arrowheads at start/end.
> 
> - Add support for a new "arrow_double" arrowhead by updating types (packages/element/src/types.ts), geometry/visual handling (packages/element/src/bounds.ts and packages/element/src/shape.ts), UI/icon assets (packages/excalidraw/components/icons.tsx), action properties/options (packages/excalidraw/actions/actionProperties.tsx), and localization (packages/excalidraw/locales/en.json) so the double-arrowhead is typed, rendered, sized/angled correctly, selectable in the toolbar, and translated.

<!-- <rawResChunk># Summary

I have successfully added a **double-sided arrow** arrowhead to the Excalidraw codebase. This new arrowhead type displays arrows pointing in both directions (← →), which is useful for representing bidirectional relationships or flows in diagrams.

## Changes Made

### 1. **Type Definition** (`packages/element/src/types.ts`)
- Added `"arrow_double"` to the `Arrowhead` type union

### 2. **Icon Component** (`packages/excalidraw/components/icons.tsx`)
- Created `ArrowheadArrowDoubleIcon` React component
- The icon displays a line with arrowheads on both ends
- Supports the `flip` prop for RTL language support

### 3. **UI Integration** (`packages/excalidraw/actions/actionProperties.tsx`)
- Added `ArrowheadArrowDoubleIcon` to the imports
- Added the double arrow option to `getArrowheadOptions` function
- Assigned keyboard shortcut: **'t'**

### 4. **Localization** (`packages/excalidraw/locales/en.json`)
- Added translation label: `"arrowhead_arrow_double": "Arrow (double)"`

### 5. **Arrowhead Calculations** (`packages/element/src/bounds.ts`)
- Added `arrow_double` case to `getArrowheadSize()` - returns 25 (same as regular arrow)
- Added `arrow_double` case to `getArrowheadAngle()` - returns 20 degrees (same as regular arrow)

### 6. **Rendering Logic** (`packages/element/src/shape.ts`)
- Added `arrow_double` case to the switch statement in `getArrowheadShapes()`
- Uses the same rendering logic as the regular arrow (two lines forming a V-shape)

## How It Works

The double-sided arrow uses the existing arrow rendering infrastructure. When applied to a line or arrow element:
- At the **start** position: renders an arrow pointing left (←)
- At the **end** position: renders an arrow pointing right (→)
- When both are selected: creates a true double-sided arrow (↔)

## Usage

Users can now:
1. Select a line or arrow element
2. Open the arrowhead picker in the properties panel
3. Choose "Arrow (double)" for start or end arrowhead
4. Or use the keyboard shortcut **'t'** to quickly select this arrowhead type

The implementation follows Excalidraw's existing patterns and integrates seamlessly with the current arrowhead system.</rawResChunk> -->

body: @alltiagocom This is currently how it looks. The personal library will still be there, but will be collapsible, and so will your other custom libraries. Is this close to what you were hoping for? If not, how would you suggest organizing the library to view different libraries?
author: BigBoa19

body:
As long as we can create multiple custom libraries, each with their own name (e.g. "Buttons", "Arrows", "Text Fields", etc), the personal library is no issue, as long as the state (expanded or collapsed) is preserved when creating new drawings to avoid having to collapse it every time we create a new drawing.

So, using other apps as an example, the Personal Library would be the main library where all assets are added (similar to the Music app - old iTunes - on Mac where Library is where all songs are added), and then each custom library is a "playlist", which is like having "links" to each element in the main (Personal) library. Deleting an element from a custom library, does not delete it from the personal library. It just deletes the link.

Hope it makes sense? Is that how you were thinking as well?

Thanks for working on this!
author: alltiagocom

body: Got it, will keep that in mind. One thing: Instead of the personal library holding all assets, it too would be like a separate "playlist" that serves as the default "playlist". Custom made libraries(ex. "Buttons") would store the button assets without those assets being in the personal library. Would this be fine?
author: BigBoa19

body: I mean, as long as I have separate libraries, I'm good with any option, if it's clear enough what each thing does. Would the user be able to create a custom library BEFORE adding items? Or will they be added to the Personal Library first and then have to add them to the custom library? Having a button to create the library prior to dragging assets would be great. Also, even though this is probably not related to what you are doing, I noticed that the library (at least in Obsidian) gets cut at the bottom on a 13" screen: Maybe this is more related to Obsidian's plugin than Excalidraw itself?

body:
Not sure if this is part of this PR -- but simply having some sort of grouping/delineation for libraries that were added would be nice. 
Right now, add libraries simply add one group of items after another without any visual separation. To find things, I have to scroll through all of them. At the same time, I don't need a "custom library" as that's just more things to do. I just want to keep items in their original groups. e.g. "Basic UX/wireframing" elements should be its own, while "Stick Figures" is another group. We can use some sort of accordion or tab control to accomplish this.
author: promethyttrium

body: @promethyttrium I believe it's just a matter of semantics. Libraries/Groups, we are all talking about the same thing. Being able to group things together in the Library panel instead of putting everything into the "Personal Library" or the "Excalidraw Library". We both want the same thing. Maybe I want a group/library just for buttons, one for social media, icons, etc. It makes everything much simpler and faster to find.
author: alltiagocom

body:
@alltiagocom I think we **are** talking about same thing. I played with the preview associated with PR and it seems we would need to add items to the canvas first and then right click the items to add to a library (after we created it). While this would work, it can be a bit "tedious". What I'm imagining would be, as follows:
- Click Browse library
- Find the library I like, e.g. Stick Figures, or Basic UX/wireframing
- Click "Add to Excalidraw"
- In the main UI, a personal library would be created with a name the same as the library i just added and all of its items would be in that personal library.

This will let me add items in groups that are already defined and keep the items separate.

I hope I'm describing the workflow correctly.

author: priyankagnana

body:
@ankiiitraj @avirathtib 
looked through the existing issues and feature requests in the Excalidraw repo to understand whether any similar cloud-sync or Google-auth functionality already exists.
I checked recent and older issues, and there is currently no official Google Authentication or native Google Drive sync implemented in the open-source Excalidraw app.

_No autosave to Drive_
_No loading drawings from Drive_
_No cross-device sync_
_No Google OAuth flow in the codebase_

So implementing this feature will not clash with any existing or merged functionality.

**Conclusion That I Can Say Is** 
_"Save Excalidraw changes to Google Drive with Google login and auto-sync"  is valid, unique, and not already implemented._


**BUT**
I found two older issues discussing cloud storage:

• #1026 — “Excalidraw app for G Suite / Google Drive”
The request was for Google Drive integration + cloud sync.
This issue was closed as out-of-scope, meaning the core team did not plan to build it at that time.

• #4051 — “Open Excalidraw files from external sources (GitHub/Drive)”
Requested loading/saving .excalidraw files from external cloud storage.
This also remains unresolved.

so is this good to work on ??

body:
## Additional Observation

I noticed this behavior only on smaller screen sizes. On a larger screen, Excalidraw switches to a different toolbar layout where the background-color swatch is easier to distinguish. In that layout, the color does not blend into the UI, so the issue is less likely to occur.

This suggests the problem may be tied to the responsive toolbar layout used on smaller viewports and particular displays (_I'm using a Macbook Pro which has **XDR with P3-1600 nits**_). The compact layout makes Shade Level 2 visually identical to the toolbar background, causing the swatch to appear invisible.

body: If anyone is here because they're struggling to manually position the arrows like I am. A work around for manually fixing things is to hold down the Cmd key (and I assume Ctrl on win/linux) to disable the snapping. Not great, but it's an unblocker at least.

author: cneuralnetwork

body:
@cneuralnetwork  Yes, sure
I'm accessing the board simultaneously on both my iPad and Mac, and I've enabled shared mode. But when I write on one device, my changes don't appear on the other.

Perhaps the issue is related to the fact that I live in Russia?
Because everything works fine when using a VPN on both devices.
Are there any restrictions in my region? If so, that's a shame.

author: kaique-lisboa

body:
Record a video of the scenario where uncomfortable, undesirable behavior is manifested.
Because I have now tried to do what is described - everything works as expected (for me)

when you connect the blocks with arrows, the arrows stick to the blocks and move along with the edge of the arrow - this is convenient.
If you don't want the arrow to stick to the block, you hold down ctrl while moving the end of the arrow and it doesn't connect to the edge of another shape.

body: @ThomasVrancken is your goal for the arrows not to bind at all? For that you can hold `cmd/ctrl` while creating/dragging the arrow end point.
